# =============================================================================
# Agent Factory — Single Source of Truth
# =============================================================================
# Defines the top-level factory configuration for the IPAI Agent Orchestration
# Platform. All agent lifecycle, routing, and governance flows derive from this.
# =============================================================================

id: agent_factory
name: IPAI Agent Factory
version: 1.0.0
status: active
owner: platform-team
description: >
  Central factory for agent creation, promotion, orchestration, and retirement.
  Governs the full lifecycle from concept through production deployment and
  eventual decommission.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lifecycle Phases
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ordered sequence every agent traverses. Phases map to stage gates (stage_gates.yaml).

lifecycle_phases:
  - id: concept
    name: Concept
    purpose: Capture agent intent, scope, and initial constraints.
    order: 1

  - id: design
    name: Design
    purpose: Define capabilities, interfaces, and interaction contracts.
    order: 2

  - id: scaffold
    name: Scaffold
    purpose: Generate project structure, boilerplate, and initial configuration.
    order: 3

  - id: implement
    name: Implement
    purpose: Build agent logic, tool bindings, and prompt templates.
    order: 4

  - id: unit_test
    name: Unit Test
    purpose: Validate individual components in isolation.
    order: 5

  - id: integration_test
    name: Integration Test
    purpose: Verify agent interactions with external services and other agents.
    order: 6

  - id: review
    name: Review
    purpose: Human and automated review of code, prompts, and security posture.
    order: 7

  - id: staging
    name: Staging
    purpose: Deploy to staging environment for end-to-end validation.
    order: 8

id: unit_test name: Unit Test purpose: Validate individual components in isolation. order: 5 - id: integration_test name: Integration Test purpose: Verify agent interactions with external services and other agents. order: 6 - id: review name: Review purpose: Human and automated review of code, prompts, and security posture. order: 7 - id: staging name: Staging purpose: Deploy to staging environment for end-to-end validation. order: 8

  - id: canary
    name: Canary
    purpose: Gradual traffic shift to validate production behavior under real load.
    order: 9

  - id: production
    name: Production
    purpose: Full production deployment with monitoring and alerting.
    order: 10

  - id: observe
    name: Observe
    purpose: Continuous monitoring of performance, cost, and drift.
    order: 11

  - id: optimize
    name: Optimize
    purpose: Tune prompts, parameters, and resource allocation based on telemetry.
    order: 12

  - id: deprecate
    name: Deprecate
    purpose: Signal intent to retire, migrate consumers, freeze changes.
    order: 13

  - id: decommission
    name: Decommission
    purpose: Remove agent from runtime, archive artifacts, revoke credentials.
    order: 14

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Phase-to-Stage Mapping
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 14 lifecycle phases map to 16 operational stages (S01-S16).
# Some stages span multiple phases; some phases map 1:1 to a stage.
# Canonical stage definitions: stage_gates.yaml

phase_to_stage_mapping:
  - phase: concept
    stages: [S01]
    description: Intake — blueprint submitted, passport created.
  - phase: design
    stages: [S02]
    description: Contract drafted, I/O schemas defined.
  - phase: scaffold
    stages: [S03]
    description: Project structure generated, CI configured, registry entry created.
  - phase: implement
    stages: [S04]
    description: Core logic built, tool bindings functional, prompt templates versioned.
  - phase: unit_test
    stages: [S05]
    description: Unit-level evaluations written and passing.
  - phase: integration_test
    stages: [S06]
    description: Cross-agent and tool-binding tests passing.
  - phase: review
    stages: [S07, S08]
    description: Security review (S07) and code review (S08).
  - phase: staging
    stages: [S09]
    description: Staging deployment with end-to-end validation.
  - phase: canary
    stages: [S10, S11]
    description: Judge evaluation (S10) and canary deploy (S11) with limited real traffic.
  - phase: production
    stages: [S12]
    description: Full production traffic shift.
  - phase: observe
    stages: [S13]
    description: Continuous monitoring of performance, cost, and drift.
  - phase: optimize
    stages: [S14]
    description: Tune prompts, parameters, and resources based on telemetry.
  - phase: deprecate
    stages: [S15]
    description: Deprecation notice published, consumers migrated, changes frozen.
  - phase: decommission
    stages: [S16]
    description: Agent removed from runtime, credentials revoked, artifacts archived.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Planes
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

planes:
  control:
    description: >
      Governance, policy enforcement, stage gate evaluation, and promotion decisions.
    components:
      - stage_gate_engine
      - maturity_evaluator
      - policy_enforcer
      - promotion_controller

description: Tune prompts, parameters, and resources based on telemetry. - phase: deprecate stages: [S15] description: Deprecation notice published, consumers migrated, changes frozen. - phase: decommission stages: [S16] description: Agent removed from runtime, credentials revoked, artifacts archived. # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Planes # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- planes: control: description: > Governance, policy enforcement, stage gate evaluation, and promotion decisions. components: - stage_gate_engine - maturity_evaluator - policy_enforcer - promotion_controller

  runtime:
    description: >
      Agent execution, task routing, health monitoring, and scaling.
    components:
      - task_bus
      - agent_runtime
      - health_monitor
      - autoscaler
      - circuit_breaker

  artifact:
    description: >
      Versioned storage for agent definitions, prompts, tools, and evaluation results.
    components:
      - agent_registry
      - prompt_store
      - tool_catalog
      - evaluation_archive
      - audit_log

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core Services
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

core_services:
  - id: agent_registry
    description: Canonical registry of all agent definitions and their current maturity level.
    backing_store: supabase

  - id: task_bus
    description: Envelope-based task routing between agents and external systems.
    backing_store: supabase_realtime
    spec: task_bus.yaml

  - id: stage_gate_engine
    description: Evaluates promotion criteria and enforces gate requirements.
    spec: stage_gates.yaml

  - id: maturity_evaluator
    description: Assesses agent maturity level based on evidence and controls.
    spec: agent_maturity_model.yaml

  - id: prompt_store
    description: Versioned prompt templates with A/B variant support.
    backing_store: supabase

and their current maturity level. backing_store: supabase - id: task_bus description: Envelope-based task routing between agents and external systems. backing_store: supabase_realtime spec: task_bus.yaml - id: stage_gate_engine description: Evaluates promotion criteria and enforces gate requirements. spec: stage_gates.yaml - id: maturity_evaluator description: Assesses agent maturity level based on evidence and controls. spec: agent_maturity_model.yaml - id: prompt_store description: Versioned prompt templates with A/B variant support. backing_store: supabase

  - id: tool_catalog
    description: Registry of available tools with capability declarations and auth requirements.
    backing_store: supabase

  - id: evaluation_engine
    description: Runs judge agents against agent outputs for quality scoring.
    depends_on:
      - agent_registry
      - task_bus

  - id: audit_log
    description: Immutable record of all agent actions, promotions, and policy decisions.
    backing_store: supabase
    retention: 365d

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Repository Paths
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

repo_paths:
  root: /
  ssot: ssot/agent-platform/
  specs: spec/agent-factory/
  agents: ssot/agents/
  edge_functions: supabase/functions/
  workflows: .github/workflows/
  docs: docs/ai/

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Integration Points
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

integrations:
  azure:
    compute: Azure Container Apps
    ai: Azure OpenAI (oai-ipai-dev)
    search: Azure AI Search (srch-ipai-dev)
    identity: Managed Identity + Keycloak (transitional)
    secrets: Azure Key Vault (kv-ipai-dev)
    monitoring: Azure Monitor + Application Insights
    container_registry: cripaidev.azurecr.io

  foundry:
    role: Agent hosting, identity lifecycle, MCP/A2A transport
    sdk: Python + .NET

    reference: ssot/agent-platform/foundry_designer_adapter.yaml

  supabase:
    instance: spdtwktxdalcfigzeqrz
    role: Task bus, agent registry, prompt store, audit log, Realtime pub/sub
    edge_functions: true
    vault: true
    pgvector: true

  slack:
    role: Human-in-the-loop notifications, approval workflows, agent status alerts
    transport: Slack API via n8n

  github:
    role: CI/CD, spec validation, agent artifact versioning
    workflows: .github/workflows/

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# References
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

spec_bundle: spec/agent-factory/
architecture_doc: docs/architecture/agent-platform/agent-factory.md
maturity_model: ssot/agent-platform/agent_maturity_model.yaml
task_bus_spec: ssot/agent-platform/task_bus.yaml
stage_gates_spec: ssot/agent-platform/stage_gates.yaml

# =============================================================================
# Agent Maturity Model — Single Source of Truth
# =============================================================================
# Six-level maturity framework (L0-L5) governing agent promotion from concept
# through autonomous operation. Each level defines required capabilities,
# evidence, controls, and blast radius constraints.
# =============================================================================

id: agent_maturity_model
name: IPAI Agent Maturity Model
version: 1.0.0
status: active
owner: platform-team

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Maturity Levels
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

levels:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L0 — Concept
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L0
    name: concept
    definition: >
      Agent exists as an idea with documented intent and scope but no executable code.
    required_capabilities: []
    required_evidence:
      - agent_brief_document
      - stakeholder_sign_off
      - scope_boundary_definition
    required_controls:
      - naming_convention_compliance
      - no_duplicate_scope_check
    evaluation_expectations: []
    observability_expectations: []
    allowed_blast_radius: none
    promotion_criteria:
      - approved_agent_brief
      - assigned_owner
      - defined_capability_contract
    rollback_triggers:
      - scope_rejected_by_owner
      - duplicate_agent_detected
    allowed_stages:
      - S01
      - S02

# L0 — Concept # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: L0 name: concept definition: > Agent exists as an idea with documented intent and scope but no executable code. required_capabilities: [] required_evidence: - agent_brief_document - stakeholder_sign_off - scope_boundary_definition required_controls: - naming_convention_compliance - no_duplicate_scope_check evaluation_expectations: [] observability_expectations: [] allowed_blast_radius: none promotion_criteria: - approved_agent_brief - assigned_owner - defined_capability_contract rollback_triggers: - scope_rejected_by_owner - duplicate_agent_detected allowed_stages: - S01 - S02

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L1 — Prototype
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L1
    name: prototype
    definition: >
      Agent has executable code that demonstrates core capability in a sandboxed environment.
    required_capabilities:
      - single_tool_execution
      - structured_input_parsing
      - structured_output_generation
    required_evidence:
      - passing_unit_tests
      - sandbox_execution_log
      - tool_binding_verification
    required_controls:
      - sandboxed_execution_only
      - no_production_data_access
      - credential_scoping_to_dev
    evaluation_expectations:
      - output_format_validation
      - basic_correctness_check
    observability_expectations:
      - structured_logging_enabled
      - execution_duration_tracked
    allowed_blast_radius: sandbox_only
    promotion_criteria:
      - all_unit_tests_passing
      - tool_bindings_verified
      - output_schema_validated
      - code_review_approved
    rollback_triggers:
      - unit_test_regression
      - tool_binding_failure
      - security_finding_critical
    allowed_stages:
      - S03
      - S04
      - S05

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L2 — Integrated
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L2
    name: integrated
    definition: >
      Agent interacts with external services and other agents in a staging environment with real schemas but synthetic data.
    required_capabilities:
      - multi_tool_orchestration
      - error_handling_and_retry
      - context_window_management
      - inter_agent_communication
    required_evidence:
      - integration_test_results
      - staging_deployment_log
      - error_recovery_demonstration
      - latency_baseline_measurement
    required_controls:
      - staging_environment_isolation
      - synthetic_data_only
      - rate_limiting_enforced
      - audit_logging_active
    evaluation_expectations:
      - judge_agent_scoring_above_threshold
      - error_rate_below_5_percent
      - latency_p95_within_budget
    observability_expectations:
      - distributed_tracing_enabled
      - error_rate_dashboard
      - latency_percentile_tracking
      - token_usage_metering
    allowed_blast_radius: staging_environment
    promotion_criteria:
      - integration_tests_passing
      - judge_score_ge_70
      - error_rate_lt_5_percent
      - p95_latency_lt_10s
      - security_review_passed
    rollback_triggers:
      - integration_test_failure
      - judge_score_below_60
      - error_rate_above_10_percent
      - security_vulnerability_detected
    allowed_stages:
      - S06
      - S07

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L3 — Supervised Production
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L3
    name: supervised_production
    definition: >
      Agent operates in production with human-in-the-loop approval for consequential actions.
    required_capabilities:
      - graceful_degradation
      - human_escalation_workflow
      - cost_awareness
      - output_confidence_scoring
    required_evidence:
      - canary_deployment_metrics
      - human_approval_workflow_verification
      - cost_projection_under_load
      - rollback_procedure_tested
    required_controls:
      - human_approval_for_writes
      - cost_ceiling_enforced
      - circuit_breaker_configured
      - rollback_procedure_documented
      - pii_handling_validated
    evaluation_expectations:
      - judge_score_ge_80
      - user_satisfaction_tracked
      - false_positive_rate_tracked
      - cost_per_task_within_budget
    observability_expectations:
      - real_time_alerting
      - cost_tracking_per_invocation
      - human_override_rate_tracking
      - drift_detection_enabled
    allowed_blast_radius: limited_production_with_human_gate
    promotion_criteria:
      - canary_success_rate_ge_95
      - human_override_rate_lt_15_percent
      - cost_per_task_within_budget
      - no_critical_incidents_in_7_days
      - judge_score_ge_80
    rollback_triggers:
      - human_override_rate_above_25_percent
      - cost_ceiling_breached
      - critical_incident_detected
      - judge_score_below_70
    allowed_stages:
      - S08
      - S09

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L4 — Autonomous Production
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L4
    name: autonomous_production
    definition: >
      Agent operates in production without human approval for routine actions, with monitoring and automatic rollback.
    required_capabilities:
      - autonomous_decision_making
      - self_monitoring
      - adaptive_retry_strategies
      - multi_agent_coordination
      - output_self_evaluation
    required_evidence:
      - 30_day_supervised_production_log
      - autonomous_decision_audit
      - load_test_results
      - disaster_recovery_drill
    required_controls:
      - automated_rollback_on_anomaly
      - cost_hard_ceiling
      - blast_radius_containment
      - weekly_audit_review
      - model_version_pinning
    evaluation_expectations:
      - judge_score_ge_90
      - autonomous_accuracy_ge_95_percent
      - cost_efficiency_improving_or_stable
      - zero_critical_incidents_in_30_days
    observability_expectations:
      - anomaly_detection_active
      - predictive_cost_forecasting
      - agent_behavior_drift_monitoring
      - cross_agent_dependency_tracking
    allowed_blast_radius: full_production_with_automatic_containment
    promotion_criteria:
      - 30_days_at_L3_without_critical_incident
      - autonomous_accuracy_ge_95_percent
      - cost_trend_stable_or_declining
      - disaster_recovery_drill_passed
      - judge_score_ge_90
    rollback_triggers:
      - anomaly_detection_alert
      - cost_hard_ceiling_breached
      - accuracy_below_90_percent
      - cross_agent_cascade_failure
    allowed_stages:
      - S10
      - S11
      - S12

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # L5 — Optimizing
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: L5
    name: optimizing
    definition: >
      Agent self-tunes prompts, parameters, and resource allocation based on continuous feedback and evaluation.
    required_capabilities:
      - prompt_self_optimization
      - resource_auto_scaling
      - feedback_loop_integration
      - cross_agent_load_balancing
      - capability_self_assessment
    required_evidence:
      - optimization_improvement_log
      - self_tuning_safety_bounds_verification
      - 90_day_production_stability_log
      - optimization_rollback_demonstration
    required_controls:
      - optimization_bounds_enforced
      - optimization_audit_trail
      - human_review_of_optimization_decisions_weekly
      - no_self_modification_of_safety_controls
      - prompt_version_history_preserved
    evaluation_expectations:
      - continuous_judge_scoring
      - optimization_delta_positive
      - no_quality_regression_from_optimization
      - cost_efficiency_measurably_improved
    observability_expectations:
      - optimization_decision_logging
      - before_after_comparison_dashboards
      - safety_bound_proximity_alerting
      - prompt_version_lineage_tracking
    allowed_blast_radius: full_production_with_self_healing
    promotion_criteria:
      - 90_days_at_L4_without_critical_incident
      - demonstrated_measurable_optimization
      - optimization_safety_bounds_tested
      - human_review_cadence_established
    rollback_triggers:
      - optimization_causes_quality_regression
      - safety_bound_breach
      - cost_increase_from_optimization
      - human_review_flags_concern
    allowed_stages:
      - S13

# Agent Factory — Boards Mapping SSOT
# Maps spec/agent-factory/tasks.md to Azure Boards decomposition.

id: agent_factory_boards_mapping
version: 1.0.0
source: spec/agent-factory/tasks.md
task_id_pattern: "AF-\\d+"

epics:
  - id: EPIC-AF-01
    title: Agent Factory Foundations
    description: Types, schemas, repo structure, base packages
    phases: [phase_0]
    task_range: AF-001 to AF-008

  - id: EPIC-AF-02
    title: Task Bus
    description: Typed envelope, router, state machine, event log
    phases: [phase_1]
    task_range: AF-010 to AF-019

  - id: EPIC-AF-03
    title: Registry and Passports
    description: Agent CRUD, passport schema, migration of existing agents
    phases: [phase_2]
    task_range: AF-020 to AF-029

  - id: EPIC-AF-04
    title: Assembly Line and Stage Gates
    description: 16-stage config, gate engine, criteria evaluator
    phases: [phase_3]
    task_range: AF-030 to AF-045

  - id: EPIC-AF-05
    title: Evaluations and Judge Agents
    description: Eval specs, rubrics, judge contracts, eval runner
    phases: [phase_4]
    task_range: AF-050 to AF-060

  - id: EPIC-AF-06
    title: Promotion and Release Controls
    description: Promotion records, release lanes, maturity calculator
    phases: [phase_5]
    task_range: AF-070 to AF-078

  - id: EPIC-AF-07
    title: Runtime Observability
    description: Metrics, alerts, dashboards, SLA tracking
    phases: [phase_6]
    task_range: AF-080 to AF-088

  - id: EPIC-AF-08
    title: Portfolio Governance and Retirement
    description: Portfolio KPIs, debt tracker, retirement workflows
    phases: [phase_7, phase_8]
    task_range: AF-090 to AF-106

stage_to_issue_ownership:
  S01-S02: platform-team
  S03-S07: maker-agent-owner
  S08-S09: platform-team
  S10-S13: runtime-ops
  S14-S16: platform-team

work_item_defaults:
  type: Task
  area_path: InsightPulseAI/Agent-Platform
  iteration_path: current
  parent_rule: phase_to_epic

task_range: AF-070 to AF-078 - id: EPIC-AF-07 title: Runtime Observability description: Metrics, alerts, dashboards, SLA tracking phases: [phase_6] task_range: AF-080 to AF-088 - id: EPIC-AF-08 title: Portfolio Governance and Retirement description: Portfolio KPIs, debt tracker, retirement workflows phases: [phase_7, phase_8] task_range: AF-090 to AF-106 stage_to_issue_ownership: S01-S02: platform-team S03-S07: maker-agent-owner S08-S09: platform-team S10-S13: runtime-ops S14-S16: platform-team work_item_defaults: type: Task area_path: InsightPulseAI/Agent-Platform iteration_path: current parent_rule: phase_to_epic

notes:
  - Task IDs (AF-NNN) are stable identifiers from spec/agent-factory/tasks.md
  - Epics group by delivery phase, not by stage
  - Do not create duplicate work items — derive from tasks.md
  - Update this file if tasks.md phases are renumbered

# Foundry Designer Adapter — Machine-Readable SSOT
# Generated: 2026-03-19

id: foundry_designer_adapter
service: designer-agent-api
path: platform/services/designer-agent-api/
provider: microsoft_foundry
sdk: "@azure/ai-projects"
auth: DefaultAzureCredential
runtime_model:
  - agent
  - conversation
  - response
agent_strategy: reuse_or_create
response_contract: DesignerAgentResponse
status: proposed

env_vars:
  - AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT (required)
  - AZURE_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_CONNECTION_STRING (optional)
  - AZURE_FOUNDRY_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT (optional)
  - AZURE_FOUNDRY_DESIGNER_AGENT_ID (optional)
  - AZURE_FOUNDRY_DESIGNER_AGENT_NAME (required)
  - DESIGNER_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS (optional, default 45000)

adapter_files:
  client: src/adapters/foundry/client.ts
  agents: src/adapters/foundry/agents.ts
  execute: src/adapters/foundry/execute.ts
  normalize: src/adapters/foundry/normalize.ts
  errors: src/adapters/foundry/errors.ts

service_files:
  service: src/services/designer-agent-service.ts
  route: src/routes/designer-agent.ts
  config: src/config/env.ts

contracts:
  backend: src/contracts/designer-agent.ts
  foundry: src/contracts/foundry.ts
  frontend: web/packages/fluent-designer-contract/src/types.ts

integration:
  frontend_app: web/apps/designer-agent/
  spec_bundle: spec/fluent-designer-agent/
  architecture_doc: docs/architecture/agent-platform/fluent-designer-backend-adapter.md

request_contract: DesignerAgentRequest
timeout_ms_default: 45000
provider_abstraction: true

notes:
  - SDK method names may shift in preview; only client.ts/agents.ts/execute.ts need updates
  - No thread/run concepts — uses current agent/conversation/response model
  - Frontend remains backend-agnostic through DesignerAgentService adapter interface

  - id: RISK-03
    description: Task bus is design-only — no runtime messages flowing
    severity: medium
    mitigation: Implement Phase 1 (AF-010 to AF-019) of agent-factory tasks
    owner: platform-team

# =============================================================================
# Stage Gates — Single Source of Truth
# =============================================================================
# Defines 16 stages (S01-S16) governing agent progression from intake through
# decommission. Each stage has explicit entry/exit criteria, required artifacts,
# ownership, and failure modes.
# =============================================================================

id: stage_gates
name: IPAI Agent Stage Gates
version: 1.0.0
status: active
owner: platform-team
description: >
  Sequential gate system ensuring agents meet quality, security, and operational
  requirements before advancing. Maps to lifecycle phases in agent_factory.yaml
  and maturity levels in agent_maturity_model.yaml.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stages
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

stages:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S01 — Intake
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S01
    name: intake
    purpose: Capture and validate the initial agent request with scope and justification.
    order: 1
    entry_criteria:
      - agent_request_submitted
      - requestor_identified
    exit_criteria:
      - agent_brief_approved
      - no_duplicate_agent_in_registry
      - scope_boundary_documented
      - owner_assigned
    required_artifacts:
      - agent_brief
      - scope_boundary_document
    owner: human
    automation_level: semi-auto
    failure_modes:
      - duplicate_scope_with_existing_agent
      - unclear_or_unbounded_scope
      - no_owner_available
    feeds_into: S02

# Stages # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- stages: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # S01 — Intake # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S01 name: intake purpose: Capture and validate the initial agent request with scope and justification. order: 1 entry_criteria: - agent_request_submitted - requestor_identified exit_criteria: - agent_brief_approved - no_duplicate_agent_in_registry - scope_boundary_documented - owner_assigned required_artifacts: - agent_brief - scope_boundary_document owner: human automation_level: semi-auto failure_modes: - duplicate_scope_with_existing_agent - unclear_or_unbounded_scope - no_owner_available feeds_into: S02

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S02 — Design
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S02
    name: design
    purpose: Define the agent's capability contract, tool requirements, and interaction patterns.
    order: 2
    entry_criteria:
      - agent_brief_approved
      - owner_assigned
    exit_criteria:
      - capability_contract_defined
      - tool_requirements_listed
      - input_output_schemas_specified
      - interaction_pattern_documented
      - security_requirements_identified
    required_artifacts:
      - capability_contract
      - io_schema_definition
      - tool_dependency_list
      - security_requirements
    owner: human
    automation_level: semi-auto
    failure_modes:
      - ambiguous_capability_contract
      - missing_tool_availability
      - security_requirements_incomplete
    feeds_into: S03

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # S02 — Design # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S02 name: design purpose: Define the agent's capability contract, tool requirements, and interaction patterns. order: 2 entry_criteria: - agent_brief_approved - owner_assigned exit_criteria: - capability_contract_defined - tool_requirements_listed - input_output_schemas_specified - interaction_pattern_documented - security_requirements_identified required_artifacts: - capability_contract - io_schema_definition - tool_dependency_list - security_requirements owner: human automation_level: semi-auto failure_modes: - ambiguous_capability_contract - missing_tool_availability - security_requirements_incomplete feeds_into: S03

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S03 — Scaffold
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S03
    name: scaffold
    purpose: Generate the agent project structure, configuration, and CI pipeline stubs.
    order: 3
    entry_criteria:
      - capability_contract_approved
      - repo_path_allocated
    exit_criteria:
      - project_structure_generated
      - ci_pipeline_configured
      - manifest_registered_in_agent_registry
      - dev_environment_functional
    required_artifacts:
      - agent_manifest
      - ci_pipeline_definition
      - project_skeleton
    owner: automation
    automation_level: full-auto
    failure_modes:
      - naming_convention_violation
      - ci_template_incompatibility
      - registry_write_failure
    feeds_into: S04

- security_requirements_incomplete feeds_into: S03 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # S03 — Scaffold # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S03 name: scaffold purpose: Generate the agent project structure, configuration, and CI pipeline stubs. order: 3 entry_criteria: - capability_contract_approved - repo_path_allocated exit_criteria: - project_structure_generated - ci_pipeline_configured - manifest_registered_in_agent_registry - dev_environment_functional required_artifacts: - agent_manifest - ci_pipeline_definition - project_skeleton owner: automation automation_level: full-auto failure_modes: - naming_convention_violation - ci_template_incompatibility - registry_write_failure feeds_into: S04

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S04 — Implement
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S04
    name: implement
    purpose: Build the agent's core logic, tool bindings, and prompt templates.
    order: 4
    entry_criteria:
      - scaffold_complete
      - dev_environment_functional
    exit_criteria:
      - core_logic_implemented
      - tool_bindings_functional
      - prompt_templates_versioned
      - coding_standards_met
    required_artifacts:
      - source_code
      - prompt_templates
      - tool_binding_config
    owner: human
    automation_level: semi-auto
    failure_modes:
      - tool_api_breaking_change
      - prompt_injection_vulnerability
      - coding_standard_violations
    feeds_into: S05

- registry_write_failure feeds_into: S04 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # S04 — Implement # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S04 name: implement purpose: Build the agent's core logic, tool bindings, and prompt templates. order: 4 entry_criteria: - scaffold_complete - dev_environment_functional exit_criteria: - core_logic_implemented - tool_bindings_functional - prompt_templates_versioned - coding_standards_met required_artifacts: - source_code - prompt_templates - tool_binding_config owner: human automation_level: semi-auto failure_modes: - tool_api_breaking_change - prompt_injection_vulnerability - coding_standard_violations feeds_into: S05

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S05 — Unit Test
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S05
    name: unit_test
    purpose: Validate individual agent components in isolation with deterministic tests.
    order: 5
    entry_criteria:
      - implementation_complete
      - test_harness_configured
    exit_criteria:
      - all_unit_tests_passing
      - code_coverage_ge_80_percent
      - no_critical_linting_errors
      - prompt_template_validation_passing
    required_artifacts:
      - unit_test_suite
      - coverage_report
      - lint_report
    owner: automation
    automation_level: full-auto
    failure_modes:
      - test_flakiness
      - insufficient_coverage
      - prompt_template_schema_mismatch
    feeds_into: S06

- coding_standard_violations feeds_into: S05 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # S05 — Unit Test # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S05 name: unit_test purpose: Validate individual agent components in isolation with deterministic tests. order: 5 entry_criteria: - implementation_complete - test_harness_configured exit_criteria: - all_unit_tests_passing - code_coverage_ge_80_percent - no_critical_linting_errors - prompt_template_validation_passing required_artifacts: - unit_test_suite - coverage_report - lint_report owner: automation automation_level: full-auto failure_modes: - test_flakiness - insufficient_coverage - prompt_template_schema_mismatch feeds_into: S06

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S06 — Integration Test
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S06
    name: integration_test
    purpose: Verify agent interactions with external services and peer agents using synthetic data.
    order: 6
    entry_criteria:
      - unit_tests_passing
      - integration_test_environment_available
      - synthetic_test_data_prepared
    exit_criteria:
      - all_integration_tests_passing
      - error_recovery_demonstrated
      - latency_baseline_established
      - inter_agent_protocol_validated
    required_artifacts:
      - integration_test_suite
      - latency_baseline_report
      - error_recovery_evidence
    owner: automation
    automation_level: full-auto
    failure_modes:
      - external_service_unavailable
      - protocol_version_mismatch
      - synthetic_data_inadequacy
      - timeout_threshold_exceeded
    feeds_into: S07

S06 — Integration Test # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- - id: S06 name: integration_test purpose: Verify agent interactions with external services and peer agents using synthetic data. order: 6 entry_criteria: - unit_tests_passing - integration_test_environment_available - synthetic_test_data_prepared exit_criteria: - all_integration_tests_passing - error_recovery_demonstrated - latency_baseline_established - inter_agent_protocol_validated required_artifacts: - integration_test_suite - latency_baseline_report - error_recovery_evidence owner: automation automation_level: full-auto failure_modes: - external_service_unavailable - protocol_version_mismatch - synthetic_data_inadequacy - timeout_threshold_exceeded feeds_into: S07

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # S07 — Security Review
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: S07
    name: security_review
    purpose: Assess agent for prompt injection, data leakage, credential handling, and blast radius.
    order: 7
    entry_criteria:
      - integration_tests_passing
      - security_requirements_documented
    exit_criteria:
      - no_critical_or_high_security_findings
      - prompt_injection_resistance_validated
      - credential_handling_verified
      - pii_handling_validated
      - blast_radius_assessed_and_bounded
    required_artifacts:
      - security_review_report
      - prompt_injection_test_results
      - credential_audit
    owner: human
    automation_level: semi-auto
    failure_modes:
      - prompt_injection_vulnerability
      - credential_exposure_risk
      - unbounded_blast_radius

# =============================================================================
# Task Bus — Single Source of Truth
# =============================================================================
# Defines the envelope schema, state machine, routing rules, and failure
# handling for the IPAI agent task bus. All inter-agent and agent-to-system
# communication flows through this bus.
# =============================================================================

id: task_bus
name: IPAI Task Bus
version: 1.0.0
status: active
owner: platform-team
backing_store: supabase_realtime
description: >
  Envelope-based asynchronous task routing system. Tasks flow between agents,
  external systems, and human reviewers via typed envelopes with correlation
  tracking and guaranteed delivery semantics.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Envelope Schema
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

envelope_schema:
  fields:
    - name: task_id
      type: uuid
      required: true
      description: Globally unique task identifier, generated at creation.

    - name: correlation_id
      type: uuid
      required: true
      description: Groups related tasks into a single logical workflow.

    - name: parent_task_id
      type: uuid
      required: false
      description: ID of the task that spawned this one (for sub-task trees).

    - name: type
      type: string
      required: true
      description: >
        Task type determining routing and handler selection.
        Format: <domain>.<action> (e.g., odoo.create_invoice, agent.evaluate).

    - name: source
      type: string
      required: true
      description: Identifier of the originating agent or system.

    - name: target
      type: string
      required: false
      description: >
        Explicit target agent or system. If null, routing rules determine target.

task that spawned this one (for sub-task trees). - name: type type: string required: true description: > Task type determining routing and handler selection. Format: <domain>.<action> (e.g., odoo.create_invoice, agent.evaluate). - name: source type: string required: true description: Identifier of the originating agent or system. - name: target type: string required: false description: > Explicit target agent or system. If null, routing rules determine target.

    - name: priority
      type: enum
      values: [critical, high, normal, low, background]
      required: true
      default: normal
      description: Scheduling priority. Critical tasks preempt lower priorities.

    - name: state
      type: string
      required: true
      description: Current task state per the state machine below.

    - name: payload
      type: object
      required: true
      description: >
        Task-specific data. Schema depends on task type. Must be JSON-serializable.

    - name: metadata
      type: object
      required: false
      description: >
        Non-functional metadata (trace IDs, timestamps, retry count, cost tracking).

    - name: created_at
      type: timestamp_iso8601
      required: true
      description: Task creation time in UTC.

    - name: updated_at
      type: timestamp_iso8601
      required: true
      description: Last state transition time in UTC.

    - name: expires_at
      type: timestamp_iso8601
      required: false
      description: >
        Task expiry time. Expired tasks transition to timed_out.
        Default: created_at + 1 hour for normal priority.

    - name: attempt
      type: integer
      required: true
      default: 0
      description: Current retry attempt number (0 = first execution).

    - name: result
      type: object
      required: false
      description: >
        Task output. Populated when state transitions to completed or failed.

in UTC. - name: expires_at type: timestamp_iso8601 required: false description: > Task expiry time. Expired tasks transition to timed_out. Default: created_at + 1 hour for normal priority. - name: attempt type: integer required: true default: 0 description: Current retry attempt number (0 = first execution). - name: result type: object required: false description: > Task output. Populated when state transitions to completed or failed.

    - name: error
      type: object
      required: false
      description: >
        Error details when state is failed or dead_letter.
        Fields: code (string), message (string), stack (string, optional).

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task States
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

states:
  - id: pending
    description: Task created and awaiting pickup by a handler.
    transitions_to:
      - assigned
      - cancelled
      - timed_out

  - id: assigned
    description: Task claimed by a specific agent or handler.
    transitions_to:
      - running
      - pending        # released back to queue
      - cancelled

  - id: running
    description: Task actively being executed by the assigned handler.
    transitions_to:
      - completed
      - failed
      - blocked
      - timed_out

  - id: completed
    description: Task finished successfully with a result payload.
    transitions_to: []  # terminal

  - id: failed
    description: Task execution failed. May be retried depending on policy.
    transitions_to:
      - pending         # retry
      - dead_letter     # exhausted retries

  - id: blocked
    description: Task waiting on an external dependency or human approval.
    transitions_to:
      - running         # dependency resolved
      - cancelled
      - timed_out

  - id: cancelled
    description: Task explicitly cancelled by source, operator, or policy.
    transitions_to: []  # terminal

# terminal - id: failed description: Task execution failed. May be retried depending on policy. transitions_to: - pending # retry - dead_letter # exhausted retries - id: blocked description: Task waiting on an external dependency or human approval. transitions_to: - running # dependency resolved - cancelled - timed_out - id: cancelled description: Task explicitly cancelled by source, operator, or policy. transitions_to: [] # terminal

  - id: timed_out
    description: Task exceeded its expiry window without completing.
    transitions_to:
      - pending         # retry if retries remain
      - dead_letter     # exhausted retries

  - id: dead_letter
    description: >
      Task permanently failed after exhausting retries. Requires human investigation.
    transitions_to: []  # terminal

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Routing
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

routing:
  strategy: rule_based_with_capability_fallback
  description: >
    Tasks route by explicit target first, then by type-based rules, then by
    capability matching against the agent registry.

  rules:
    - condition: "type starts_with 'odoo.'"
      target: odoo_agent
      description: All Odoo domain tasks route to the Odoo agent.

    - condition: "type starts_with 'data.'"
      target: data_agent
      description: Data pipeline and analytics tasks.

    - condition: "type starts_with 'agent.evaluate'"
      target: judge_agent_pool
      description: Evaluation tasks fan out to judge agents.

    - condition: "type starts_with 'infra.'"
      target: infra_agent
      description: Infrastructure provisioning and management.

    - condition: "type starts_with 'notify.'"
      target: notification_service
      description: Human notifications via Slack or email.

    - condition: "type starts_with 'approval.'"
      target: human_approval_queue
      description: Tasks requiring human-in-the-loop sign-off.

"type starts_with 'data.'" target: data_agent description: Data pipeline and analytics tasks. - condition: "type starts_with 'agent.evaluate'" target: judge_agent_pool description: Evaluation tasks fan out to judge agents. - condition: "type starts_with 'infra.'" target: infra_agent description: Infrastructure provisioning and management. - condition: "type starts_with 'notify.'" target: notification_service description: Human notifications via Slack or email. - condition: "type starts_with 'approval.'" target: human_approval_queue description: Tasks requiring human-in-the-loop sign-off.

    - condition: "target is null AND no rule matches"
      target: unroutable_queue
      description: >
        Tasks with no explicit target and no matching rule land here
        for manual triage.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Failure Handling
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

failure_handling:
  max_retries: 3
  retry_backoff:
    strategy: exponential
    base_delay_ms: 1000
    max_delay_ms: 60000
    jitter: true
  dead_letter_policy:
    destination: dead_letter_queue
    notification:
      channel: slack
      target: "#agent-platform-alerts"
    retention: 30d
    requires_human_review: true
  timeout_defaults:
    critical: 300s
    high: 600s
    normal: 3600s
    low: 7200s
    background: 86400s

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Correlation
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

description: > Tasks with no explicit target and no matching rule land here for manual triage. # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Failure Handling # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- failure_handling: max_retries: 3 retry_backoff: strategy: exponential base_delay_ms: 1000 max_delay_ms: 60000 jitter: true dead_letter_policy: destination: dead_letter_queue notification: channel: slack target: "#agent-platform-alerts" retention: 30d requires_human_review: true timeout_defaults: critical: 300s high: 600s normal: 3600s low: 7200s background: 86400s # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Correlation # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

correlation:
  id_format: "uuidv4"
  description: >
    Correlation IDs group related tasks into logical workflows. A single
    user request may spawn a tree of correlated tasks across multiple agents.
  propagation_rules:
    - Sub-tasks inherit the parent's correlation_id.
    - Sub-tasks set parent_task_id to the spawning task's task_id.
    - Correlation ID must be passed through all inter-agent calls.
    - External system calls include correlation_id in request headers (X-Correlation-ID).
    - Audit log entries reference correlation_id for full workflow reconstruction.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Observability
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

observability:
  metrics:
    - task_throughput_per_second
    - task_latency_p50_p95_p99
    - queue_depth_by_priority
    - retry_rate_by_type
    - dead_letter_rate
    - agent_utilization
  tracing:
    propagation: W3C Trace Context
    sampling_rate: 1.0  # 100% in dev, reduce in production
  logging:
    format: structured_json
    fields:
      - task_id
      - correlation_id
      - type
      - state
      - source
      - target
      - duration_ms

# SSOT: Assistant Surfaces — Product Taxonomy
# Three distinct surfaces on one shared runtime plane.
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/agents/ASSISTANT_SURFACES.md
# Updated: 2026-03-23

version: "1.0.0"

assistant_surfaces:
  odoo_copilot:
    role: erp_transactional_assistant
    scope: authenticated
    grounding_model:
      - odoo_runtime_context
      - curated_odoo_docs
      - bounded_web_fallback
    action_mode: read_only_first
    launch_posture: internal_beta
    ssot_ref: ssot/agents/diva_copilot.yaml#modes.odoo
    a2a_role: participant
    a2a_enabled: true
    a2a_allowed_targets:
      - diva_copilot
      - genie
      - document_intelligence
      - studio_copilot
    a2a_default_mode: bounded_escalation

  diva_copilot:
    role: orchestration_and_schema_assistant
    scope: authenticated
    routing_modes:
      - strategy
      - odoo
      - tax_guru
      - capability
      - governance
    action_mode: route_and_assemble
    ssot_ref: ssot/agents/diva_copilot.yaml
    a2a_role: hub
    a2a_enabled: true
    a2a_allowed_targets:
      - odoo_copilot
      - studio_copilot
      - genie
      - document_intelligence
      - tax_guru
      - capability_assistant
      - governance_assistant
    a2a_default_mode: route_and_aggregate

  studio_copilot:
    role: creative_finishing_and_mediaops_assistant
    scope: authenticated
    workflow_backbone:
      - capture_ingest
      - ai_polish
      - brand_presets
      - platform_exports
      - scheduling_publish_handoff
      - analytics_next_steps
    generation_policy:
      stills_fast: gemini_direct
      stills_premium: imagen
      mixed_media: fal
      multimodal_review: openai
    product_name: W9 Studio
    a2a_role: participant
    a2a_enabled: true
    a2a_allowed_targets:
      - diva_copilot
      - genie
      - document_intelligence
      - odoo_copilot
    a2a_default_mode: workflow_handoff

a2a_enabled: true a2a_allowed_targets: - odoo_copilot - studio_copilot - genie - document_intelligence - tax_guru - capability_assistant - governance_assistant a2a_default_mode: route_and_aggregate studio_copilot: role: creative_finishing_and_mediaops_assistant scope: authenticated workflow_backbone: - capture_ingest - ai_polish - brand_presets - platform_exports - scheduling_publish_handoff - analytics_next_steps generation_policy: stills_fast: gemini_direct stills_premium: imagen mixed_media: fal multimodal_review: openai product_name: W9 Studio a2a_role: participant a2a_enabled: true a2a_allowed_targets: - diva_copilot - genie - document_intelligence - odoo_copilot a2a_default_mode: workflow_handoff

domain_intelligence_shells:
  marketing:
    diva_role: customer_360_campaign_brand_intelligence
    odoo_role: crm_campaigns_partners
    studio_role: campaign_creative_social_content
  media:
    diva_role: audience_content_monetization_qoe
    odoo_role: scheduling_rights_revenue
    studio_role: content_production_platform_export
  retail:
    diva_role: inventory_supply_chain_demand_forecasting
    odoo_role: purchase_orders_inventory_sales
    studio_role: product_visuals_catalog
  financial_operations:
    diva_role: growth_protection_efficiency_risk_compliance
    odoo_role: journals_invoices_tax_close
    studio_role: financial_reports_rare
  doctrine: >
    Diva decides/routes/analyzes.
    Odoo executes/governs/tracks.
    Studio creates/polishes/exports when creative activation is relevant.

naming_rules:
  use:
    - Odoo Copilot
    - Diva Copilot
    - W9 Studio
    - Studio Copilot
    - Genie
    - Document Intelligence
  avoid:
    - calling everything Diva
    - calling Genie a copilot
    - calling public landing assistant Odoo Copilot
    - making Studio Copilot sound like generic social-media chatbot
    - using Foundry as user-facing brand

doctrine: > Diva decides/routes/analyzes. Odoo executes/governs/tracks. Studio creates/polishes/exports when creative activation is relevant. naming_rules: use: - Odoo Copilot - Diva Copilot - W9 Studio - Studio Copilot - Genie - Document Intelligence avoid: - calling everything Diva - calling Genie a copilot - calling public landing assistant Odoo Copilot - making Studio Copilot sound like generic social-media chatbot - using Foundry as user-facing brand

a2a_interop:
  status: planned
  doctrine_ref: docs/architecture/A2A_INTEROP.md
  doctrine_summary: >
    Use A2A for delegate/handoff/coordinate/aggregate across distinct assistant
    surfaces. NOT for simple retrieval or single-step tool calls.
  hub_assistant: diva_copilot
  initial_message_types:
    - delegate_task
    - request_context
    - return_result
  tenancy_rules:
    customer_tenant_id_required: true
    workspace_id_required_when: workspace_scoped_surface
    public_surfaces_excluded: true
    cross_tenant_delegation: forbidden
  prohibited_patterns:
    - a2a_for_every_tool_call
    - a2a_for_simple_retrieval
    - recursive_delegation_loops
    - silent_delegation
    - public_surface_initiating_a2a
    - bypassing_hub_for_cross_domain_aggregation
  max_delegation_depth: 2
  rollout:
    phase_1:
      target: "Q2 2026"
      scope: hub_to_participant_read_only
      participants: [odoo_copilot, genie]
    phase_2:
      target: "Q3 2026"
      scope: full_participant_mesh_with_writes
      participants: [odoo_copilot, genie, studio_copilot, document_intelligence]
    phase_3:
      target: "Q4 2026"
      scope: multi_tenant_production
      features: [cross_workspace, sla_enforcement, external_onboarding]

hub_assistant: diva_copilot initial_message_types: - delegate_task - request_context - return_result tenancy_rules: customer_tenant_id_required: true workspace_id_required_when: workspace_scoped_surface public_surfaces_excluded: true cross_tenant_delegation: forbidden prohibited_patterns: - a2a_for_every_tool_call - a2a_for_simple_retrieval - recursive_delegation_loops - silent_delegation - public_surface_initiating_a2a - bypassing_hub_for_cross_domain_aggregation max_delegation_depth: 2 rollout: phase_1: target: "Q2 2026" scope: hub_to_participant_read_only participants: [odoo_copilot, genie] phase_2: target: "Q3 2026" scope: full_participant_mesh_with_writes participants: [odoo_copilot, genie, studio_copilot, document_intelligence] phase_3: target: "Q4 2026" scope: multi_tenant_production features: [cross_workspace, sla_enforcement, external_onboarding]
