# SSOT: AI Agent Policies
# Behavioral policies governing agent modes.
# Cross-referenced by: agents.yaml (policy_ref)

policies:
  policy-read-only:
    label: Read-Only
    description: >
      Agent can only read and respond. No writes, no drafts,
      no tool calls that mutate state.
    write_access: none
    tool_access: none
    memory: inherit          # inherits from physical agent default (false)
    citation_required: true  # must cite sources when using retrieval
    grounding_required: true # must use knowledge grounding, no fabrication
    failure_behavior: >
      If retrieval returns no results or tools are unavailable,
      state clearly that the information is not available.
      Never fabricate an answer.

  policy-draft-only:
    label: Draft-Only
    description: >
      Agent can produce draft content (documents, emails, reports)
      but cannot commit or send. Human approval required.
    write_access: draft-only
    tool_access: none
    memory: inherit
    citation_required: true
    grounding_required: true
    failure_behavior: >
      If unable to draft, explain what information is missing.
      Never produce content with fabricated data.

  policy-bounded-write:
    label: Bounded Write (Future)
    description: >
      Agent can propose bounded CRUD operations on an allowlist
      of models. All proposals require human approval before commit.
      v1: this policy is defined but not active (no tools wired).
    write_access: draft-then-approve
    tool_access: bounded
    memory: inherit
    citation_required: true
    grounding_required: true
    allowed_models:          # future: when tools are wired
      - res.partner
      - sale.order
      - purchase.order
      - account.move
    blocked_models:
      - ir.model
      - ir.model.fields
      - ir.rule
      - ir.config_parameter
      - res.users
    failure_behavior: >
      If a requested operation is outside the allowed model set
      or requires elevated permissions, refuse and explain why.

# SSOT: AI Agent Prompt References
# Maps logical agents to their instruction sections.
# Cross-referenced by: agents.yaml (prompt_ref)
# Full instruction text: ssot/ai/foundry_instructions.md

prompts:
  prompt-ask-agent:
    label: Ask Agent Instructions
    agent_ref: ask-agent
    instruction_source: ssot/ai/foundry_instructions.md
    section: "## Ask Agent Mode"
    behavior:
      - answer questions using retrieval results
      - cite sources when retrieval is used
      - never fabricate data or citations
      - refuse gracefully when information is unavailable

  prompt-authoring-agent:
    label: Authoring Agent Instructions
    agent_ref: authoring-agent
    instruction_source: ssot/ai/foundry_instructions.md
    section: "## Authoring Agent Mode"
    behavior:
      - draft documents, reports, and emails
      - use retrieved knowledge for accuracy
      - mark all output as DRAFT
      - never send or commit without human approval

  prompt-livechat-agent:
    label: Livechat Agent Instructions
    agent_ref: livechat-agent
    instruction_source: ssot/ai/foundry_instructions.md
    section: "## Livechat Agent Mode"
    behavior:
      - answer visitor questions using knowledge base
      - maintain professional, helpful tone
      - escalate to human agent when uncertain
      - never expose internal system details

  prompt-transaction-agent:
    label: Transaction Agent Instructions
    agent_ref: transaction-agent
    instruction_source: ssot/ai/foundry_instructions.md
    section: "## Transaction Agent Mode"
    behavior:
      - assist with bounded CRUD operations
      - always produce drafts, never commit directly
      - show preview of proposed changes
      - require explicit human approval before any mutation

# SSOT: AI Knowledge Sources
# Data sources available for knowledge grounding.
# Cross-referenced by: agents.yaml (source_refs)

sources:
  source-odoo-knowledge:
    label: Odoo Knowledge Base
    type: azure-ai-search
    description: >
      Azure AI Search index containing Odoo documentation,
      module help text, process descriptions, and company policies.
    connection_config_param: ipai_odoo_copilot.foundry_search_connection
    index_config_param: ipai_odoo_copilot.foundry_search_index
    content_types:
      - odoo-module-docs
      - company-policies
      - process-guides
      - faq
    refresh_strategy: manual   # v1: manual index updates
    citation_format: >
      When citing from this source, include the document title
      and section. Format: [Source: <title> § <section>]
    status: planned            # index not yet provisioned
    used_by:
      - ask-agent
      - authoring-agent
      - livechat-agent
      - transaction-agent

tools:

# SSOT: AI Agent Topic Domains
# Knowledge domains the agents can address.
# Cross-referenced by: sources.yaml, prompts.yaml

topics:
  odoo-erp:
    label: Odoo ERP Operations
    description: >
      General Odoo CE operations: modules, settings, workflows,
      records, reports, and administration.
    agents:
      - ask-agent
      - authoring-agent
      - transaction-agent

  finance:
    label: Finance & Accounting
    description: >
      Chart of accounts, journal entries, invoicing, BIR compliance,
      payroll, month-end close, financial reports.
    agents:
      - ask-agent
      - authoring-agent
      - transaction-agent

  hr:
    label: Human Resources
    description: >
      Employee records, leave management, attendance, expense
      liquidation, payroll computation.
    agents:
      - ask-agent
      - authoring-agent

  helpdesk:
    label: Helpdesk & Support
    description: >
      Ticket management, SLA tracking, customer support workflows.
    agents:
      - ask-agent
      - livechat-agent

  website:
    label: Website & Public
    description: >
      Public-facing website questions, product information,
      company policies.
    agents:
      - livechat-agent

version: "1.0.0"

# --- Active Services (Reduced Azure-Native Stack) ---

active_services:

  # Identity Plane
  entra_id:
    role: identity_authority
    type: azure_native
    scope: [sso, agent_identity, rbac, conditional_access]
    odoo_integration: "OAuth provider → /auth_oauth/signin (Odoo 19 native)"
    note: "Canonical IdP for all services. Replaces Keycloak."

  # Edge Plane
  azure_front_door:
    role: edge_routing
    type: azure_native
    resource: ipai-fd-dev
    scope: [tls_termination, waf, public_ingress]

  # ERP Plane
  odoo_ce_19:
    role: operational_system_of_record
    type: aca_hosted
    resources: [ipai-odoo-dev-web, ipai-odoo-dev-worker, ipai-odoo-dev-cron]
    database: pg-ipai-odoo
    scope: [erp, crm, hr, finance, inventory, approvals, documents]
    note: "Transactional SoR. Foundry agents call Odoo APIs — they do not own business state."

  # AI Plane
  azure_ai_foundry:
    role: ai_build_govern_scale
    type: azure_native
    resource: aifoundry-ipai-dev
    scope: [agent_runtime, orchestration, model_management, eval, safety]
    note: "Pulser agentic behavior plane. agents/ owns behavior, Foundry owns execution."

  azure_openai:
    role: llm_inference
    type: azure_native
    resource: oai-ipai-dev
    scope: [text_generation, eval_judges, multimodal_understanding]

  azure_doc_intelligence:
    role: document_extraction
    type: azure_native
    resource: docai-ipai-dev
    scope: [ocr, invoice_extraction, bir_forms, custom_models, receipt_extraction, vendor_bills]
    flow: "File → Odoo attachment → Doc Intel → structured output → Odoo record"
    note: "Use Read/Layout for documents. Use Azure AI Vision OCR for non-document images."

    decommissioned: 2026-03-25
    note: "ACA app deleted, DNS removed."
  - name: shelf
    reason: "Replaced by Odoo Knowledge + Databricks"
    decommissioned: 2026-03-25
    note: "ACA app deleted, DNS removed."
  - name: standalone_crm
    reason: "Redundant — Odoo CRM is canonical"
    decommissioned: 2026-03-25
    note: "ACA app deleted, DNS removed."
  - name: keycloak
    reason: "Never operationalized — Entra is target IdP"
    decommissioned: 2026-03-25
    note: "ACA app deleted, DNS removed."
  - name: cloudflare_proxy
    reason: "Proxy/WAF role retired — Front Door handles edge. DNS authority remains on Cloudflare until Azure DNS migration."
    retired: 2026-03-25
    note: "Cloudflare DNS-only mode is still active. See active_services.cloudflare_dns."

# --- Doctrinal Split ---
# Entra authenticates. Foundry thinks. Document Intelligence reads. Odoo records.

# SSOT: Tenancy Model
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/TENANCY_MODEL.md
# Updated: 2026-03-24

version: "1.0.0"

tenant_identifiers:
  customer_tenant_id:
    scope: business_tenant
    description: Maps to a customer org / Odoo company
    example: cust_abc123
    note: Primary business boundary. Never conflate with entra_tenant_id.

  entra_tenant_id:
    scope: identity_plane
    description: Microsoft Entra directory identifier
    example: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
    note: Identity only. Not a business tenant key.

  workspace_id:
    scope: creative_workspace
    description: Studio / creative workspace boundary
    example: ws_studio_001

  environment_id:
    scope: deployment_environment
    description: dev / staging / prod
    allowed_values:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod

  instance_id:
    scope: runtime_instance
    description: Service instance within an environment
    example: ipai-odoo-dev-web

isolation_planes:
  public:
    tenant_awareness: none
    surfaces:
      - marketing_landing
      - public_docs
      - public_assistant_advisory
    rule: No tenant data. No customer_tenant_id in request context.

  multitenant_control_plane:
    tenant_awareness: logical
    surfaces:
      - diva_copilot_orchestration
      - platform_metadata
      - governance_observability
    rule: All queries include customer_tenant_id. Cross-tenant leakage is P0.

  tenant_isolated_execution:
    tenant_awareness: isolated
    surfaces:
      - odoo_erp_runtime
      - odoo_copilot
      - document_intelligence
      - genie_analytics
    rule: Company-scoped. No cross-company data in single response.

  tenant_isolated_creative:
    tenant_awareness: isolated
    surfaces:
      - w9_studio
      - studio_copilot
    rule: Workspace-scoped. Brand presets and assets do not leak.

No tenant data. No customer_tenant_id in request context. multitenant_control_plane: tenant_awareness: logical surfaces: - diva_copilot_orchestration - platform_metadata - governance_observability rule: All queries include customer_tenant_id. Cross-tenant leakage is P0. tenant_isolated_execution: tenant_awareness: isolated surfaces: - odoo_erp_runtime - odoo_copilot - document_intelligence - genie_analytics rule: Company-scoped. No cross-company data in single response. tenant_isolated_creative: tenant_awareness: isolated surfaces: - w9_studio - studio_copilot rule: Workspace-scoped. Brand presets and assets do not leak.

cross_plane_rules:
  - from: public
    to: control_plane
    requirement: authentication
  - from: control_plane
    to: execution_plane
    requirement: customer_tenant_id_propagated
  - from: execution_plane
    to: creative_workspace
    requirement: workspace_id_validated_against_customer_tenant_id

current_state:
  public: implemented
  multitenant_control_plane: partial
  tenant_isolated_execution: single_company_only
  tenant_isolated_creative: not_implemented
  note: Multi-customer tenancy is future. Current runtime is single-company.

schema_version: "1.0"
target_state: azure_devops_config
description: Canonical SSOT for Azure DevOps Project, Boards, and Pipeline environments.

execution_authority:
  source_control: github
  ci_cd: azure_devops
  github_actions: decommissioned  # effective 2026-03-18

project:

  source_control: github

boards:

    - epic
    - issue
    - task

    - github_app: true
      linking_syntax: "AB#{id}"

# =============================================================================
# Pipeline configuration
# =============================================================================
pipelines:
  strategy: yaml_in_repo
  path_prefix: .azure/pipelines/
  agent_policy:
    default_pool: ubuntu-latest
    self_hosted: deprecated  # ipai-build-pool removed from all stages 2026-03-19
    rationale: AzureCLI@2 + service connection eliminates VNet access requirement

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
current_topology:

    - name: ci-validation

    - name: azure-infra-deploy
      status: active

  environments:
    - name: insightpulseai-dev
      trigger: automatic
      branches:
        - main
    - name: insightpulseai-staging
      trigger: manual_approval
      approvers:
        - platform_owner
    - name: insightpulseai-prod
      trigger: manual_approval
      approvers:
        - platform_owner

target_topology:

    - stamp-delivery
    - runtime-delivery
    - quality-governance
  reference: docs/architecture/AZDO_TARGET_PIPELINE_ARCHITECTURE.md

  environment_classes:
    - shared-dev
    - shared-staging
    - shared-prod
    - stamp-dev
    - stamp-staging
    - stamp-prod

  deployment_policy:
    use_deployment_jobs: true

  multitenancy_model:
    architecture_style: shared_control_plane_with_selective_data_plane_isolation
    deployment_pattern: deployment_stamps
    rollout_patterns:
      - deployment_rings
      - feature_flags

  stamp_policy:
    deploy_unit: stamp
    required_metadata:
      - stamp_id
      - environment
      - region
      - ring
    optional_metadata:
      - tenant_segment
      - shard_id
    reference: ssot/azure/stamp_topology.yaml

  secrets_policy:
    default_model: key_vault_backed
    isolation_modes:
      - shared
      - per_stamp
      - per_tenant_when_required

platform-shared-delivery - stamp-delivery - runtime-delivery - quality-governance reference: docs/architecture/AZDO_TARGET_PIPELINE_ARCHITECTURE.md environment_classes: - shared-dev - shared-staging - shared-prod - stamp-dev - stamp-staging - stamp-prod deployment_policy: use_deployment_jobs: true multitenancy_model: architecture_style: shared_control_plane_with_selective_data_plane_isolation deployment_pattern: deployment_stamps rollout_patterns: - deployment_rings - feature_flags stamp_policy: deploy_unit: stamp required_metadata: - stamp_id - environment - region - ring optional_metadata: - tenant_segment - shard_id reference: ssot/azure/stamp_topology.yaml secrets_policy: default_model: key_vault_backed isolation_modes: - shared - per_stamp - per_tenant_when_required

upstream_reference_authority:
  odoo_reference_map: ssot/odoo/upstream_reference_map.yaml
  odoo_ci_translation: ssot/odoo/upstream_ci_translation.yaml
  github_actions_adoption: false
  rationale: >
    Official Odoo upstream barely uses GitHub Actions for core runtime/release

    intentional and upstream-aligned.

service_connections:

    environments:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod

#
# Maps every Azure resource to the target architecture box and a
# keep/delete/review/consolidate decision.
#
# Target boxes:
#   1  = Source / Ingest
#   3  = Storage
#   4/7 = Foundry / gen-AI / agent plane
#   6  = BI / reporting
#   9  = Databricks core / governance
#   11 = Platform / network / runtime / DevOps
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-22

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DELETE NOW — safe strays
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
delete_now:
  - resource: pg-ipai-dev
    rg: rg-ipai-data-dev
    type: PostgreSQL Flexible Server
    target_box: 1
    justification: >
      Stray generic PG server. pg-ipai-odoo is the canonical Odoo source
      feeding Databricks. No active app binding found.
    precondition: Confirm no app references this host

  - resource: id-ipai-aca-dev
    rg: rg-ipai-agents-dev
    type: Managed Identity
    target_box: 11
    justification: >
      Leftover from old ACA lane (rg-ipai-dev, now deleted).
      New ACA apps use system-assigned identities.
    precondition: Confirm no ACA app references this identity

      Duplicate Foundry estate. data-intel-ph-resource in rg-data-intel-ph
      is the canonical Foundry hub.

  storage_sprawl:
    keep: stipaidevlake
    review:
      - stipaidev
      - stipaiodoodev
    target: stipaidevlake for lakehouse, one for Odoo filestore
    precondition: Check container/blob references

# =============================================================================
# Azure Services Matrix — Target State (SSOT)
# =============================================================================
#
# Canonical framing:
#   Databricks  = governed data backbone
#   AI Foundry  = primary gen-AI / agent plane
#   Fabric / Power BI = downstream semantic and reporting plane
#   Azure platform = control envelope
#
# Tier 1: canonical (must have)
# Tier 2: bounded / conditional (add only with justification)
# Tier 3: not core (out of scope)
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-22
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-22"

canonical_framing: >
  Databricks is the governed data intelligence backbone;
  AI Foundry is the agent and generative AI application plane;
  Fabric / Power BI is the downstream semantic and reporting plane;
  Purview is the governance overlay;
  Azure + Entra + GitHub + Azure DevOps form the control and engineering envelope.

services:
  # =========================================================================
  # Tier 1 — Canonical
  # =========================================================================

  - name: AI Foundry
    tier: 1
    role: Primary agent + gen-AI application plane
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep_and_expand
    owner: ai
    notes: >
      Agent runtime, model deployments, orchestration, tracing, evals.
      Complements Databricks (data) with Foundry (gen-AI apps).

  - name: Azure
    tier: 1
    role: Core landing zone / infra / identity / networking / runtime
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep
    owner: infra

  - name: Azure DevOps
    tier: 1
    role: Governance spine — pipelines, environments, approvals, Boards
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep
    owner: devops

in_use target_status: keep_and_expand owner: ai notes: > Agent runtime, model deployments, orchestration, tracing, evals. Complements Databricks (data) with Foundry (gen-AI apps). - name: Azure tier: 1 role: Core landing zone / infra / identity / networking / runtime current_status: in_use target_status: keep owner: infra - name: Azure DevOps tier: 1 role: Governance spine — pipelines, environments, approvals, Boards current_status: in_use target_status: keep owner: devops

  - name: Databricks
    tier: 1
    role: Governed data intelligence backbone
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep_and_expand
    owner: lakehouse
    notes: >
      Ingest, process, serve, governed data products.
      Bundle-based CI/CD. Unity Catalog governance anchor.

  - name: Entra ID
    tier: 1
    role: Canonical identity, RBAC, service principals, workload identities
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep
    owner: infra

  - name: Fabric / Power BI
    tier: 1
    role: Downstream semantic and reporting plane
    current_status: target_consumption
    target_status: keep_downstream_only
    owner: platform
    notes: >
      Published semantic models, analyst reports, Copilot BI.
      NOT a Databricks bundle deployment target.

  - name: GitHub
    tier: 1
    role: Repo / PR / engineering truth
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep
    owner: devops

  - name: Purview
    tier: 1
    role: Enterprise metadata, lineage, governance overlay
    current_status: target
    target_status: keep_and_add
    owner: infra
    notes: >
      Unified governance across Databricks + Foundry + Fabric.
      Platform integration concern, not bundle-local.

  - name: Visual Studio Code
    tier: 1
    role: Primary local/dev IDE surface
    current_status: in_use
    target_status: keep
    owner: devops

  # =========================================================================
  # Tier 2 — Bounded / Conditional
  # =========================================================================

    current_status: not_active
    target_status: keep_bounded
    owner: platform
    notes: >
      Copilot licensing, agent publish/reject workflows,
      Copilot Control System. Not core runtime.

  - name: Copilot Studio
    tier: 2
    role: Low-code M365-facing business copilots
    current_status: not_active
    target_status: optional
    owner: platform
    notes: >
      Use only for Teams/M365 distribution. Foundry is primary agent factory.

  - name: Power Automate
    tier: 2
    role: Business workflow automation adjunct
    current_status: not_active
    target_status: optional
    owner: platform
    notes: >
      Only where it beats Databricks Jobs / Foundry / existing automation.

  - name: Power Platform

    target_status: optional_bounded
    owner: platform

  - name: Defender

pattern: deployment_stamps

routing_model:
  tenant_to_stamp_resolution: required
  rollout_controls:
    - deployment_ring
    - feature_flag

stamp_classes:
  - id: shared_small_tenant_stamp
    isolation: multitenant
    intended_for:
      - smb
      - standard_tier

  - id: isolated_enterprise_stamp
    isolation: single_tenant_or_dedicated
    intended_for:
      - regulated
      - high_scale
      - custom_isolation

required_tags:
  - stamp_id
  - environment
  - region
  - ring

optional_tags:
  - shard_id
  - tenant_segment

rules:
  - no_global_all_stamp_deploy_by_default: true
  - one_pipeline_run_targets_one_stamp_or_bounded_stamp_batch: true
  - tenant_routing_metadata_must_be_explicit: true

workspace_target_grouping:

naming_rules:

view_policy:

    rule: supabase_is_platform_control_plane_and_app_backend_not_sor_or_lakehouse
  - name: power_bi_boundary
    rule: power_bi_is_semantic_consumption_layer_not_data_intelligence_core
  - name: portal_template_boundary
    rule: guided_deployment_and_portal_templates_are_reference_only_not_canonical_runtime
  - name: managed_identity_first
    rule: prefer_managed_identity_over_static_secrets_where_possible

# SSOT Contract: Analytics Pipeline (Odoo → Databricks → Power BI)
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §4.7
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

pipeline:
  source:
    system: odoo_postgresql
    resource: pg-ipai-odoo
    access: jdbc_read_only
    auth: service_principal (databricks_extract)
    tables:
      - sale_order
      - sale_order_line
      - account_move
      - account_move_line
      - crm_lead
      - hr_expense
      - hr_expense_sheet
      - project_project
      - project_task
      - documents_document
      - res_partner
    extraction: incremental_by_write_date

  transform:
    engine: databricks_dlt
    repo: data-intelligence/pipelines/
    layers:
      bronze:
        format: delta
        schema: bronze_odoo
        policy: "Raw ingestion. No transformation. Append-only."
      silver:
        format: delta
        schema: silver_odoo
        policy: "Cleaned, typed, deduplicated. PII tagged. SCD Type 2 for dimensions."
      gold:
        format: delta
        schema: gold_business
        policy: "Business metrics, KPIs, aggregations. Ready for reporting."

  govern:
    catalog: unity_catalog
    policies:
      - data_lineage_tracking
      - column_level_access_control
      - pii_tagging_and_masking
      - quality_rules_per_table
    access: entra_sso_plus_catalog_acls

  serve:
    endpoint: databricks_sql_warehouse
    consumers:
      - power_bi (directquery or import)
      - foundry_agents (read-only for Pulser Genie)

  present:
    tool: power_bi
    connection: databricks_sql_endpoint
    dashboards:
      - revenue_pipeline
      - expense_tracking
      - document_processing_metrics
      - crm_conversion_funnel
      - project_utilization

text:
# SSOT Contract: Public API Facade
# The facade sits between browser and Odoo. No direct browser-to-Odoo coupling.
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/PUBLIC_TO_ODOO_INTEGRATION_FLOW.md
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

facade:
  name: ipai-api-facade
  type: aca_container
  repo: web/packages/api-facade/
  runtime: node  # or python — thin proxy, either works

responsibilities:
  - json_schema_validation
  - captcha_verification
  - rate_limiting
  - input_sanitization
  - odoo_json_rpc_proxy
  - error_abstraction

does_not:
  - store_data
  - authenticate_users
  - execute_business_logic
  - reason_about_data
  - expose_odoo_errors

endpoints:
  leads:
    path: /api/v1/leads
    method: POST
    input_schema:
      type: object
      required: [name, email]
      properties:
        name: { type: string, maxLength: 200 }
        email: { type: string, format: email }
        company: { type: string, maxLength: 200 }
        message: { type: string, maxLength: 2000 }
        captcha_token: { type: string }
    odoo_call: crm.lead.create
    response: { status: 201, body: { id: integer, status: "created" } }

  demo:
    path: /api/v1/demo
    method: POST
    input_schema:
      type: object
      required: [name, email]
      properties:
        name: { type: string, maxLength: 200 }
        email: { type: string, format: email }
        preferred_date: { type: string, format: date }
        captcha_token: { type: string }
    odoo_call: calendar.event.create
    response: { status: 201, body: { id: integer, status: "created" } }

  contact:
    path: /api/v1/contact
    method: POST
    input_schema:
      type: object
      required: [name, email, subject]
      properties:
        name: { type: string, maxLength: 200 }
        email: { type: string, format: email }
        subject: { type: string, maxLength: 200 }
        body: { type: string, maxLength: 5000 }
        captcha_token: { type: string }
    odoo_call: mail.mail.create
    response: { status: 201, body: { status: "sent" } }

id: integer, status: "created" } } contact: path: /api/v1/contact method: POST input_schema: type: object required: [name, email, subject] properties: name: { type: string, maxLength: 200 } email: { type: string, format: email } subject: { type: string, maxLength: 200 } body: { type: string, maxLength: 5000 } captcha_token: { type: string } odoo_call: mail.mail.create response: { status: 201, body: { status: "sent" } }

security:
  odoo_api_key_source: azure_key_vault
  odoo_api_key_name: odoo-api-key-facade
  network: internal_vnet_only
  ingress: front_door_only
  captcha_provider: turnstile  # or reCAPTCHA v3

# SSOT Contract: Document Upload / OCR / Extraction
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §4.5
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

flow:
  trigger: ir.attachment.create
  trigger_types: [invoice, receipt, vendor_bill, expense_receipt, bir_form, custom_document]

  steps:
    - step: 1
      action: Odoo sends binary to Document Intelligence
      endpoint: "{doc_intel_endpoint}/documentModels/{model}:analyze"
      auth: managed_identity
      models:
        invoice: prebuilt-invoice
        receipt: prebuilt-receipt
        general: prebuilt-read
        layout: prebuilt-layout
        bir_form: custom-bir-2307  # custom-trained model
      addons: [highResolution, barcodes, keyValuePairs]

    - step: 2
      action: Document Intelligence returns structured JSON
      output:
        text: extracted_full_text
        tables: extracted_tables
        key_values: extracted_key_value_pairs
        layout: page_layout_elements
        confidence: per_field_confidence_score

    - step: 3
      action: Odoo maps extracted data to target model
      mapping:
        invoice: account.move (type=in_invoice)
        receipt: hr.expense
        vendor_bill: account.move (type=in_invoice)
        bir_form: ipai_bir.filing
        general: documents.document

    - step: 4
      action: Confidence check + Pulser review
      threshold: 0.85
      below_threshold: flag_for_human_review
      pulser_agent: pulser-docs-agent
      review_actions: [verify_amounts, check_vendor, validate_tax_id, flag_anomaly]

    - step: 5
      action: Odoo stores final record + original attachment
      attachment: linked_to_target_record
      audit: mail.tracking + auditlog

security:
  upload_size_limit: 50MB
  allowed_mime_types: [application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png, image/tiff]
  auth: entra_bearer
  network: internal_vnet (OCR container)
  pii_handling: "Documents may contain PII. Process in-region (Southeast Asia). No external logging of content."

text:
# SSOT Contract: Foundry Agent Tool Bindings
# Maps Foundry agent tools to backend services.
# Rule: Foundry reasons and orchestrates. Odoo records. Doc Intel reads.
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §4.3
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

tools:
  # Odoo tools (read/write business data)
  odoo_read_records:
    target: odoo_json_rpc
    method: search_read
    auth: managed_identity_to_api_key
    direction: read
    used_by: [pulser_erp, pulser_diva, pulser_genie]
    key_vault_secret: odoo-api-key-pulser

  odoo_create_record:
    target: odoo_json_rpc
    method: create
    auth: managed_identity_to_api_key
    direction: write
    used_by: [pulser_erp, pulser_diva]
    key_vault_secret: odoo-api-key-pulser
    note: "Creates draft records. Human approval required for financial transactions."

  odoo_execute_action:
    target: odoo_json_rpc
    method: call_kw
    auth: managed_identity_to_api_key
    direction: write
    used_by: [pulser_erp, pulser_diva]
    key_vault_secret: odoo-api-key-pulser
    note: "Executes Odoo actions (stage transitions, activity assignments). Respects Odoo permission model."

  # Document Intelligence tools
  doc_intel_analyze:
    target: document_intelligence_api
    method: analyze
    auth: managed_identity
    direction: read
    used_by: [pulser_docs]
    models: [prebuilt-read, prebuilt-layout, prebuilt-invoice, prebuilt-receipt]
    key_vault_secret: doc-intel-key

  # Search tools
  search_query:
    target: azure_ai_search
    method: search
    auth: managed_identity
    direction: read
    used_by: [pulser_diva, pulser_genie, ask_pulser]

  # Creative tools
  creative_generate:
    target: gemini_direct_api
    method: generate_image
    auth: api_key
    direction: read
    used_by: [pulser_studio]
    key_vault_secret: gemini-api-key
    policy_ref: ssot/creative/provider_policy.yaml

metadata.title: SSOT Contract: Foundry Agent Tool Bindings

# Agent-to-tool access matrix
access_matrix:
  pulser_erp:     [odoo_read_records, odoo_create_record, odoo_execute_action, search_query]

# SSOT Contract: Identity / Authentication
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §7
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

identity_provider: microsoft_entra_id

# Entra app registrations
app_registrations:

  insightpulseai_odoo:
    purpose: Odoo ERP SSO
    redirect_uri: erp.insightpulseai.com/auth_oauth/signin
    scopes: [User.Read]
    conditional_access: mfa_for_finance_group
    note: "Entra provides SSO. Odoo retains groups, record rules, finance permissions."

  pulser_gateway:
    purpose: Pulser copilot gateway
    auth_type: managed_identity
    key_vault_access: [openai-api-key, gemini-api-key]
    scopes: [odoo_api_custom]

  pulser_diva_agent:
    purpose: Pulser Diva orchestration agent
    auth_type: managed_identity
    key_vault_access: [odoo-api-key-pulser]
    foundry_binding: true

  pulser_studio_agent:
    purpose: Pulser Studio creative agent
    auth_type: managed_identity
    key_vault_access: [gemini-api-key, fal-api-key]
    odoo_access: none

  pulser_docs_agent:
    purpose: Pulser Docs extraction agent
    auth_type: managed_identity
    key_vault_access: [doc-intel-key]
    odoo_access: read_only

  ask_pulser_agent:
    purpose: Public Ask Pulser (anonymous)
    auth_type: managed_identity
    key_vault_access: [openai-api-key]
    odoo_access: none
    pii_access: none

  databricks_extract:
    purpose: Databricks JDBC extract from Odoo PG
    auth_type: service_principal
    key_vault_access: [pg-odoo-jdbc-password]
    odoo_access: read_only_jdbc

# Auth boundaries
auth_model:
  user_authentication: entra_id  # SSO for all human users
  user_authorization: odoo       # groups, record rules, finance permissions
  agent_identity: entra_agent_id # managed identity per agent
  service_to_service: managed_identity_to_key_vault
  public_api: front_door_waf_plus_captcha

managed_identity key_vault_access: [doc-intel-key] odoo_access: read_only ask_pulser_agent: purpose: Public Ask Pulser (anonymous) auth_type: managed_identity key_vault_access: [openai-api-key] odoo_access: none pii_access: none databricks_extract: purpose: Databricks JDBC extract from Odoo PG auth_type: service_principal key_vault_access: [pg-odoo-jdbc-password] odoo_access: read_only_jdbc # Auth boundaries auth_model: user_authentication: entra_id # SSO for all human users user_authorization: odoo # groups, record rules, finance permissions agent_identity: entra_agent_id # managed identity per agent service_to_service: managed_identity_to_key_vault public_api: front_door_waf_plus_captcha

# What Entra does NOT do
entra_does_not:
  - replace_odoo_groups
  - replace_odoo_record_rules
  - manage_finance_permissions
  - store_business_authorization
  - control_odoo_menu_visibility

# Deploy identity principals
deploy_identities:
  azure_devops:
    name: InsightPulseAI Azure DevOps Automation
    app_id: ae2df138  # ipai-azure-devops-automation-prod
    type: service_principal
    role: deploy_principal
    scope: Azure DevOps pipeline execution (ACA, ACR, infra)
    note: "This is the deploy authority for Azure DevOps pipelines."

  github_oidc:
    name: ipai-github-oidc
    type: federated_credential
    role: deploy_principal
    scope: GitHub Actions OIDC to Azure (website deploys, CI checks)
    note: "Federated identity for GitHub Actions. Uses OIDC, no stored secrets."

  # Workload identities are NOT deploy principals
  workload_identities_note: >
    ACA managed identities (ipai-odoo-dev-web, ipai-copilot-gateway, etc.)
    are workload identities for runtime token acquisition (Key Vault, Foundry,
    Databricks). They are NOT deploy principals and must not be used for
    pipeline or CI/CD execution.

role: deploy_principal scope: GitHub Actions OIDC to Azure (website deploys, CI checks) note: "Federated identity for GitHub Actions. Uses OIDC, no stored secrets." # Workload identities are NOT deploy principals workload_identities_note: > ACA managed identities (ipai-odoo-dev-web, ipai-copilot-gateway, etc.) are workload identities for runtime token acquisition (Key Vault, Foundry, Databricks). They are NOT deploy principals and must not be used for pipeline or CI/CD execution.

  # Legacy / decommission
  n8n_identities_note: >
    n8n-related identities (n8n-entra app registration) are legacy from the
    n8n automation era. n8n is decommissioned from the active baseline.
    These identities should be rotated and eventually deleted.

        phase: 2

# ODOO API IDENTITY
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

odoo_api_identity:
  auth_method: bearer_api_key
  api_surface: json_2
  endpoint_pattern: "/json/2/<model>/<method>"
  user_type: dedicated_bot_user
  user_name: chatgpt-connector@insightpulseai.com
  acl_policy: "Minimum required Odoo ACLs per feature group"
  secret_source: azure_key_vault
  secret_name: odoo-chatgpt-connector-api-key
  notes: >
    Model/method mapping is deployment-specific. Available models and
    fields vary by database and can be inspected via /doc. Tool names
    and scopes are stable; underlying mappings are tenant-specific.

destructiveHint: false idempotentHint: false # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # ODOO API IDENTITY # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── odoo_api_identity: auth_method: bearer_api_key api_surface: json_2 endpoint_pattern: "/json/2/<model>/<method>" user_type: dedicated_bot_user user_name: chatgpt-connector@insightpulseai.com acl_policy: "Minimum required Odoo ACLs per feature group" secret_source: azure_key_vault secret_name: odoo-chatgpt-connector-api-key notes: > Model/method mapping is deployment-specific. Available models and fields vary by database and can be inspected via /doc. Tool names and scopes are stable; underlying mappings are tenant-specific.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# HARD BOUNDARIES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

allowed:
  - search_read
  - read
  - message_post
  - create (on approved models only)
  - write (on approved fields only)
  - purpose-built wrapper methods

not_allowed:
  - arbitrary model selection
  - arbitrary method selection
  - generic execute_kw passthrough
  - bulk writes without explicit policy
  - direct destructive finance/inventory actions from free-form prompts
  - chained multi-step state changes (use single server-side method)

# SSOT Contract: Odoo Integration Points
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §4.4
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

integrations:

  entra_sso:
    direction: inbound
    protocol: oauth2_oidc
    auth: entra_app_registration
    odoo_module: auth_oauth  # Odoo 19 native
    redirect_uri: /auth_oauth/signin
    scopes: [User.Read]
    note: "Entra provides SSO. Odoo retains internal authorization (groups, record rules, finance permissions)."

  foundry_agents:
    direction: bidirectional
    protocol: json_rpc
    auth: api_key_via_key_vault
    key_vault_secret: odoo-api-key-pulser
    network: internal_vnet
    odoo_module: ipai_ai_copilot
    methods: [search_read, create, write, call_kw]
    note: "Foundry agents reason and orchestrate. Odoo is the transactional SoR."

  document_intelligence:
    direction: outbound
    protocol: rest
    auth: managed_identity
    odoo_module: ipai_doc_intelligence
    flow: "ir.attachment → Doc Intel API → structured output → account.move / hr.expense / documents.document"
    note: "Doc Intel extracts. Odoo stores records."

  zoho_smtp:
    direction: outbound
    protocol: smtp_tls
    port: 587
    auth: credentials_in_key_vault
    key_vault_secrets: [zoho-smtp-user, zoho-smtp-password]
    odoo_config: ir.mail_server
    note: "Odoo sends mail via standard ir.mail_server with Zoho SMTP."

  databricks_extract:
    direction: outbound
    protocol: jdbc
    auth: service_principal
    key_vault_secret: pg-odoo-jdbc-password
    access: read_only
    tables: [sale_order, account_move, crm_lead, hr_expense, project_project, documents_document]
    note: "Databricks extracts incrementally. No write access to Odoo."

→ account.move / hr.expense / documents.document" note: "Doc Intel extracts. Odoo stores records." zoho_smtp: direction: outbound protocol: smtp_tls port: 587 auth: credentials_in_key_vault key_vault_secrets: [zoho-smtp-user, zoho-smtp-password] odoo_config: ir.mail_server note: "Odoo sends mail via standard ir.mail_server with Zoho SMTP." databricks_extract: direction: outbound protocol: jdbc auth: service_principal key_vault_secret: pg-odoo-jdbc-password access: read_only tables: [sale_order, account_move, crm_lead, hr_expense, project_project, documents_document] note: "Databricks extracts incrementally. No write access to Odoo."

  api_facade:
    direction: inbound
    protocol: json_rpc
    auth: api_key_via_key_vault
    key_vault_secret: odoo-api-key-facade
    network: internal_vnet
    source: web/packages/api-facade/
    methods: [create]
    targets: [crm.lead, calendar.event, mail.mail]
    note: "Thin proxy for public form submissions. No browser-to-Odoo coupling."

# Prisma Consulting → InsightPulseAI Integration Contract
# Source repo: github.com/jgtolentino/Prismaconsulting (private)
# Target: Odoo SoR + Pulser AI assistant + Azure-native data plane

version: 1
created: "2026-03-28"
status: planned

# --- Source system ---
source:
  repo: jgtolentino/Prismaconsulting
  stack: React 18 + Vite 6 + Radix UI + Tailwind + Recharts
  current_backend: Supabase (DEPRECATED — remove entirely)
  current_ai: PrismaAssistant (RAG via Supabase pgvector + OpenAI — DEPRECATED)
  current_deploy: DigitalOcean (DEPRECATED)
  legacy_db_schema:
    table: prisma_document_embeddings
    columns: [id, content, embedding, metadata, source_file, chunk_index, created_at, updated_at]
    vector_dim: 1536  # text-embedding-3-small
    search_fn: match_documents(query_embedding, threshold, count)
    note: Schema to be recreated on Azure PostgreSQL. No data migration from Supabase.

# --- Target integration ---
target:
  erp: Odoo CE 19 (erp.insightpulseai.com)
  assistant_brand: Pulser
  data_plane: Azure PostgreSQL (pg-ipai-odoo) with pgvector extension
  ai_backend: Azure AI Foundry (oai-ipai-dev)
  embedding_model: text-embedding-3-small (via Foundry)
  search_alternative: Azure AI Search (srch-ipai-dev) if pgvector insufficient
  deploy_target: Azure Container Apps (ipai-website-dev or dedicated ACA)
  auth: Microsoft Entra (workforce) / local Odoo (fallback)

# --- Wiring plan ---
wiring:
  # 1. Replace Supabase with Azure PostgreSQL pgvector
  data_migration:
    from: supabase/migrations/001_vector_store.sql
    to: Azure PostgreSQL pg-ipai-odoo
    action: |
      - Enable pgvector extension on pg-ipai-odoo
      - Create prisma_document_embeddings table (same schema)
      - Create match_documents() function
      - Migrate existing embeddings if any
    auth: Managed Identity → Key Vault → connection string

dedicated ACA) auth: Microsoft Entra (workforce) / local Odoo (fallback) # --- Wiring plan --- wiring: # 1. Replace Supabase with Azure PostgreSQL pgvector data_migration: from: supabase/migrations/001_vector_store.sql to: Azure PostgreSQL pg-ipai-odoo action: | - Enable pgvector extension on pg-ipai-odoo - Create prisma_document_embeddings table (same schema) - Create match_documents() function - Migrate existing embeddings if any auth: Managed Identity → Key Vault → connection string

  # 2. Replace Supabase client with direct PG / Foundry client
  api_rewire:
    remove:
      - "'@supabase/supabase-js' dependency"
      - "'@jsr/supabase__supabase-js' dependency"
      - src/supabase/ functions
      - supabase/ directory
    add:
      - Azure Foundry SDK for embeddings
      - Direct PostgreSQL client (via API endpoint on Odoo or dedicated Azure Function)
    endpoints:
      embed: POST /api/v1/embed (Azure Function or Odoo controller)
      search: POST /api/v1/search (semantic search via match_documents)
      context: GET /api/v1/odoo/context (CRM/project data from Odoo)

  # 3. Rebrand PrismaAssistant → Pulser
  assistant_rebrand:
    component: src/components/PrismaAssistant.tsx
    rename_to: PulserAssistant.tsx
    changes:
      - Replace "Prisma" branding with "Pulser"
      - Wire to Foundry chat completion (not direct OpenAI)
      - Add Odoo context injection (current leads, projects, tasks)
      - Maintain RAG search against pgvector
      - Add CTA actions: send_prompt, navigate, open_scheduler, open_contact

  # 4. Odoo integration points
  odoo_integration:
    read:
      - res.partner (contacts/leads for context)
      - project.task (active tasks)
      - crm.lead (pipeline data)
    write:
      - crm.lead (create from assistant)
      - mail.message (log assistant interactions)
    auth: API key or OAuth token via /ipai/mail_plugin/session
    protocol: JSON-RPC 2.0

Add Odoo context injection (current leads, projects, tasks) - Maintain RAG search against pgvector - Add CTA actions: send_prompt, navigate, open_scheduler, open_contact # 4. Odoo integration points odoo_integration: read: - res.partner (contacts/leads for context) - project.task (active tasks) - crm.lead (pipeline data) write: - crm.lead (create from assistant) - mail.message (log assistant interactions) auth: API key or OAuth token via /ipai/mail_plugin/session protocol: JSON-RPC 2.0

  # 5. Deploy to Azure
  deployment:
    from: DigitalOcean
    to: Azure Container Apps (or Azure Static Web Apps for SPA)
    domain: prisma.insightpulseai.com (or subdomain TBD)
    tls: Azure Front Door
    ci: Azure DevOps Pipeline

# --- Dependencies ---
dependencies:
  blocked_by:
    - ipai_mail_plugin module (C-1 from audit — backend endpoints)
    - pgvector extension enabled on pg-ipai-odoo
    - Azure Foundry embedding endpoint operational
  nice_to_have:
    - ipai_auth_oidc module (for Entra SSO on the consulting site)
    - Pulser widget shared component (from web/ipai-landing/)

# --- Sequence ---
sequence:
  - phase: 0  # Can start now
    tasks:
      - Enable pgvector on pg-ipai-odoo
      - Create schema (port 001_vector_store.sql)
      - Rebrand PrismaAssistant → PulserAssistant in source
      - Remove Supabase dependencies from package.json

  - phase: 1  # After ipai_mail_plugin exists
    tasks:
      - Wire search endpoint to Azure PG pgvector
      - Wire embedding endpoint to Azure Foundry
      - Add Odoo context injection via mail_plugin bridge
      - Deploy to Azure Container Apps

  - phase: 2  # Polish
    tasks:
      - Add Entra SSO (after ipai_auth_oidc)
      - Migrate historical embeddings from Supabase
      - Add Pulser CTA actions (navigate, scheduler, contact)
      - Wire to Azure Front Door with custom domain

# SSOT: Creative Evaluation Policy
# Scoring dimensions, thresholds, retry rules for AI-generated assets
# Implementation: infra/ai/provider_router/creative_eval.py
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: 1

image_dimensions:
  - id: prompt_adherence
    weight: 0.25
    description: "Does the image match what was requested?"
  - id: composition
    weight: 0.15
    description: "Layout balance, hierarchy, visual weight distribution"
  - id: aesthetic_quality
    weight: 0.15
    description: "Overall polish, lighting, color harmony, professional finish"
  - id: text_rendering_accuracy
    weight: 0.20
    description: "Legibility, spelling, font quality of any text in the image"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.5
  - id: brand_compliance
    weight: 0.15
    description: "Correct brand names, no deprecated terms, style alignment"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.5
  - id: artifact_detection
    weight: 0.10
    description: "No corrupted regions, watermarks, gibberish, broken anatomy"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.3

video_dimensions:
  - id: prompt_adherence
    weight: 0.25
    description: "Does the video match what was requested?"
  - id: temporal_coherence
    weight: 0.25
    description: "Frame-to-frame consistency, no flicker, stable subjects"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.4
  - id: motion_quality
    weight: 0.15
    description: "Camera stability, natural movement, no jitter"
  - id: visual_quality
    weight: 0.15
    description: "Resolution, sharpness, color grading, professional look"
  - id: brand_compliance
    weight: 0.10
    description: "Correct brand names if visible, style alignment"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.5
  - id: artifact_detection
    weight: 0.10
    description: "No corrupted frames, morphing errors, or visual glitches"
    hard_fail_if_below: 0.3

thresholds:
  pass: 0.85
  warn: 0.70
  fail_below: 0.70

hard_fail_if_below: 0.4 - id: motion_quality weight: 0.15 description: "Camera stability, natural movement, no jitter" - id: visual_quality weight: 0.15 description: "Resolution, sharpness, color grading, professional look" - id: brand_compliance weight: 0.10 description: "Correct brand names if visible, style alignment" hard_fail_if_below: 0.5 - id: artifact_detection weight: 0.10 description: "No corrupted frames, morphing errors, or visual glitches" hard_fail_if_below: 0.3 thresholds: pass: 0.85 warn: 0.70 fail_below: 0.70

surface_presets:
  brand_og:
    text_rendering_accuracy_min: 0.9
    brand_compliance_min: 0.95
    overall_min: 0.85
  landing_hero:
    composition_min: 0.8
    aesthetic_quality_min: 0.8
    overall_min: 0.80
  product_ui:
    prompt_adherence_min: 0.85
    composition_min: 0.8
    overall_min: 0.80
  ad_creative:
    text_rendering_accuracy_min: 0.9
    brand_compliance_min: 0.95
    aesthetic_quality_min: 0.8
    overall_min: 0.85
  video_teaser:
    temporal_coherence_min: 0.8
    motion_quality_min: 0.75
    overall_min: 0.75

retry_policy:
  max_attempts: 3
  reroute_on_fail:
    stills_fast: stills_standard
    stills_standard: stills_premium
    stills_premium: reject
    video_default: reject
  retry_triggers:
    - score_below_warn
    - hard_fail_dimension
    - brand_violation
  no_retry_triggers:
    - prompt_fundamentally_wrong
    - unsafe_content

model_comparison:
  image_models:
    - id: nano-banana-2-preview
      tier: fast
      cost_relative: 1.0
    - id: nano-banana-pro-preview
      tier: standard
      cost_relative: 3.0
    - id: imagen-4
      tier: premium
      cost_relative: 5.0
  video_models:
    - id: veo-3.1-preview
      tier: premium
      cost_relative: 10.0

overall_min: 0.85 video_teaser: temporal_coherence_min: 0.8 motion_quality_min: 0.75 overall_min: 0.75 retry_policy: max_attempts: 3 reroute_on_fail: stills_fast: stills_standard stills_standard: stills_premium stills_premium: reject video_default: reject retry_triggers: - score_below_warn - hard_fail_dimension - brand_violation no_retry_triggers: - prompt_fundamentally_wrong - unsafe_content model_comparison: image_models: - id: nano-banana-2-preview tier: fast cost_relative: 1.0 - id: nano-banana-pro-preview tier: standard cost_relative: 3.0 - id: imagen-4 tier: premium cost_relative: 5.0 video_models: - id: veo-3.1-preview tier: premium cost_relative: 10.0

brand_rules:
  required_corporate: InsightPulseAI
  required_assistant: Pulser
  allowed_public_names:
    - InsightPulseAI
    - Pulser
    - Odoo on Cloud
    - Cloud Operations
    - Analytics & Dashboards
    - Ask Pulser
    - Pulser for Odoo
  forbidden_public_names:
    - Odoo Copilot
    - Ask Odoo Copilot
    - Pulsar
    - InsightPulse AI
  text_rendering_check: true
  forbidden_in_generated_text: true

references:
  generation_service: infra/ai/provider_router/creative.py
  eval_runner: infra/ai/provider_router/creative_eval.py
  judge_image: agents/evals/creative/judge_image.md
  judge_video: agents/evals/creative/judge_video.md
  rubric_image: agents/evals/creative/rubric_image.yaml
  rubric_video: agents/evals/creative/rubric_video.yaml
  golden_fixtures: agents/evals/creative/fixtures/
  benchmark: agents/evals/creative/benchmark.py

# SSOT: Creative Provider Policy
# Generation vs understanding split is mandatory.
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/CREATIVE_PROVIDER_POLICY.md
# Updated: 2026-03-23

version: "1.0.0"

canonical_rule: >
  Use OpenAI to understand, evaluate, extract, route, and assist.
  Use Gemini / Imagen / fal to generate by modality and quality tier.

creative_surfaces:
  landing_creative_studio:
    primary_provider: gemini_direct
    primary_models:
      default: gemini-2.5-flash-image
      premium: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
    secondary_provider: imagen
    use_cases:
      - concepting
      - conversational_image_editing
      - ad_mockups
      - social_visuals
      - fast_variants
    fal_enabled: false
    fal_enable_condition:
      - video_generation
      - audio_generation
      - multimodal_public_studio

  agency_workspace:
    architecture: provider_broker
    image_default:
      provider: gemini_direct
      models:
        - gemini-2.5-flash-image
        - gemini-3-pro-image-preview
    image_quality_lane:
      provider: imagen
      models:
        - imagen-4.0-generate-001
        - imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001
        - imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001
    media_lane:
      provider: fal
      capabilities:
        - image_generation
        - image_editing
        - video_generation
        - speech
        - music
        - sound_effects
    default_policy:
      stills_editing: gemini_direct
      premium_brand_assets: imagen
      ugc_video_audio: fal

  batch_asset_generation:
    image_fast_lane:
      provider: gemini_direct
      model: gemini-2.5-flash-image
    image_quality_lane:
      provider: imagen
      model_family: imagen-4
    mixed_media_lane:
      provider: fal
      mode: async_queue_webhook

- audio_generation - multimodal_public_studio agency_workspace: architecture: provider_broker image_default: provider: gemini_direct models: - gemini-2.5-flash-image - gemini-3-pro-image-preview image_quality_lane: provider: imagen models: - imagen-4.0-generate-001 - imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001 - imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 media_lane: provider: fal capabilities: - image_generation - image_editing - video_generation - speech - music - sound_effects default_policy: stills_editing: gemini_direct premium_brand_assets: imagen ugc_video_audio: fal batch_asset_generation: image_fast_lane: provider: gemini_direct model: gemini-2.5-flash-image image_quality_lane: provider: imagen model_family: imagen-4 mixed_media_lane: provider: fal mode: async_queue_webhook

openai_multimodal_role:
  doctrine: >
    Use OpenAI to understand, evaluate, extract, route, and assist.
    Use Gemini / Imagen / fal to generate by modality and quality tier.
  primary_jobs:
    - multimodal_understanding
    - vision_and_document_understanding
    - ocr_alternative_extraction
    - pdf_parsing_for_rag
    - image_evaluation
    - creative_quality_review
    - multimodal_rag
    - speech_and_transcript_processing
    - asset_captioning_and_tagging
    - multimodal_assistant_orchestration
  non_primary_jobs:
    - default_still_image_generation
    - premium_brand_still_generation
    - mixed_media_generation
    - ugc_video_generation
    - audio_music_sfx_generation

surface_policy:
  landing_creative_studio:
    openai_role:
      enabled: true
      jobs:
        - multimodal_qa
        - captioning
        - tagging
        - public_assistant_understanding
      default_generation_provider: gemini_direct
      premium_still_provider: imagen
      mixed_media_provider: fal

# Service principal and identity provisioning for Finance PPM workload
id: finance_ppm_identity
version: 1.0.0
status: planned

service_principals:
  - id: finance-ppm-service-principal
    display_name: Finance PPM Service Principal
    purpose: Run-as identity for Databricks jobs, DLT pipelines, and data access
    provisioning_status: not_provisioned
    required_permissions:
      - databricks_workspace_access
      - unity_catalog_grants (bronze, silver, gold schemas)
      - azure_key_vault_reader (kv-ipai-dev)
    environments:
      dev:
        workspace_root: /finance-ppm-dev
        catalog: dev_ppm
      staging:
        workspace_root: /finance-ppm-staging
        catalog: staging_ppm
      prod:
        workspace_root: /finance-ppm-prod
        catalog: ppm
    notes: >-
      Previously named notion-ppm-service-principal. Renamed to reflect
      the Finance PPM capability domain rather than a specific source system.

# =============================================================================
# Data Intelligence Platform Contract — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Canonical technology assignments for the data intelligence plane.
# Source: Microsoft reference architecture (Databricks + Fabric E2E) adapted
# for InsightPulse AI operating model.
#
# Ref: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azurearchitectureblog/data-intelligence-end-to-end-with-azure-databricks-and-microsoft-fabric/4232621
# Cross-refs:
#   - docs/architecture/data/ENTERPRISE_DATA_PLATFORM.md
#   - ssot/data-intelligence/migration_inventory.yaml
#   - ssot/foundry/runtime_inventory.yaml
# =============================================================================

schema_version: 1
kind: data_intelligence_platform_contract
last_reviewed: "2026-03-26"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Platform Role Assignments
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

lakehouse_platform: databricks
storage_platform: adls_gen2
governance_catalog: unity_catalog
metadata_plane: purview
bi_consumption_plane: power_bi_fabric

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Ingestion Technologies
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ingestion:
  batch:
    technology: auto_loader
    sources:
      - odoo_pg_extracts

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Modeling Pattern
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modeling_pattern:
  - bronze    # Raw/immutable landing
  - silver    # Validated/conformed, schema-enforced
  - gold      # Business-ready aggregates and data products

transformation_engine: delta_live_tables
compute_engine: photon

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Serving Model
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

serving:
  primary:
    technology: databricks_sql
    purpose: "Concurrency-optimized warehouse for gold-layer queries"
  consumption:
    technology: power_bi_semantic_models
    purpose: "Published from Databricks SQL, consumed in Fabric dashboards and Copilot"
  supplemental:
    technology: superset
    purpose: "Platform-internal operational dashboards only"

invariants:
  - "Databricks is the engineering/transformation plane — Fabric is not"
  - "Fabric is the BI/Copilot consumption plane — Databricks SQL serves it"
  - "The analytical lake is never the source of truth for operational data"
  - "Reverse ETL is bounded, typed, and contract-governed"
  - "Business users access Power BI — they never query Databricks directly"
  - "Unity Catalog governs; Purview provides estate-wide visibility"

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "$id": "ipai.deployment_contract.v1",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["service_name", "runtime_profile", "image", "replicas", "resources", "storage", "secrets", "config", "probes", "environment", "promotion_policy"],
  "properties": {
    "service_name": { "type": "string" },
    "runtime_profile": { "type": "string", "enum": ["stateless_judge", "single_writer_supervisor", "task_router"] },
    "image": { "type": "string" },
    "replicas": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1 },
    "resources": { "type": "object" },
    "storage": { "type": "object" },
    "secrets": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } },
    "config": { "type": "object" },
    "probes": { "type": "object" },
    "environment": { "type": "string", "enum": ["dev", "staging", "prod"] },
    "promotion_policy": { "type": "string" }
  }
}

version: 1
environments:
  dev:
    promotion_allowed: true
    evidence_required: basic
  staging:
    promotion_allowed: true
    evidence_required: extended
  prod:
    promotion_allowed: conditional
    evidence_required: strict
    requires_topology_match: true

health_contract:
  endpoints:
    liveness: /healthz
    readiness: /readyz
    metrics: /metrics
  thresholds:
    timeout_seconds: 5
    failure_threshold: 3
    success_threshold: 1
  startup:
    grace_period_seconds: 30

version: 1
profiles:
  stateless_judge:
    storage: ephemeral_ok
    replicas_max: 3
    shared_lock_required: false
    readiness_required: true
    liveness_required: true
  single_writer_supervisor:
    storage: shared_persistent_required
    replicas_max: 1
    shared_lock_required: true
    readiness_required: true
    liveness_required: true
  task_router:
    storage: substrate_declared
    replicas_max: 2
    shared_lock_required: conditional
    readiness_required: true
    liveness_required: true

# Docs Content Build Manifest
# Schema: ssot.docs.build.v1
# Purpose: Build pipeline configuration for published documentation
# Updated: 2026-03-13

version: "1.0"
schema: ssot.docs.build.v1

content_roots:
  - path: docs-site/docs/
    type: authored_content
    format: markdown
  - path: docs/architecture/
    type: source_reference
    format: markdown
    note: "Referenced by published docs, not directly published"

static_roots:
  - path: docs-site/docs/stylesheets/
    type: css
  - path: docs-site/docs/assets/
    type: images_media

build:
  tool: mkdocs
  config: mkdocs.yml
  command: "mkdocs build"
  test_command: "mkdocs build --strict"
  serve_command: "mkdocs serve -a 127.0.0.1:8100"
  output_dir: docs-site/site/
  requirements: requirements-docs.txt

versioning:
  strategy: branch_per_major
  current_version: "1.0"
  version_field: site_url_path_prefix
  archive_policy: "Keep last 3 major versions published"

language:
  default: en
  supported: [en]
  future: [fil]
  policy: "English-first; Filipino when compliance requires"

validation:
  - name: build_succeeds
    command: "mkdocs build --strict"
    gate: ci_required
  - name: no_broken_links
    command: "linkchecker docs-site/site/"
    gate: ci_warning
  - name: content_lint
    command: "markdownlint docs-site/docs/"
    gate: ci_warning

publishing:
  target: azure_static_web_apps
  url: "https://docs.insightpulseai.com"
  ci_workflow: ".github/workflows/docs-publish.yml"
  trigger: "push to main (docs-site/ changes)"
  status: planned

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Consolidation rules (machine-readable reference)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
rules:
  stays_in_repo:
    - "Machine-readable SSOT (YAML, JSON, DBML, SQL)"
    - "CI/CD workflow definitions (.github/workflows/)"
    - "Code-adjacent docs (CLAUDE.md, module READMEs, inline docs)"
    - "CI-enforced contracts and schemas"
    - "Evidence bundles (docs/evidence/, CI-generated)"
    - "Docker/IaC configs (docker/, deploy/, infra/)"
    - "Lock files (oca.lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml)"
    - "Spec bundle constitutions (spec/*/constitution.md)"
    - "Agent configs (.claude/)"

  migrates_to_plane_wiki:
    - "Architecture overviews and strategy docs"
    - "PRD summaries (full PRD stays in repo, summary in wiki)"
    - "Delivery plans and roadmaps"
    - "Runbooks and operational procedures"
    - "Integration specs (human-readable portions)"
    - "Decision records (ADRs)"
    - "Onboarding guides"
    - "Governance and process docs"
    - "Release notes (human-readable)"

  archives_then_deletes:
    - "Duplicate content (same doc in multiple locations)"
    - "Stale content referencing deprecated systems"
    - "Draft documents with no review in 180+ days"
    - "Legacy migration artifacts from completed migrations"

# ATC Code Mapping — Canonical Crosswalk for Withholding Tax Codes
# This file exists because Odoo and platform/Supabase use different
# ATC code namespaces. No agent, benchmark, or production workflow
# involving withholding tax may proceed until this mapping is resolved.

version: 2
status: draft_crosswalk_complete
purpose: canonical_crosswalk_for_withholding_tax_codes
created_at: "2026-03-18"
updated_at: "2026-03-19"
blocker_ref: "ssot/evals/tax_bir_benchmark_gap_analysis.yaml#GAP-ATC-001"

canonical_namespace: bir_standard
canonical_namespace_description: >
  The canonical namespace uses BIR-standard Alphanumeric Tax Codes (ATC)
  as defined in RMC 19-2007, RMC 25-2011, and subsequent BIR issuances.
  Format: <prefix><number> where prefix indicates tax type:
    WI = Withholding tax on Income (expanded, creditable)
    WC = Withholding tax on Compensation
    WB = Withholding tax on Business income
    WV = Withholding tax on VAT (VAT withholding)
    WF = Withholding tax — Final
    GR = Government — Royalties
    FI = Final tax on Interest

systems:
  odoo:
    observed_namespace: W_series
    examples:
      - W010
      - W020
    source: "addons/ipai/ipai_bir_tax_compliance/"
    namespace_notes: >
      Simplified internal alias scheme. W010-W170 are shorthand codes
      that do NOT match BIR-published ATC codes. Used in ph_rates_2025.json,
      ewt.rules.yaml, and test fixtures. Must be translated to BIR-standard
      codes for any BIR form filing (1601-EQ, 1604-E, 2307).

    source: "/tmp/TaxPulse-PH-Pack-salvage/packs/ph/rates/ph_rates_2025.json"
    notes: >
      TaxPulse-PH-Pack uses identical W-series codes as the Odoo
      ipai_bir_tax_compliance module. Both originate from the same codebase.
      The W-series codes are simplified aliases (W010 = professional fees
      individuals 10%) rather than BIR-canonical ATC codes. The F-series
      codes (F001-F005) cover final withholding tax (interest, dividends,
      royalties, prizes). TaxPulse also uses WC/WF prefixes in bir_1601c.py
      compute_withholding_tax_from_moves() to classify tax lines by
      compensation (WC prefix) vs final (WF/F prefix) withholding.

Rate follows TRAIN Law graduated brackets. No corresponding Odoo W-series code exists — compensation is handled separately via payroll module.

  - canonical: "WC011"

    rate: 0

      BIR ATC WC011: Minimum wage earners are exempt from withholding tax

# ============================================================
# Reconciliation Notes
# ============================================================

reconciliation_notes:
  summary: >
    The ATC code namespace divergence has three layers:

    1. Odoo W-series (W010-W170, F001-F005): Simplified internal aliases
       originally adopted from TaxPulse-PH-Pack. These do NOT correspond to
       any BIR-published ATC code and cannot be used on BIR forms directly.

    2. Supabase WI/WC-series: Closer to BIR-standard ATC codes. The Supabase
       60_localization_ph.yaml uses proper BIR prefixes (WI for expanded/income
       withholding, WC for compensation withholding).

    3. BIR-standard ATC codes: The official codes published by the Bureau of
       Internal Revenue via Revenue Memorandum Circulars (RMC 19-2007,
       RMC 25-2011, and amendments). These are the legally required codes on
       BIR Forms 2307, 2306, 1601-EQ, 1601-FQ, 1601-C, 1604-E, 1604-CF.

metadata.title: ATC Code Mapping — Canonical Crosswalk for Withholding Tax Codes

text:
directly. 2. Supabase WI/WC-series: Closer to BIR-standard ATC codes. The Supabase 60_localization_ph.yaml uses proper BIR prefixes (WI for expanded/income withholding, WC for compensation withholding). 3. BIR-standard ATC codes: The official codes published by the Bureau of Internal Revenue via Revenue Memorandum Circulars (RMC 19-2007, RMC 25-2011, and amendments). These are the legally required codes on BIR Forms 2307, 2306, 1601-EQ, 1601-FQ, 1601-C, 1604-E, 1604-CF.

  key_findings:
    - >
      The Odoo W-series codes are a many-to-one mapping. W010, W030, W040,
      W050 all map to a single BIR ATC code WI010 (professional fees to
      individuals at 10%). The internal subdivisions are useful for reporting
      but must collapse to WI010 when generating BIR forms.
    - >
      The F-series codes (F001-F005) are also internal aliases. BIR uses
      WF-series (WF010, WF020, WF050) and GR-series (GR010) for final
      withholding taxes.
    - >
      The Supabase layer is closer to BIR-standard but has a rate discrepancy
      on WI020 (shows 15%, BIR standard is 10% per RR 11-2018).
    - >
      Compensation codes (WC010, WC011) exist only in Supabase. Odoo handles
      compensation through payroll modules, not through the EWT code table.

  action_required:
    - >
      Odoo module -- Add a bir_atc_code field to the rate lookup table to
      store the BIR-canonical code alongside the internal W-series alias.
      This allows the rules engine to use W-series internally while emitting
      BIR-standard codes on generated forms.
    - "Supabase -- Verify and correct the WI020 rate (10% vs 15%)."
    - >
      BIR form generation -- Any code that produces BIR Forms 2307/2306/1601-EQ
      must translate W-series to BIR-standard via this crosswalk.
    - >
      Test fixtures -- The atc_crosswalk_expected.csv provides validation data
      for unit tests that confirm the mapping is applied correctly.

# Rules (unchanged)
# ============================================================

  no_benchmark_pass_claim_for_withholding_tasks_without_mapping: true
  unmapped_code_must_fail_closed: true

next_actions:
  - Verify WI020 rate discrepancy between Supabase (15%) and BIR standard (10%)
  - Add bir_atc_code field to Odoo ipai_bir_tax_compliance rate tables
  - Confirm GR010 mapping for royalties (F004) against BIR ATC schedule
  - Confirm WF050 mapping for prizes (F005) against BIR ATC schedule
  - Add Supabase-side codes for final withholding (WF010, WF020, GR010, WF050)
  - Update agent rule pack to use canonical codes only
  - Run crosswalk validation test against atc_crosswalk_expected.csv

  # ===== BENCHMARK HARNESS GAPS =====

  - gap_id: GAP-BH-001
    category: benchmark_harness
    title: No end-to-end test harness (Odoo -> Supabase -> Agent)
    severity: high
    description: |
      The rules engine has unit tests in Python, and the Supabase tax-compute function
      is functional, but there is no integration test that verifies the full pipeline:
      Odoo posted moves -> webhook -> Supabase computation -> findings -> agent tools.
    current_state: Unit tests only in ipai_bir_tax_compliance
    target_state: End-to-end test with golden dataset, Odoo test moves, Supabase verification
    blocking: true
    recommended_action: |
      Build a test harness that:
      1. Creates test account.move records in Odoo test DB
      2. Triggers the tax-compute webhook
      3. Verifies findings in ops.tax_findings
      4. Verifies risk scoring matches expected values
    spec_ref: spec/tax-pulse-sub-agent/prd.md

  - gap_id: GAP-BH-002
    category: benchmark_harness
    title: BIR-specific eval datasets not created
    severity: medium
    description: |
      The PRD specifies 3 eval datasets (bir_advisory, bir_ops, bir_actions) with 120 total
      test cases, plus SFT training assets. None of these exist yet.
    current_state: Only copilot-level eval dataset (eval-dataset-v2.json) which partially covers BIR
    target_state: 3 dedicated BIR eval datasets + SFT training/validation sets
    blocking: false
    recommended_action: Create datasets per PRD spec (50+30+40 cases)
    spec_ref: spec/tax-pulse-sub-agent/prd.md

      Update Odoo rules and rates to use WI/WC codes.
      Add migration path for any persisted W-series data.

schema_version: 1
kind: entra_app_registrations
scope: azure_native_with_odoo_backend

tenant:
  primary_domain: insightpulseai.com
  fallback_domain: ceoinsightpulseai.onmicrosoft.com

selection_policy:
  include:
    - Azure/Microsoft-native platform operations
    - Odoo backend and Odoo workforce sign-in because Odoo is a first-class Azure-hosted workload
  exclude:
    - Tableau-specific identities
    - Diva Goals identities
    - legacy n8n identities
    - external-federation identities whose primary caller is not Azure-native or Odoo workload-native

benchmark_policy:
  sap_reference:
    enabled: true
    classification: benchmark_only
    implementation_dependency: false
    note: >
      SAP remains a reference benchmark for workload lifecycle/operator patterns.
      The Azure CLI workloads extension is SAP-focused and is not a canonical
      implementation dependency for the Odoo backend lane.

The Azure CLI workloads extension is SAP-focused and is not a canonical implementation dependency for the Odoo backend lane.

# SSOT: External Service Dependencies
# Services outside Azure that require explicit justification.
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §8
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

external_dependencies:

  squarespace:
    role: domain_registrar
    scope: "NS record delegation to Cloudflare (current) or Azure DNS (target)"
    does_not: [host_website, manage_dns_records, provide_email, serve_content]
    domain: insightpulseai.com
    note: "Registrar only. NS currently points to Cloudflare."

  cloudflare:
    role: authoritative_dns
    scope: "DNS-only mode for insightpulseai.com (proxy mode disabled)"
    does_not: [proxy_traffic, terminate_tls, serve_waf]
    note: "Current DNS authority. Azure DNS is target replacement (not deployed)."
    migration_target: azure_dns

  zoho_mail:
    role: domain_email_provider
    scope: "Inbound + outbound mail for insightpulseai.com"
    protocol: smtp_tls
    port: 587
    host: smtp.zoho.com
    credentials: azure_key_vault
    key_vault_secrets: [zoho-smtp-user, zoho-smtp-password]
    dns_records: [MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC]
    does_not: [host_website, manage_dns, provide_identity]

  gemini_direct_api:
    role: creative_image_generation
    justification: "Nano Banana / Imagen / Veo models not available on Azure OpenAI"
    models: [nano-banana-2-preview, nano-banana-pro-preview, imagen-4, veo-3.1-preview]
    credentials: azure_key_vault
    key_vault_secret: gemini-api-key
    migration_path: "Switchable to Vertex AI if needed"
    policy_ref: ssot/creative/provider_policy.yaml

  fal:
    role: mixed_media_generation
    justification: "Kling video, LTX, audio, SFX not available on Azure"
    models: [kling-video/v3/pro, ltx-2.3, audio models]
    credentials: azure_key_vault
    key_vault_secret: fal-api-key
    usage: "Via infra/ai/provider_router/ only"

does_not: [host_website, manage_dns, provide_identity] gemini_direct_api: role: creative_image_generation justification: "Nano Banana / Imagen / Veo models not available on Azure OpenAI" models: [nano-banana-2-preview, nano-banana-pro-preview, imagen-4, veo-3.1-preview] credentials: azure_key_vault key_vault_secret: gemini-api-key migration_path: "Switchable to Vertex AI if needed" policy_ref: ssot/creative/provider_policy.yaml fal: role: mixed_media_generation justification: "Kling video, LTX, audio, SFX not available on Azure" models: [kling-video/v3/pro, ltx-2.3, audio models] credentials: azure_key_vault key_vault_secret: fal-api-key usage: "Via infra/ai/provider_router/ only"

  github:
    role: source_control
    scope: "Git hosting, code review, issues"
    migration: "CI/CD migrating to Azure DevOps Pipelines"
    does_not: [host_infrastructure, manage_secrets, deploy_to_production]

# Retired external services
retired:
  - name: cloudflare_proxy
    was: edge_proxy_and_waf
    replaced_by: azure_front_door
    retired: 2026-03-25
    note: "Proxy role only. DNS authority remains on Cloudflare."
  - name: digitalocean
    was: hosting
    replaced_by: azure_container_apps
    retired: 2026-03-15
  - name: vercel
    was: web_deployment
    replaced_by: azure_container_apps
    retired: 2026-03-11
  - name: mailgun
    was: smtp
    replaced_by: zoho_smtp
    retired: 2026-03-11

# Unified Finance System — SSOT Ownership Artifact

version: "1.0.0"
schema: ssot.finance.v1
last_reviewed: "2026-03-13"

system:
  name: Unified Finance System
  scope:
    - BIR tax compliance (Philippine regulatory)
    - Month-end close (5-phase checklist)
    - Finance PPM / Clarity parity (portfolio management)
  team: TBWA\SMP Finance SSC
  team_size: 9

modules:
  active:
    - id: ipai_finance_ppm
      version: "19.0.1.0.0"
      layer: foundation
      installable: true
      role: Clarity PPM parity, OKR dashboard, analytic controls
      tests: false

    - id: ipai_bir_tax_compliance
      version: "18.0.1.0.0"  # pending bump to 19.0.1.0.0
      layer: foundation
      installable: true
      role: 36 eBIRForms, filing deadlines, compliance dashboard
      tests: false

    - id: ipai_bir_notifications
      version: "19.0.1.0.0"
      layer: satellite
      installable: false  # planned, not yet validated
      depends_on: ipai_bir_tax_compliance
      role: Email digest + urgent alerts for BIR deadlines
      tests: false

    - id: ipai_bir_plane_sync
      version: "19.0.1.0.0"
      layer: satellite
      installable: false  # planned, not yet validated
      depends_on: ipai_bir_tax_compliance
      role: Bidirectional sync with Plane.so
      tests: false

    - id: ipai_finance_close_seed
      version: "19.0.1.0.0"
      layer: data
      installable: true
      role: Seed data — 39 close tasks + 50 BIR tasks + team + milestones
      tests: false

  deprecated:
    - id: ipai_finance_workflow
      reason: Superseded by ipai_finance_close_seed
      installable: false

    - id: ipai_finance_tax_return
      reason: Migrated to ipai_enterprise_bridge
      installable: false
