# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Repo Ownership
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
repo_ownership:
  odoo:
    - "config/environments/"
    - "addons/ipai/"
    - "docker/Dockerfile.*"
    - ".github/workflows/ci-*"
  infra:
    - "infra/terraform/"
    - "infra/dns/"
    - "infra/keyvault/"
    - "infra/monitoring/"
    - "infra/frontdoor/"
  ops-platform:
    - "ops-platform/backup/"
    - "ops-platform/staging/"
    - "ops-platform/supabase/"
  agents:
    - "agents/foundry/"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Implementation Phases
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
phases:
  - id: 1
    name: foundation
    status: mostly_complete
    items:
      - "ACA runtime deployed ✓"
      - "PostgreSQL connectivity ✓"
      - "Key Vault secrets ✓"
      - "Cloudflare → Front Door ✓"
      - "CI workflows (partial) ✓"
      - "ACR with automated builds — pending"
      - "Per-env config files — pending"

  - id: 2
    name: ci_cd_pipeline
    status: planned
    items:
      - "Build pipeline: commit → image → test → push"
      - "Deploy pipeline: dev (auto) → staging (gated) → prod (gated)"
      - "Odoo test matrix (--test-enable per changed module)"
      - "Spec validation gate"
      - "Deployment evidence capture"

# =============================================================================
# Azure Pipelines Catalog — SSOT
# =============================================================================
#
# Every Azure DevOps pipeline definition in this repo, with disposition.
# This is the single source of truth for what exists, what to keep, and what
# to retire.
#
# Decision basis: ssot/governance/platform-authority-split.yaml (Option C)
# Dedup analysis: ssot/governance/ci-dedup-analysis.yaml
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-30"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "approved"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Template Spine (shared templates consumed by pipelines)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
templates:
  extends:
    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/extends/base-ci.yml
      description: >
        Policy-enforced CI skeleton. Provides pool defaults and stage passthrough.
        All CI entrypoints should extend this.

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/extends/base-deploy.yml
      description: >
        Policy-enforced deploy skeleton for container workloads.
        Stages: Build -> Deploy -> Smoke -> Evidence using shared job templates.

  jobs:
    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/build-container.yml
      description: Build and push container image to ACR

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/deploy-containerapp.yml
      description: Deploy container image to Azure Container Apps

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/deploy-bicep.yml
      description: Deploy Bicep template to a resource group

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/validate-bicep.yml
      description: Lint and validate Bicep templates

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/smoke-http.yml
      description: HTTP smoke test with retries and content matching

Deploy -> Smoke -> Evidence using shared job templates. jobs: - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/build-container.yml description: Build and push container image to ACR - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/deploy-containerapp.yml description: Deploy container image to Azure Container Apps - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/deploy-bicep.yml description: Deploy Bicep template to a resource group - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/validate-bicep.yml description: Lint and validate Bicep templates - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/smoke-http.yml description: HTTP smoke test with retries and content matching

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/publish-evidence.yml
      description: Publish deployment evidence artifacts

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/databricks-bundle-validate.yml
      description: Validate a Databricks bundle

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/databricks-bundle-promote.yml
      description: Promote a Databricks bundle across targets

    - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/foundry-eval.yml
      description: Run Foundry AI evaluation gate

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pipeline Catalog
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
pipelines:

  # =========================================================================
  # DEPLOY PIPELINES (AzDO is CD authority — Option C)
  # =========================================================================

smoke test with retries and content matching - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/publish-evidence.yml description: Publish deployment evidence artifacts - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/databricks-bundle-validate.yml description: Validate a Databricks bundle - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/databricks-bundle-promote.yml description: Promote a Databricks bundle across targets - yaml_path: azure-pipelines/templates/jobs/foundry-eval.yml description: Run Foundry AI evaluation gate # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Pipeline Catalog # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- pipelines: # ========================================================================= # DEPLOY PIPELINES (AzDO is CD authority — Option C) # =========================================================================

  - name: odoo-deploy
    yaml_path: azure-pipelines/odoo-deploy.yml
    folder: deploy/
    purpose: deploy
    owning_repo: odoo
    template_source: templates/jobs/build-container.yml, templates/jobs/deploy-containerapp.yml, templates/jobs/smoke-http.yml, templates/jobs/publish-evidence.yml
    triggers:
      branches: [main]
      paths: [addons/ipai/**, config/**, platform/runtime/docker/docker/Dockerfile.odoo-copilot]
    environments: [odoo-dev]
    depends_on: []
    supersedes: null
    status: keep
    authority_basis: >
      Odoo deploy requires ARM service connection (azure-ipai) and ACA update.
      AzDO is the CD authority for Odoo per Option C.
    notes: >
      Canonical Odoo deploy. Build -> Deploy ACA -> Smoke (erp.insightpulseai.com) -> Evidence.
      Uses shared job templates.

AzDO is the CD authority for Odoo per Option C. notes: > Canonical Odoo deploy. Build -> Deploy ACA -> Smoke (erp.insightpulseai.com) -> Evidence. Uses shared job templates.

  - name: odoo-container-deploy
    yaml_path: azure-pipelines/odoo-container-deploy.yml
    folder: deploy/
    purpose: deploy
    owning_repo: odoo
    template_source: templates/jobs/build-container.yml, templates/jobs/deploy-containerapp.yml, templates/jobs/smoke-http.yml, templates/jobs/publish-evidence.yml

    notes: >
      Detect changed bundles -> Validate -> Deploy to dev -> Smoke (SQL Warehouse health)

Inline deploy logic (no environment gates for dev).

  - name: databricks-bundles-promote

    status: keep
    authority_basis: >

    notes: >

      Stages: dev validate -> staging deploy -> prod deploy.
      Each stage uses AzDO deployment jobs with environment approval gates.

AzDO environment checks enforce the approval policy. notes: > Manual-trigger pipeline. Parameters: bundle name, git ref. Stages: dev validate -> staging deploy -> prod deploy. Each stage uses AzDO deployment jobs with environment approval gates.

  - name: web-landing-deploy
    yaml_path: azure-pipelines/web-landing-deploy.yml
    folder: deploy/
    purpose: deploy
    owning_repo: web
    template_source: templates/jobs/build-container.yml, templates/jobs/deploy-containerapp.yml, templates/jobs/smoke-http.yml, templates/jobs/publish-evidence.yml
    triggers:
      branches: [main]
      paths: [web/ipai-landing/**]
    environments: []
    depends_on: []
    supersedes: null
    status: keep
    authority_basis: >
      Warm standby pipeline. GitHub Actions (deploy-ipai-landing.yml) is the

    notes: >
      Build -> Deploy ACA (ipai-website-dev) -> Smoke (insightpulseai.com) -> Evidence.
      Currently inactive — GHA OIDC federation handles active deploys.

  - name: infra-deploy
    yaml_path: infra/ci/azure-pipelines.yml
    folder: deploy/
    purpose: infra-deploy
    owning_repo: infra
    template_source: templates/jobs/deploy-bicep.yml

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enforcement rules for CI contract test
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
enforcement:
  # Repos where prod deploy via GitHub Actions is forbidden
  azdo_cd_only:
    - odoo
    - data-intelligence
    - infra
    - agents
    - platform
    - agent-platform
    - mcp

  # Repos where prod deploy via Azure DevOps is forbidden
  gha_cd_only:
    - web
    - docs
    - design
    - mobile

azure_devops notes: "MCP server implementations deployed to ACA" # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Enforcement rules for CI contract test # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- enforcement: # Repos where prod deploy via GitHub Actions is forbidden azdo_cd_only: - odoo - data-intelligence - infra - agents - platform - agent-platform - mcp # Repos where prod deploy via Azure DevOps is forbidden gha_cd_only: - web - docs - design - mobile

  # Exception: a repo may declare mirror_only to have both systems
  # for warm-standby purposes (e.g., web has an AzDO pipeline blocked
  # on parallelism grant — not active prod deploy)
  mirror_exceptions:
    - repo: web
      mirror_system: azure_devops
      reason: "AzDO website pipeline is warm standby, blocked on parallelism grant"
      active: false

# =============================================================================
# CI Deduplication Analysis — Option C Authority Split
# =============================================================================
#
# Maps every CI check to its authoritative system (GHA or AzDO), identifies
# overlapping checks, and recommends keep/remove/consolidate for each.
#
# Context: azure-pipelines-ci.yml was created to replace GHA CI when billing
# was locked (2026-03). Option C decision restored GHA as CI authority.
# AzDO retains deploy authority for Odoo/Databricks/Infra only.
#
# Parent SSOTs:
#   - ssot/governance/platform-authority-split.yaml (decision: option_c)
#   - ssot/governance/ci-cd-authority-matrix.yaml (per-repo assignments)
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-30"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "analysis-complete"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Decision Summary
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
decision_summary: >
  GitHub Actions is the CI authority (Option C). The root azure-pipelines-ci.yml
  was created as a stopgap when GHA billing was locked. Now that GHA is restored
  as CI authority, azure-pipelines-ci.yml should be removed. AzDO pipelines under
  azure-pipelines/ are deploy-only and remain canonical.

azdo_ci_checks:
  - id: azdo-boundary-check
    stage: RepoSanity
    description: "Top-level directory allowlist, blocked file patterns, addons structure"
    equivalent_gha:
      - ssot-yaml-lint.yml (boundary-check job)
      - platform-authority-check.yml (governance YAML lint)
    overlap: full
    recommendation: remove
    rationale: >
      GHA workflows already enforce repo boundary rules. The allowlist in AzDO
      is a static copy that drifts from the GHA source. Single-source in GHA.

  - id: azdo-blocked-patterns
    stage: RepoSanity
    description: "Scan for committed .env files (non-template)"
    equivalent_gha:
      - "(implicit in repo-wide pre-commit / gitignore enforcement)"
    overlap: partial
    recommendation: consolidate
    rationale: >
      This check has value but belongs in GHA as a lightweight PR gate.
      If no existing GHA workflow covers .env scanning, create one.
      Remove from AzDO CI.

  wave_4:

# =============================================================================
# Databricks Auth Target State — SSOT
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines the canonical authentication model for Databricks access
# across local dev, CI/CD, and unattended workloads.
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-22
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-22"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "approved"

target_statement: >
  Azure CLI auth for human local development.
  AzureCLI@2 + service connection for Azure DevOps pipelines.
  Databricks OAuth M2M for Databricks-only unattended workloads.
  Federation/OIDC over long-lived secrets wherever possible.
  No hybrid identity dependency unless on-prem AD is actually in scope.

auth_targets:
  - surface: local_dev
    method: azure_cli
    identity: user_interactive
    target: enable_now
    why: >
      Clean for developers who already need Azure access.
      User sign-in for interactive local work.
    notes: >
      Use Entra service principal only for scripted local
      automation or headless tooling.

  - surface: azure_devops_pipelines
    method: azure_cli_v2_task
    identity: entra_service_principal
    target: enable_now
    why: >
      Matches Azure-first governance spine. Databricks explicitly
      documents AzureCLI@2 as the supported pattern for CLI/bundle
      commands with DATABRICKS_HOST set.
    config:
      task: AzureCLI@2
      auth: azure_service_connection
      env_var: DATABRICKS_HOST
    reference: >
      https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/dev-tools/auth/auth-with-azure-devops

  - surface: github_actions
    method: workload_identity_federation
    identity: oidc
    target: defer
    why: Avoids long-lived client secrets in GitHub
    notes: >
      Enable when GitHub Actions is used for Databricks work.
      Currently Azure DevOps is the primary CI/CD surface.

> Matches Azure-first governance spine. Databricks explicitly documents AzureCLI@2 as the supported pattern for CLI/bundle commands with DATABRICKS_HOST set. config: task: AzureCLI@2 auth: azure_service_connection env_var: DATABRICKS_HOST reference: > https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/dev-tools/auth/auth-with-azure-devops - surface: github_actions method: workload_identity_federation identity: oidc target: defer why: Avoids long-lived client secrets in GitHub notes: > Enable when GitHub Actions is used for Databricks work. Currently Azure DevOps is the primary CI/CD surface.

  - surface: databricks_native_automation
    method: databricks_oauth_m2m
    identity: databricks_service_principal
    target: enable_now
    why: >
      Databricks' preferred unattended auth for Databricks-only
      access. Short-lived OAuth tokens, no long-lived secrets.
    reference: >
      https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/dev-tools/auth/oauth-m2m

  - surface: cross_service_bots
    method: azure_cli
    identity: entra_service_principal
    target: enable_now
    why: >
      Use when authenticating to Databricks AND other Azure
      resources simultaneously.

do_not_target:
  - pattern: manual_pat_based_auth
    why: Not strategic — use OAuth or Azure CLI instead

  - pattern: long_lived_entra_client_secrets
    why: Prefer federation/OIDC; rotate if secrets are required

  - pattern: entra_connect_hybrid_sync
    why: Cloud-native target — no on-prem AD to integrate

  - pattern: cross_tenant_sync
    why: Single-tenant Entra — not needed unless multi-tenant

  - pattern: secret_based_auth_everywhere
    why: Federation and managed identity are preferred

# =============================================================================
# GitHub + Entra Target State Configuration — SSOT
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines the minimal enterprise control model for:
#   - GitHub Enterprise/Org access and security
#   - Entra ID identity authority
#   - GitHub Packages publishing strategy
#   - External collaboration model
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-22
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-22"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "approved"

canonical_statement: >
  GitHub = engineering and operational workflow surface.
  Entra = canonical identity authority.
  SAML + secure 2FA = enterprise access baseline.
  GitHub Packages = selective SDK/contract publishing only.
  External Identities = optional B2B collaboration layer.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub Enterprise — Auth & Security
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
github_enterprise:
  require_2fa:
    target: true
    when: enable_now
    why: Enterprise baseline — all members must use 2FA

  secure_2fa_methods_only:
    target: true
    when: enable_now
    why: Disallow SMS-based 2FA — require TOTP, WebAuthn, or passkeys

  require_saml_sso:
    target: true
    when: enable_now
    why: Entra is the canonical IdP — enforce SAML for all org access
    provider: entra_id
    notes: Configure after Entra SAML app registration

  ip_allow_list:
    target: false
    when: defer
    why: >
      Causes friction before stable egress model for CI runners,
      GitHub Apps, developer environments, and office IPs.
    enable_condition: >
      Enable only after documenting: office/static IPs, CI runner IPs,
      GitHub App IPs, developer environment egress

when: enable_now why: Entra is the canonical IdP — enforce SAML for all org access provider: entra_id notes: Configure after Entra SAML app registration ip_allow_list: target: false when: defer why: > Causes friction before stable egress model for CI runners, GitHub Apps, developer environments, and office IPs. enable_condition: > Enable only after documenting: office/static IPs, CI runner IPs, GitHub App IPs, developer environment egress

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub Org — Planning & Projects
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
github_org:
  projects_enabled:
    target: true
    when: enable_now

  member_project_visibility_changes:
    target: false
    when: enable_now
    why: Prevent members from changing project visibility without admin approval

  project_boards:
    - name: PROJECT TEMPLATE
      type: template
      status: keep
    - name: Execution Board
      type: execution
      status: keep
    - name: Roadmap
      type: roadmap
      status: keep

  issue_types:
    core:
      - Feature
      - Bug
      - Task
    optional:
      - Incident
      - Spike
    do_not_add:
      - Initiative  # use Azure Boards for portfolio-level planning
      - Epic        # use Azure Boards for epic-level planning
    notes: >
      Keep lean. Do not recreate Azure Boards hierarchy inside GitHub.
      GitHub is the engineering surface; Azure DevOps is the governance spine.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub Packages — Publishing Strategy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
github_packages:
  strategy: selective
  when: enable_now

- Task optional: - Incident - Spike do_not_add: - Initiative # use Azure Boards for portfolio-level planning - Epic # use Azure Boards for epic-level planning notes: > Keep lean. Do not recreate Azure Boards hierarchy inside GitHub. GitHub is the engineering surface; Azure DevOps is the governance spine. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # GitHub Packages — Publishing Strategy # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- github_packages: strategy: selective when: enable_now

  target_package_lanes:
    - repo: platform
      type: sdk_contracts
      status: target
    - repo: agents
      type: sdk_contracts
      status: target
    - repo: web
      type: shared_client_types
      status: target
    - repo: data-intelligence
      type: shared_contract
      status: optional

  current_packages:
    - name: ipai-superset
      status: existing
      notes: Review if still needed

  do_not_use_for:
    - runtime app artifacts
    - one-off internal modules
    - repo-local code that is not reused
    - commercial offers or plugin marketplace

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entra ID — Identity & Licensing
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
entra_id:
  license_target:
    minimum: P1
    preferred: P2
    why_p2: >
      Identity governance, access reviews, privileged workflows,
      higher-assurance enterprise controls match the platform ambition.

  external_identities:
    target: true
    when: enable_if_needed
    why: B2B collaborators, customer/partner guest access
    use_cases:
      - managed client workspace access
      - partner collaboration
      - external consultant access

  cross_tenant_sync:
    target: false
    when: defer
    why: Only needed if synchronizing across separate Entra tenants
    enable_condition: Multi-tenant deployment with shared identity needs

minimum: P1 preferred: P2 why_p2: > Identity governance, access reviews, privileged workflows, higher-assurance enterprise controls match the platform ambition. external_identities: target: true when: enable_if_needed why: B2B collaborators, customer/partner guest access use_cases: - managed client workspace access - partner collaboration - external consultant access cross_tenant_sync: target: false when: defer why: Only needed if synchronizing across separate Entra tenants enable_condition: Multi-tenant deployment with shared identity needs

  entra_connect:
    target: false
    when: defer
    why: Only needed for on-prem AD / hybrid identity sync
    enable_condition: On-premises Active Directory exists

  backup_recovery_preview:
    target: false
    when: ignore
    why: Preview feature, not in current license, not a planning blocker

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary Matrix
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
summary:
  enable_now:
    - "GitHub: Require 2FA"
    - "GitHub: Secure 2FA methods only"
    - "GitHub: Require SAML SSO via Entra"
    - "GitHub: Projects enabled"
    - "GitHub: Block member project visibility changes"
    - "GitHub: Issue types — Feature, Bug, Task"
    - "GitHub: Packages — selective SDK/contract publishing"
    - "Entra: P1 minimum (P2 preferred)"

  defer:
    - "GitHub: IP allow list"
    - "Entra: Cross-tenant sync"
    - "Entra: Entra Connect"
    - "GitHub: Broad package publishing"

  ignore:
    - "Entra: Backup/recovery preview"
    - "GitHub: Recreating Azure Boards hierarchy in issue types"
    - "GitHub: Plugin/offer commercialization via Packages"

# =============================================================================
# Identity Enforcement Checklist — SSOT
# =============================================================================
#
# Machine-readable checklist tracking identity and access enforcement items
# for the Insightpulseai GitHub organization + Entra ID integration.
#
# Sources:
#   ssot/governance/github-entra-target-state.yaml  — target state
#   infra/ssot/github/org-policy.yaml               — current drift
#   infra/ssot/auth/oidc_clients.yaml               — Entra tenant details
#
# Status values: not_started | in_progress | blocked | done
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-30"
owner: "platform-architecture"

# Entra tenant context
entra_tenant:
  directory: ceoinsightpulseai.onmicrosoft.com
  custom_domain: insightpulseai.com
  license: P2

github_org: Insightpulseai

# =============================================================================
# Enforcement Items
# =============================================================================
items:

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # 1. GitHub SAML SSO via Entra ID
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: github_saml_sso
    item: "GitHub SAML SSO enforcement via Entra ID"
    description: >
      Configure Entra ID as the SAML IdP for the Insightpulseai GitHub org.
      All org members must authenticate through Entra to access org resources.
    target: true
    current: false
    status: in_progress

# =============================================================================
# Enforcement Sequence (recommended order)
# =============================================================================
enforcement_sequence:
  - phase: 1
    label: "Foundation — teams and permissions"
    items:
      - default_repo_permission_read
      - disable_member_repo_creation
      - disable_private_fork
    rationale: >
      Establish least-privilege base before enabling identity enforcement.
      Create teams first so write access is not lost when base drops to read.

  - phase: 2
    label: "Authentication hardening"
    items:
      - github_2fa_enforcement
      - secure_2fa_methods
    rationale: >
      Enforce 2FA before SAML SSO. Members need valid 2FA before SAML
      becomes mandatory, reducing lockout risk.

  - phase: 3
    label: "Identity federation"
    items:
      - github_saml_sso
    rationale: >
      Enable SAML SSO enforcement after 2FA is in place and break-glass
      bypass is confirmed working. This is the final identity gate.

  - phase: 4
    label: "CI/CD hardening"
    items:
      - workflow_permissions_read
    rationale: >
      Downgrade workflow token permissions last, after all workflows
      have been audited and updated with explicit permissions blocks.

This is the final identity gate. - phase: 4 label: "CI/CD hardening" items: - workflow_permissions_read rationale: > Downgrade workflow token permissions last, after all workflows have been audited and updated with explicit permissions blocks.

# =============================================================================
# Operating Model — InsightPulse AI
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines tool authority boundaries between Azure DevOps and GitHub.
# Answers: "Which tool owns what?" for planning, code, CI/CD, and deployment.
#
# Cross-references:
#   - azdo-project-repo-alignment.yaml (AzDo project → repo mapping)
#   - repo-azure-alignment.yaml (repo → Azure resource mapping)
#   - ADR-GSR-001 (goals SoR decision)
#   - .claude/rules/github-governance.md (GitHub Apps, PR discipline)
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/github/features-timeline
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/devops/plan/scaling-agile
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
schema: ssot.governance.operating-model.v1
last_updated: "2026-03-17"

organization: insightpulseai

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool Authority Matrix
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Each tool has exactly one primary role. No overlap in authority.

tool_authority:

azure_devops:
    role: selective-execution-surface
    owns:
      - id: azdo-goals
        domain: "Execution Coordination & GitHub Traceability"
        description: >
          Azure Boards is the execution coordination and GitHub traceability
          system of record for strategic objectives, scoped workstreams,
          concrete tasks, dependency links, cadence, team ownership, and
          implementation linkage across branches, pull requests, merge commits,
          builds, and work-item state transitions.
          In the Basic process: Epics = strategic objectives, Issues = scoped
          requirement/workstream items, Tasks = concrete execution items.
          Currently transitional — YAML in ssot/governance/ is canonical until
          5 graduation gates pass (per ADR-GSR-001). Once graduated, AzDo Boards
          becomes the authoritative write surface for objectives, OKRs, and
          work items. Detailed specs, architecture doctrine, SSOT files, and
          runtime evidence remain in the repo.
        status: transitional
        graduation_ref: "ADR-GSR-001 §Phase 2"
        planning_layer:
          delivery_plans: "Timeline coordination across Issues and iterations"
          area_paths: "Ownership/lane boundaries per workstream"
          iteration_paths: "Sprint/release cadence (14-day sprints)"
          dependency_links: "Sequencing and cross-workstream visibility"
          dashboards: "Leadership, program, and team reporting views"

      - id: azdo-pipelines
        domain: "Azure Deployments"
        description: >
          Azure Pipelines owns deployment to Azure resources (Container Apps,
          Functions, Databricks). Triggered by repo events (push, tag, PR merge).
          Service connections use workload identity federation (no long-lived secrets).
        status: active

across Issues and iterations" area_paths: "Ownership/lane boundaries per workstream" iteration_paths: "Sprint/release cadence (14-day sprints)" dependency_links: "Sequencing and cross-workstream visibility" dashboards: "Leadership, program, and team reporting views" - id: azdo-pipelines domain: "Azure Deployments" description: > Azure Pipelines owns deployment to Azure resources (Container Apps, Functions, Databricks). Triggered by repo events (push, tag, PR merge). Service connections use workload identity federation (no long-lived secrets). status: active

      - id: azdo-agent-pools
        domain: "Build Agents"
        description: >
          Self-hosted agent pool (ipai-build-pool) in rg-ipai-devops.
          Shared across ipai-platform and lakehouse projects.
        status: active

    does_not_own:
      - "Code reviews (GitHub PRs only)"
      - "CI gates (GitHub Actions only)"
      - "Issue triage (GitHub Issues only)"
      - "Secret scanning (GHAS)"

  github:
    role: primary-development-surface
    owns:
      - id: gh-code
        domain: "Source Code & PRs"
        description: >
          GitHub is the canonical source control and code review surface.
          All repos live in the Insightpulseai GitHub org. PRs are the
          only mechanism for code changes to reach main.
        status: active

      - id: gh-ci
        domain: "CI Gates & Validation"
        description: >
          GitHub Actions runs all CI validators (SSOT checks, linting,
          tests, spec validation). CI gates are required status checks
          on protected branches.
        status: active

      - id: gh-security
        domain: "Security Scanning"
        description: >
          GHAS (secret scanning, code scanning, dependency review) plus
          self-hosted defense-in-depth (GitLeaks, Semgrep, Trivy).
        status: active

      - id: gh-issues
        domain: "Issue Tracking & Triage"
        description: >
          GitHub Issues for bug reports, feature requests, and triage.
          Org-wide labels: priority:{critical,high,medium,low},
          type:{bug,feature,chore,docs}, agent:{claude,copilot,gemini}.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Team Topology
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/devops/plan/scaling-agile

team_topology:
  principle: "Scale with teams, not projects"
  preferred_shape: vertical-value-stream
  size_guidance: "6-9 people per team"
  current_model: "Solo operator — one person covers all area paths"
  scaling_rule: >
    When work exceeds capacity, add a team aligned to an area path
    rather than expanding an existing team. Each team owns a slice
    of the product from backlog to production.

      duration: "30 minutes"
    - name: "Retrospective"
      frequency: "Every 14 days (sprint end)"
      duration: "30 minutes"
    - name: "Coordination Meeting"
      frequency: "Weekly (mid-sprint)"
      duration: "15 minutes"
      purpose: "Cross-area risks, dependencies, blockers"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quality & Risk Communication Contract
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

quality_risk_contract:
  risk_triggers:
    - trigger: "Item blocked >3 days"
      action: "Escalate with Blocked By link + comment"
    - trigger: "Acceptance criteria missing"
      action: "Flag in Operational Dashboard"
    - trigger: "Cross-area dependency at risk"
      action: "Raise in coordination meeting"
    - trigger: "Sprint goal at risk (>30% incomplete at mid-sprint)"
      action: "Scope negotiation"
  definition_of_done:
    - "Acceptance criteria verified with evidence"
    - "Links to PRs, specs, or evidence docs present"
    - "No open Blocked By links"
    - "Dashboard widgets reflect current state"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub Execution Traceability Layer
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/github/features-timeline

github_execution_linkage:
  description: >
    Azure Boards is not only the place for objectives, workstreams, and tasks.
    It is also the GitHub-linked execution traceability surface for branches,
    pull requests, merge commits, builds, and work-item state transitions.
  capabilities:
    - "Create/link GitHub branches from work items"
    - "Create draft PRs from work items (GitHub Copilot integration)"
    - "Link PRs, merge commits, and builds back to work items"
    - "Deterministic work-item state transitions from GitHub PR keywords"
    - "YAML build status visible on linked work items"
    - "PR state and review visibility from Boards"
    - "Azure DevOps MCP capabilities for agent-assisted execution"
  traceability_rules:
    - "Every implementation PR must link back to its Azure Boards work item"
    - "Merge commits remain visible from the work item"
    - "Branch/PR/work-item associations are part of delivery evidence"
    - "State transitions use deterministic keywords (AB#ID, Fixes AB#ID)"
  mcp_execution:
    local_server: "Azure DevOps local MCP Server bridges AI assistants to AzDo resources"
    remote_server:
      status: roadmap
      note: "Hosted remote MCP Server in development — not yet canonical"
  roadmap_signals:
    status: "Direction signals only — not canonical until verified in tenant"
    items:
      - "Custom agents for the Coding Agent"
      - "Hosted remote MCP Server"
      - "Connected GitHub repositories limit increase (1,000 → 2,000)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Boundary Rules
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_rules:

  - id: BR-001
    rule: "No goal edits in GitHub Issues"
    description: >
      Strategic objectives, OKRs, and KPIs are managed in ssot/governance/
      YAML files (current) or AzDo Boards (target). GitHub Issues are for
      tactical bugs and feature requests, not goal tracking.

Coding Agent" - "Hosted remote MCP Server" - "Connected GitHub repositories limit increase (1,000 → 2,000)" # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Boundary Rules # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- boundary_rules: - id: BR-001 rule: "No goal edits in GitHub Issues" description: > Strategic objectives, OKRs, and KPIs are managed in ssot/governance/ YAML files (current) or AzDo Boards (target). GitHub Issues are for tactical bugs and feature requests, not goal tracking.

  - id: BR-002
    rule: "No code reviews in Azure DevOps"
    description: >
      All code changes flow through GitHub PRs. AzDo Repos are not used.
      AzDo Pipelines consume code from GitHub repos via service connections.

  - id: BR-003
    rule: "Pipelines trigger from repo events"
    description: >
      AzDo Pipelines trigger on GitHub push/tag/PR events via service
      connections. No manual pipeline runs in steady state. GitHub Actions
      handles CI; AzDo Pipelines handles Azure CD.

  - id: BR-004
    rule: "Secrets flow through Key Vault only"
    description: >
      AzDo variable groups reference Azure Key Vault (kv-ipai-dev).
      GitHub Actions uses GitHub secrets. Neither tool stores plaintext
      secrets in pipeline definitions or workflow files.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sync Contracts
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
sync_contracts:

  - id: SYNC-001
    name: "AzDo work items ↔ GitHub PRs"
    mechanism: "AB# convention in PR titles and commit messages"
    direction: bidirectional
    description: >
      AzDo work item IDs (AB#1234) in GitHub PR titles create automatic
      links. PR merge updates work item state. This is the primary
      traceability mechanism between goals and code.

pipeline definitions or workflow files. # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Sync Contracts # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- sync_contracts: - id: SYNC-001 name: "AzDo work items ↔ GitHub PRs" mechanism: "AB# convention in PR titles and commit messages" direction: bidirectional description: > AzDo work item IDs (AB#1234) in GitHub PR titles create automatic links. PR merge updates work item state. This is the primary traceability mechanism between goals and code.

  - id: SYNC-002
    name: "GitHub Actions → AzDo pipeline trigger"
    mechanism: "Webhook or service connection event"
    direction: github_to_azdo
    description: >
      Successful GitHub Actions CI gates can trigger AzDo Pipelines for
      Azure deployment. The CI-passes-then-CD-deploys flow ensures code
      quality before infrastructure changes.

  - id: SYNC-003
    name: "YAML goals → AzDo Boards (future)"
    mechanism: "Scripted sync (deferred)"
    direction: repo_to_azdo
    status: planned
    description: >
      Per ADR-GSR-001, once graduation gates pass, ssot/governance/ YAML
      goals will seed AzDo Boards. Until then, YAML is canonical.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/devops/devsecops/enable-devsecops-azure-github
#
# Maps Microsoft's DevSecOps reference model to the platform's tool authority.
# Does NOT change the Epic → Issue → Task hierarchy or tool ownership above.

devsecops_control_planes:

ADR-GSR-001, once graduation gates pass, ssot/governance/ YAML goals will seed AzDo Boards. Until then, YAML is canonical. # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Project Structure Reference # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # DevSecOps Control Planes # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/devops/devsecops/enable-devsecops-azure-github # # Maps Microsoft's DevSecOps reference model to the platform's tool authority. # Does NOT change the Epic → Issue → Task hierarchy or tool ownership above. devsecops_control_planes:

  planning_traceability:
    tools: ["Azure Boards", "GitHub"]
    description: >
      Azure Boards is the execution coordination and traceability system of
      record. GitHub is the code and pull-request truth. Every code change
      must trace to an Azure Boards work item via AB# convention.
    rules:
      - "Every implementation PR must reference an Azure Boards work item (AB#ID)"
      - "PRs, merge commits, and builds are part of the audit trail"
      - "Security findings must be triaged back to work items"
      - "Work-item state is not 'done' until security and runtime evidence exist"

  secure_build_release:
    tools: ["Azure Pipelines", "GitHub Actions"]
    description: >
      GitHub Actions handles CI gates and validation. Azure Pipelines handles
      Azure CD with artifact traceability (commit hash, AB# work-item ID
      embedded in container image metadata).
    rules:
      - "Build metadata (commit hash, work-item ID) embedded in container images"
      - "Pipeline definitions stored in repo (.azure/pipelines/), never portal-only"
      - "Security gates (SAST, dependency scan) are required pipeline stages"
      - "No manual portal deployments in steady state"

GitHub Actions handles CI gates and validation. Azure Pipelines handles Azure CD with artifact traceability (commit hash, AB# work-item ID embedded in container image metadata). rules: - "Build metadata (commit hash, work-item ID) embedded in container images" - "Pipeline definitions stored in repo (.azure/pipelines/), never portal-only" - "Security gates (SAST, dependency scan) are required pipeline stages" - "No manual portal deployments in steady state"

  identity_access:
    tools: ["Microsoft Entra ID", "Azure RBAC"]
    description: >
      Microsoft Entra ID is the identity plane. Azure RBAC governs resource
      access. Managed identities are preferred over service principals for
      service-to-service auth.
    rules:
      - "Prefer managed identities where supported"
      - "No long-lived secrets in pipeline definitions or workflow files"
      - "Workload identity federation (OIDC) for AzDo ↔ ARM service connections"
      - "Named accounts with MFA for all platform admins"

  secrets_authority:
    tools: ["Azure Key Vault"]
    description: >
      Azure Key Vault (kv-ipai-dev) is the canonical secret store. Secrets
      are never stored in code, pipeline definitions, or committed config files.
    rules:
      - "Runtime binding: managed identity → Key Vault → env vars"
      - "Zero secrets in git-tracked files"
      - "Secret rotation documented for Zoho SMTP, PG, and API keys"
      - "AzDo variable groups reference Key Vault, not inline secrets"

  runtime_quality_observability:
    tools: ["Azure Monitor", "Application Insights"]
    description: >
      Azure Monitor and Application Insights provide runtime quality signals,
      rollback triggers, and health evidence.
    rules:
      - "Application Insights enabled for all ACA workloads"
      - "Health/smoke checks defined for every production endpoint"
      - "Quality gates: alert → investigate → rollback decision"
      - "Deployment completion requires green health check evidence"

  container_runtime_security:
    tools: ["Defender for Containers", "Azure Policy"]
    description: >
      Defender for Containers and Azure Policy enforce container security
      posture and runtime behavior.
    rules:
      - "Defender for Cloud enabled for active container estate"
      - "Azure Policy enforces allowed container registries"
      - "Container vulnerability scanning on push to ACR"
      - "Runtime anomaly detection active for production workloads"

# ─── THREE-WAY BOUNDARY DOCTRINE ────────────────────────────────
#
# platform       = control plane / operator surfaces / shared APIs
# agent-platform = deployable agent runtime and orchestration plane
# agents         = personas / skills / judges / evals / schemas / manifests
#
# Decision rule:
#   If it runs as a deployable service → agent-platform
#   If it defines how an agent should think, act, evaluate, or route → agents
#   If it is shared control-plane or operator-facing product surface → platform

boundary_doctrine:
  platform:
    role: "control plane, operator surfaces, shared APIs, cross-plane contracts"
    decision_rule: "shared control-plane or operator-facing product surface"
  agent_platform:
    role: "deployable agent runtime and orchestration plane"
    decision_rule: "runs as a deployable service"
  agents:
    role: "agent definitions, skills, personas, judges, eval suites, routing maps, schemas"
    decision_rule: "defines how an agent should think, act, evaluate, or route"

completeness_gate:
  description: "A repo is 'done' only when it has all of these"
  required_surfaces:
    - "README.md"
    - ".github/workflows/"
    - "docs/"
    - "spec/"
    - "ssot/"
    - "scripts/"
    - "tests/"
    - "<repo-native domain directories>"
  required_answers:

    - "what it does not own"
    - "what files are authoritative"
    - "how changes are validated"
    - "how release evidence is produced"

# =============================================================================
# Planning System Index — InsightPulse AI
# =============================================================================
#
# Canonical index of all planning, strategy, OKR, KPI, and roadmap artifacts.
# Approach: A+C (index + cross-references, NOT content consolidation).
#
# Each planning layer has a distinct ownership scope and cadence.
# Files listed here are the SSOT for their respective layers.
#
# CI validator: scripts/ci/validate_planning_index.py
# CI gate: .github/workflows/planning-index-ssot-check.yml
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-18"
validator: scripts/ci/validate_planning_index.py
ci_gate: .github/workflows/planning-index-ssot-check.yml

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Planning Layer Model
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Strategy → Operational OKRs → Product Roadmap → Capability Targets
#   → Target State → Implementation Specs
#
# Each layer has:
#   - canonical_file: the SSOT file for that layer
#   - owner: who maintains it
#   - cadence: how often it changes
#   - join_keys: how it cross-references other layers

layers:

ci_gate: .github/workflows/planning-index-ssot-check.yml # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Planning Layer Model # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Strategy → Operational OKRs → Product Roadmap → Capability Targets # → Target State → Implementation Specs # # Each layer has: # - canonical_file: the SSOT file for that layer # - owner: who maintains it # - cadence: how often it changes # - join_keys: how it cross-references other layers layers:

  - id: strategy
    name: "Platform Strategy (VMOKRAPI-SPATRES)"
    canonical_file: ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml
    schema: ssot/governance/platform-strategy.schema.json
    validator: scripts/ci/validate_platform_strategy.py
    ci_gate: .github/workflows/platform-strategy-ssot-check.yml
    owner: platform-lead
    cadence: quarterly
    join_keys:
      - field: objectives[].id
        joins_to: operational_okrs.okrs[].objective_ref
      - field: kras[].id
        joins_to: strategy.objectives[].kra_ref
      - field: activities[].id
        joins_to: strategy.tasks[].activity_ref
    contains:
      - vision
      - mission
      - platform_doctrine
      - kras (6)
      - objectives (7 SMART)
      - okrs (7 with 22 key results)
      - kpis (13)
      - strategies (5)
      - programs (4 quarterly)
      - activities (8)
      - tasks (16)
      - resources
      - evidence_policy
      - risks (6)
      - spatres (7 dimensions)

join_keys: - field: objectives[].id joins_to: operational_okrs.okrs[].objective_ref - field: kras[].id joins_to: strategy.objectives[].kra_ref - field: activities[].id joins_to: strategy.tasks[].activity_ref contains: - vision - mission - platform_doctrine - kras (6) - objectives (7 SMART) - okrs (7 with 22 key results) - kpis (13) - strategies (5) - programs (4 quarterly) - activities (8) - tasks (16) - resources - evidence_policy - risks (6) - spatres (7 dimensions)

  - id: operational_okrs
    name: "Enterprise OKR Stack"
    canonical_file: ssot/governance/enterprise_okrs.yaml
    schema: ssot/governance/enterprise_okrs.schema.json
    validator: scripts/ci/validate_enterprise_okrs.py
    ci_gate: .github/workflows/enterprise-okrs-ssot-check.yml
    owner: engineering-lead
    cadence: quarterly
    join_keys:
      - field: strategic_objectives[].id
        joins_to: strategy.objectives[].id
      - field: canonical_okrs[].parent_objectives[]
        joins_to: operational_okrs.strategic_objectives[].id
      - field: "*.kpi_ref"
        joins_to: kpi_registry (platform/data/contracts/control_room_kpis.yaml)
    contains:
      - strategic_objectives (A-E)
      - canonical_okrs (O1-O3)
      - kpi_index

  - id: product_roadmap
    name: "Product Roadmap"
    canonical_file: infra/ssot/roadmap/product_roadmap.yaml
    owner: product-lead
    cadence: monthly
    status: on_main
    join_keys:
      - field: quarters[].epics[].id
        joins_to: azure_boards.epics (via title convention)
      - field: quarters[].epics[].ssot_ref
        joins_to: spec bundles or ssot files
    note: >
      Product roadmap with quarterly milestones and epic definitions.
      Epic ID format: E-<DOMAIN>-<SEQ>.

canonical_okrs[].parent_objectives[] joins_to: operational_okrs.strategic_objectives[].id - field: "*.kpi_ref" joins_to: kpi_registry (platform/data/contracts/control_room_kpis.yaml) contains: - strategic_objectives (A-E) - canonical_okrs (O1-O3) - kpi_index - id: product_roadmap name: "Product Roadmap" canonical_file: infra/ssot/roadmap/product_roadmap.yaml owner: product-lead cadence: monthly status: on_main join_keys: - field: quarters[].epics[].id joins_to: azure_boards.epics (via title convention) - field: quarters[].epics[].ssot_ref joins_to: spec bundles or ssot files note: > Product roadmap with quarterly milestones and epic definitions. Epic ID format: E-<DOMAIN>-<SEQ>.

  - id: capability_targets
    name: "Parity & Capability Targets"
    canonical_file: ssot/parity_targets.yaml
    owner: engineering-lead
    cadence: quarterly
    status: not_on_branch  # exists on ssot/parity branch (450a691590), not yet on main
    join_keys:
      - field: targets[].okr_ref
        joins_to: operational_okrs.canonical_okrs[].id
      - field: targets[].module_ref
        joins_to: addons/ipai/*/
    note: >
      This file exists on the ssot/parity branch but has not been merged to main.
      Cross-references to this layer are deferred until the file lands on main.

  - id: target_state
    name: "Platform Target State"
    canonical_file: infra/ssot/azure/PLATFORM_TARGET_STATE.md
    owner: platform-lead
    cadence: quarterly
    status: on_main
    join_keys:
      - field: objectives (prose)
        joins_to: strategy.objectives[].id
    note: >
      Markdown format. Contains the revised target-state statement with
      7 SMART objectives. Cross-referenced by platform-strategy-2026.yaml.

Contains the revised target-state statement with 7 SMART objectives. Cross-referenced by platform-strategy-2026.yaml.

  - id: implementation_specs
    name: "Implementation Spec Bundles"
    canonical_file: spec/*/
    owner: implementing-engineer
    cadence: per-feature
    join_keys:
      - field: spec/*/prd.md (objective references)
        joins_to: strategy.objectives[].id
      - field: spec/*/tasks.md (task references)
        joins_to: strategy.tasks[].id
    contains:
      - 76 spec bundles (constitution, prd, plan, tasks per feature)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Crosswalks (explicit join paths between layers)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
crosswalks:

  - from: strategy.objectives[].id
    to: operational_okrs.strategic_objectives[].id
    relationship: "strategy objectives map to operational strategic objectives"
    mapping_type: many_to_many
    note: >
      Strategy OBJ-001..007 are SMART quarterly objectives.
      Operational obj_A..E are thematic groupings.
      They complement each other, not 1:1.

  - from: operational_okrs.kpi_index
    to: platform/data/contracts/control_room_kpis.yaml
    relationship: "OKR key results reference operational KPIs"
    mapping_type: foreign_key

  - from: strategy.kpis[].id
    to: operational_okrs."*.kpi_ref"
    relationship: "strategy KPIs overlap with operational KPI references"
    mapping_type: shared_namespace
    note: >
      Both files reference kpi_001..kpi_017.
      The KPI definition SSOT is control_room_kpis.yaml.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Governance
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
governance:

  canonical_rules:
    - "Each layer has exactly one canonical file (no forks)"
    - "Cross-references use explicit join keys, not implicit naming"
    - "Content consolidation (merging files) requires ADR approval"
    - "Deprecated files must be listed in deprecations[] with replacement"

  deprecation_policy: >
    Files superseded by a canonical layer file must be:
    1. Marked deprecated with a pointer to the replacement
    2. Retained for 1 quarter after replacement is on main
    3. Deleted after the retention period

  deprecations: []
    # None currently — product_roadmap and parity_targets are not yet on main

# =============================================================================
# Platform Authority Split — SSOT
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines which platform owns which concern in the IPAI engineering system.
# Decision basis: ADR docs/architecture/adr/ADR_PLATFORM_TRANSFORMATION_DECISION.md
# Benchmark basis: Microsoft AI-led SDLC (2026-03), Data Intelligence E2E (2026-03)
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-30"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "approved"
decision: "option_c"

canonical_statement: >
  Azure Boards for portfolio/planning.
  GitHub for code, PR, CI, agentic SDLC.
  Azure DevOps Pipelines for service-connection-bound deploys (Odoo, Databricks, Infra).
  GitHub Actions for website/docs deploy (OIDC federation) and all CI.
  Azure runtime for all workloads (ACA, Foundry, Databricks, Key Vault, Entra).

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authority Boundaries
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
authority:
  code:
    owner: github
    rationale: Engineering surface, PR review, branch protection, CODEOWNERS

  ci_test_lint_scan:
    owner: github_actions
    rationale: Runs on PR, fast feedback loop, no service connection required

  website_deploy:
    owner: github_actions
    rationale: OIDC federation active (ipai-github-oidc), AzDO parallelism blocked
    surfaces: [ipai-landing, saas-landing, docs-pages]
    identity: ipai-github-oidc

  docs_deploy:
    owner: github_actions
    rationale: Native GitHub Pages deployment

  odoo_deploy:
    owner: azure_devops
    rationale: Service connection azure-ipai, ACA update, approval gates
    identity: ae2df138  # InsightPulseAI Azure DevOps Automation

Engineering surface, PR review, branch protection, CODEOWNERS ci_test_lint_scan: owner: github_actions rationale: Runs on PR, fast feedback loop, no service connection required website_deploy: owner: github_actions rationale: OIDC federation active (ipai-github-oidc), AzDO parallelism blocked surfaces: [ipai-landing, saas-landing, docs-pages] identity: ipai-github-oidc docs_deploy: owner: github_actions rationale: Native GitHub Pages deployment odoo_deploy: owner: azure_devops rationale: Service connection azure-ipai, ACA update, approval gates identity: ae2df138 # InsightPulseAI Azure DevOps Automation

  databricks_deploy:
    owner: azure_devops
    rationale: Bundle validate/promote, staging-to-prod approval gates
    identity: ae2df138

  infra_deploy:
    owner: azure_devops
    rationale: ARM service connection, Bicep what-if validation
    identity: ae2df138

  portfolio_planning:
    owner: azure_boards
    rationale: Epics, Initiatives, OKRs — not in GitHub
    project: ipai-platform
    organization: insightpulseai

  engineering_planning:
    owner: github_issues
    rationale: Feature, Bug, Task — engineering-level work items
    issue_types: [Feature, Bug, Task]
    do_not_add: [Initiative, Epic]

  identity:
    owner: entra_id
    rationale: SAML SSO for GitHub, managed identity for agents
    tenant: 402de71a

  secrets:
    owner: azure_key_vault
    rationale: Runtime binding via managed identity
    vaults: [kv-ipai-dev, kv-ipai-staging, kv-ipai-prod]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Linkage Rules
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
linkage:
  pr_tag_pattern: "AB#<work_item_id>"
  mandatory_paths:
    - "addons/"
    - "agents/"
    - "infra/"
  enforcement: ".github/workflows/planning-index-ssot-check.yml"

engineering_planning: owner: github_issues rationale: Feature, Bug, Task — engineering-level work items issue_types: [Feature, Bug, Task] do_not_add: [Initiative, Epic] identity: owner: entra_id rationale: SAML SSO for GitHub, managed identity for agents tenant: 402de71a secrets: owner: azure_key_vault rationale: Runtime binding via managed identity vaults: [kv-ipai-dev, kv-ipai-staging, kv-ipai-prod] # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Linkage Rules # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- linkage: pr_tag_pattern: "AB#<work_item_id>" mandatory_paths: - "addons/" - "agents/" - "infra/" enforcement: ".github/workflows/planning-index-ssot-check.yml"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# What This Decision Supersedes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
supersedes:
  - description: "CLAUDE.md deprecated table entry: GitHub Actions → Azure DevOps Pipelines (2026-03-21)"
    correction: >
      GitHub Actions remains CI + website/docs deploy authority.
      Azure DevOps Pipelines is deploy authority for Odoo, Databricks, and Infra only.
      The blanket deprecation was premature and contradicts Microsoft benchmarks.

# =============================================================================
# Unified Platform Capabilities Matrix — InsightPulse AI
# =============================================================================
#
# Single source of truth for ALL platform capabilities across every domain.
# Consolidates: EE parity, Odoo.sh equivalence, AI consolidation, OCA adoption,
# BIR compliance, infrastructure readiness, creative/genAI, and roadmap.
#
# Cross-references:
#   infra/ssot/parity/ee_to_oca_matrix.yaml (EE parity details)
#   ssot/governance/azure-odoosh-equivalent.yaml (Odoo.sh parity)
#   ssot/governance/ai-consolidation-foundry.yaml (AI consolidation)
#   ssot/governance/enterprise_okrs.yaml (OKR/KPI alignment)
#   ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml (VMOKRAPI strategy)
#   infra/ssot/roadmap/product_roadmap.yaml (quarterly roadmap)
#
# NOTE: Strategic KPIs in platform-strategy-2026.yaml use SKPI-* namespace
# to avoid collision with operational KPIs (kpi_001..kpi_017) in
# platform/data/contracts/control_room_kpis.yaml.
#
# Status values: active | partial | planned | blocked | deprecated | n/a
# Priority: P0 (production blocker) | P1 (operational maturity) | P2 (enhancement) | P3 (future)
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-15
# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================
# A. ERP — Odoo Enterprise Parity (CE + OCA + ipai_*)

# =============================================================================
# Platform Constitution — InsightPulse AI
# =============================================================================
#
# Non-negotiable platform rules. These are permanent unless amended by ADR.
# Consolidates rules currently scattered across CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/,
# and spec/constitution.md into a single machine-readable governance artifact.
#
# Scope: Platform-wide (all repos, all environments)
# Amendment: Requires an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) + PR approval
# Authority model: See ADR-GSR-001 (odoo/docs/architecture/GOALS_SYSTEM_OF_RECORD_DECISION.md)
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
schema: ssot.governance.platform-constitution.v1
last_updated: "2026-03-17"

project: InsightPulse AI Platform
status: ratified

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Non-Negotiable Rules
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# These rules are permanent. Violation requires an ADR to amend.

rules:

  - id: CONST-001
    name: "CE-only — no Enterprise modules"
    scope: odoo
    rule: >
      Odoo Community Edition only. No Enterprise modules, no odoo.com IAP
      dependencies, no Enterprise-gated features. Parity achieved via
      CE + OCA + ipai_* custom modules.
    source: CLAUDE.md

  - id: CONST-002
    name: "OCA-first module philosophy"
    scope: odoo
    rule: >
      Config → OCA → Delta. Prefer built-in Odoo configuration first,
      vetted OCA community modules second, custom ipai_* modules only
      for truly custom needs. Never modify OCA source — use _inherit overrides.
    source: CLAUDE.md

modules, no odoo.com IAP dependencies, no Enterprise-gated features. Parity achieved via CE + OCA + ipai_* custom modules. source: CLAUDE.md - id: CONST-002 name: "OCA-first module philosophy" scope: odoo rule: > Config → OCA → Delta. Prefer built-in Odoo configuration first, vetted OCA community modules second, custom ipai_* modules only for truly custom needs. Never modify OCA source — use _inherit overrides. source: CLAUDE.md

  - id: CONST-003
    name: "Secrets never in git"
    scope: platform
    rule: >
      Secrets must never appear in any file tracked by Git. Runtime secrets
      resolved via Azure Key Vault (kv-ipai-dev) with managed identity binding.
      Local dev uses .env files (gitignored). CI uses GitHub Actions secrets.
    source: .claude/rules/security-baseline.md

  - id: CONST-004
    name: "Azure-native deployment"
    scope: infrastructure
    rule: >
      Azure Container Apps (behind Azure Front Door) is the canonical
      deployment surface. DigitalOcean, Vercel, and self-hosted nginx
      are permanently deprecated. All new services deploy to ACA.
    deprecated_alternatives:
      - DigitalOcean (all) — deprecated 2026-03-15
      - Vercel deployment — deprecated 2026-03-11
      - Public nginx edge — deprecated 2026-03-15

  - id: CONST-005
    name: "Zoho SMTP for outbound email"
    scope: mail
    rule: >
      Odoo outbound mail routes through Zoho SMTP (smtp.zoho.com:587/TLS).
      Credentials via Azure Key Vault (zoho-smtp-user, zoho-smtp-password).
      Mailgun (mg.insightpulseai.com) is permanently deprecated.
    deprecated_alternatives:
      - Mailgun — deprecated 2026-03-11

  - id: CONST-006
    name: "Repo is SSOT"
    scope: platform
    rule: >
      The Git repository is the single source of truth. UI changes in
      external tools must be exported back: n8n → JSON, Figma → tokens,
      Cloudflare → DNS YAML. No console-only infrastructure changes.
    source: .claude/rules/ssot-platform.md

(smtp.zoho.com:587/TLS). Credentials via Azure Key Vault (zoho-smtp-user, zoho-smtp-password). Mailgun (mg.insightpulseai.com) is permanently deprecated. deprecated_alternatives: - Mailgun — deprecated 2026-03-11 - id: CONST-006 name: "Repo is SSOT" scope: platform rule: > The Git repository is the single source of truth. UI changes in external tools must be exported back: n8n → JSON, Figma → tokens, Cloudflare → DNS YAML. No console-only infrastructure changes. source: .claude/rules/ssot-platform.md

  - id: CONST-007
    name: "No console-only infrastructure changes"
    scope: infrastructure
    rule: >
      All infrastructure changes (Azure, Cloudflare, Vercel residue) must
      have a corresponding repo commit (IaC, YAML, or config). Dashboard-only
      changes are prohibited.
    source: .claude/rules/ssot-platform.md

  - id: CONST-008
    name: "Domain deprecations are permanent"
    scope: platform
    rule: >
      Deprecated domains, tools, and services are never reintroduced.
      insightpulseai.net → insightpulseai.com. Mattermost → Slack.
      Once deprecated, the item is permanently removed from active use.
    source: .claude/rules/ssot-platform.md

  - id: CONST-009
    name: "Schema changes only via migrations"
    scope: database
    rule: >
      No ad-hoc SQL, no Supabase dashboard schema edits. All schema changes
      go through migration files. Supabase ops.* tables are append-only.
    source: .claude/rules/ssot-platform.md

  - id: CONST-010
    name: "Cost-minimized self-hosted philosophy"
    scope: platform
    rule: >
      Build over buy. No expensive SaaS subscriptions, no per-seat enterprise
      licensing. Self-hosted stack: Odoo CE, Superset, Keycloak (transitional
      to Entra), n8n, PostgreSQL on Azure managed services.
    source: .claude/rules/platform-architecture.md

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
amendment:
  process: >
    To amend a constitutional rule: (1) Draft an ADR with rationale,
    (2) Create a PR modifying this file with the ADR reference,
    (3) Require platform-lead approval. No rule may be amended by
    convention or verbal agreement alone.
  adr_location: "docs/architecture/decisions/"
  approval_required: platform-lead

vision: >
  Build a self-hosted, governed, repo-first enterprise platform where Odoo is the ERP
  system of record, Azure Databricks is the data intelligence backbone, Azure AI Foundry
  is the agent control plane, and all execution is validated through machine-readable SSOT,
  CI gates, and evidence-backed operations.

mission: >
  Deliver an integrated operating platform for finance, operations, AI copilots, analytics,
  and compliance using deterministic ownership, measurable outcomes, minimal architecture
  drift, and automation-first delivery.

# =============================================================================
# Strategic Objectives (Revised Target State — 7 SMART Objectives)
# =============================================================================
objectives:
  - id: OBJ-001
    name: Identity baseline
    description: >
      Establish the canonical identity baseline across Microsoft 365, Entra ID,
      and Azure DevOps. Verify primary business domain, create emergency access
      accounts, move all platform admins to named accounts, enable MFA baseline,
      and eliminate day-to-day Global Admin usage.
    owner: identity-platform
    kra_ref:
      - KRA-GOV
      - KRA-ARCH
    target_date: 2026-04-14
    status: not_started
    success_metric: Identity bootstrap checklist fully green by end of Week 2

  - id: OBJ-002
    name: Azure DevOps operationalization
    description: >
      Make Azure DevOps the real control plane for delivery. Operationalize
      ipai-platform project, create dev/staging/prod environments, activate
      3 self-hosted agent pools and 3 ARM WIF service connections, and route
      100% of Azure deployments through YAML pipelines.
    owner: devops-platform
    kra_ref:
      - KRA-DELIVERY
      - KRA-GOV
    target_date: 2026-04-21
    status: not_started
    success_metric: First end-to-end deploy runs through Azure DevOps with no manual portal steps

  - id: OBJ-003
    name: Foundry production runtime hardening
    description: >
      Promote ipai-odoo-copilot-azure from working agent to governed production
      runtime. Complete repo-side runtime contract, activate dev deploy pipeline,
      enable Application Insights, validate Foundry role assignments, and clearly
      separate public advisory mode from internal operator mode.
    owner: ai-platform
    kra_ref:
      - KRA-AI
      - KRA-GOV
    target_date: 2026-04-28
    status: not_started
    success_metric: Production-readiness checklist for Foundry runtime reaches 100%

  - id: OBJ-004
    name: Public advisory assistant exposure
    description: >
      Expose the public advisory assistant safely through web. Implement
      marketing-site copilot shell, backend adapter path, enforce no
      browser-side privileged secrets, document optional Cloudflare Worker
      proxy, and enforce advisory-only policy.
    owner: web-platform
    kra_ref:
      - KRA-AI
      - KRA-ERP
    target_date: 2026-05-05
    status: not_started
    success_metric: Public assistant answers advisory questions in production-safe mode with zero secret leakage

      define Tableau Cloud publication contract.

    target_date: 2026-05-19

# =============================================================================
# KPIs
# =============================================================================
kpis:
  - id: SKPI-001
    name: Repo ownership codification coverage
    description: Percentage of active repos with codified boundaries
    formula: coded_repos / active_repos * 100
    target: 100
    unit: percent

  - id: SKPI-002
    name: Governance CI enforcement
    description: Whether governance CI checks run on all relevant PRs
    formula: ci_check_enabled
    target: true
    unit: boolean

  - id: SKPI-003
    name: Azure resource ownership mapping coverage
    description: Percentage of core Azure resources mapped to an owning repo/domain
    formula: mapped_resources / core_resources * 100
    target: 100
    unit: percent

  - id: SKPI-004
    name: Foundry agent repo mirror existence
    description: Whether the active agent has repo-backed manifest and prompt
    formula: repo_backed_agent_exists
    target: true
    unit: boolean

  - id: SKPI-005
    name: Evaluation artifact coverage
    description: Count of required evaluation categories scaffolded
    formula: count(eval_categories_present)
    target: 3
    unit: count

  - id: SKPI-006
    name: Odoo bridge Foundry alignment
    description: Whether Odoo bridge config validates correct Foundry endpoint taxonomy
    formula: bridge_alignment_pass
    target: true
    unit: boolean

# =============================================================================
# Strategies
# =============================================================================
strategies:
  - id: STR-001
    name: Identity-first foundation
    description: >
      Establish Microsoft Entra ID as the canonical identity plane before any runtime
      or delivery automation, ensuring RBAC, WIF, and MFA are prerequisites for all
      downstream capabilities.
    supports_objectives:
      - OBJ-001
      - OBJ-002

  - id: STR-002
    name: Azure DevOps as delivery control plane
    description: >
      Route all deployment, CI/CD, and release management through Azure DevOps with
      self-hosted agents, WIF service connections, and YAML pipelines. No ad hoc
      portal deployments.
    supports_objectives:
      - OBJ-002
      - OBJ-003
      - OBJ-007

  - id: STR-003
    name: Repo-backed Foundry lifecycle
    description: >
      Treat Azure AI Foundry portal state as managed runtime surface, but maintain
      runtime contracts, deploy pipelines, evaluation scaffolds, and mode separation
      in repos.
    supports_objectives:
      - OBJ-003
      - OBJ-004

and release management through Azure DevOps with self-hosted agents, WIF service connections, and YAML pipelines.   - id: STR-004
    name: Self-hosted core with managed exceptions
    description: >
      Self-host by default. Only Cloudflare (edge) and Tableau Cloud (BI consumption)
      are allowed managed exceptions. No DigitalOcean, no Vercel.
    supports_objectives:
      - OBJ-005
      - OBJ-006
      - OBJ-007

  - id: STR-005
    name: OLTP/OLAP separation
    description: >
      Separate transactional Odoo workloads from analytics via CDC, lakehouse medallion
      architecture, and governed Tableau publication contracts.
    supports_objectives:
      - OBJ-006

# Risks

risks:
  - id: RISK-001
    description: Identity drift — domain, accounts, or RBAC not locked before runtime expansion
    likelihood: high
    impact: high
    mitigation: Lock domain + named-account + MFA baseline first

  - id: RISK-002
    description: Over-broad RBAC assignments before proper review
    likelihood: high
    impact: high
    mitigation: Use RG-scoped roles and review before expansion
