  - id: RISK-003
    description: Foundry runtime drift from repo — portal state diverges from source-controlled contracts
    likelihood: medium
    impact: medium
    mitigation: Make runtime contract repo-backed and versioned

  - id: RISK-004
    description: Public assistant overexposure — privileged data or actions leak to advisory mode
    likelihood: high
    impact: high
    mitigation: Enforce advisory-only mode and backend secret boundary

  - id: RISK-005
    description: Automation sprawl — workflows drift across repos and tools
    likelihood: medium
    impact: medium
    mitigation: Inventory first, migrate second, delete last

  - id: RISK-006
    description: Analytics load on OLTP — BI queries degrade ERP performance
    likelihood: high
    impact: high
    mitigation: CDC separation before large-scale BI

# =============================================================================
# SPATRES Analysis
# =============================================================================
spatres:
  scope:
    - 10 active repositories with defined boundaries
    - Self-hosted core platform with Azure-native identity and control plane
    - Azure AI Foundry as production AI runtime
    - Azure DevOps as CI/CD and release control plane
    - Cloudflare edge services (allowed exception)
    - Tableau Cloud for BI consumption (allowed exception)
    - Odoo CE 19 as ERP system of record
  people:
    - Solo developer covering all roles at fractional capacity
    - Q1 focus on identity, DevOps, observability foundation
    - Q2 focus on AI runtime hardening and public exposure
    - Q3 focus on automation consolidation
    - Q4 focus on data maturity and analytics separation
  artifacts:
    - SSOT YAML files for ownership, inventories, and strategy
    - Repo-backed Foundry runtime contracts and evaluation scaffolds
    - Azure DevOps YAML pipelines for all deployments
    - Canonical reference stack documentation
    - Figma roadmap board for visual tracking
  timeline:
    - "Q1 2026: Identity baseline + DevOps operationalization + observability"
    - "Q2 2026: Foundry hardening + public advisory assistant"
    - "Q3 2026: Automation consolidation + Odoo boundary cleanup"
    - "Q4 2026: CDC implementation + lakehouse maturity + Tableau outputs"
  risks:
    - Identity drift if not locked before runtime expansion
    - Over-broad RBAC before proper review
    - Foundry runtime drift from repo-backed contracts
    - Public assistant overexposure of privileged data
    - Automation sprawl across repos and tools
    - Analytics load degrading OLTP performance
  environments:
    - Microsoft 365 + Entra ID (identity)
    - Azure DevOps (delivery control plane)
    - Azure (Foundry, ACA, Front Door, Key Vault, Monitor)
    - Cloudflare (DNS, WAF, CDN, optional Workers)
    - Self-hosted Odoo CE 19 + Postgres
    - Self-hosted lakehouse
    - Tableau Cloud (BI consumption)
  success:
    - Identity bootstrap checklist fully green
    - All deployments routed through Azure DevOps
    - Foundry runtime production-readiness at 100%
    - Public advisory assistant live with zero secret leakage
    - automations is the single canonical workflow repo
    - First governed Odoo-to-Tableau analytics path testable
    - All production workloads have telemetry and alerting

# =============================================================================
# Portfolio Migration Map — Work Item Authority & Linkage
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines where each work item type lives and how cross-system linkage works.
# Decision: Option C (Azure Boards = portfolio, GitHub Issues = engineering)
# Parent: ssot/governance/platform-authority-split.yaml
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
# =============================================================================

version: 1
last_updated: "2026-03-30"
owner: "platform-architecture"
status: "approved"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Work Item Type → System of Record
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
work_item_types:

  # Portfolio-level: Azure Boards only
  initiative:
    system: azure_boards
    project: ipai-platform
    organization: insightpulseai
    rationale: >
      Initiatives span multiple repos and domains. They require portfolio-level
      reporting, OKR alignment, and cross-team visibility that GitHub Issues
      cannot provide.
    fields:
      - title
      - description
      - state           # New, Active, Resolved, Closed
      - priority        # 1-4
      - area_path       # Maps to domain (ERP, Agents, Data, etc.)
      - iteration_path  # Sprint/quarter
      - tags
      - assigned_to
      - target_date
    do_not_create_in: github_issues

  epic:
    system: azure_boards
    project: ipai-platform
    organization: insightpulseai
    rationale: >
      Epics group related features across repos. They are the primary
      planning unit for roadmap visibility and capacity planning.
    fields:
      - title
      - description
      - state
      - priority
      - area_path
      - iteration_path
      - tags
      - assigned_to
      - target_date
      - business_value   # 0-100 score
      - effort           # Story points (aggregate)
    do_not_create_in: github_issues

assigned_to - target_date do_not_create_in: github_issues epic: system: azure_boards project: ipai-platform organization: insightpulseai rationale: > Epics group related features across repos. They are the primary planning unit for roadmap visibility and capacity planning. fields: - title - description - state - priority - area_path - iteration_path - tags - assigned_to - target_date - business_value # 0-100 score - effort # Story points (aggregate) do_not_create_in: github_issues

  # Engineering-level: GitHub Issues (per-repo)
  feature:
    system: github_issues
    scope: per_repo
    rationale: >
      Features are scoped to a single repo. They are the primary unit of
      engineering work, tracked alongside PRs and code review.
    fields:
      - title
      - body             # Markdown description
      - labels           # Type, priority, domain
      - assignees
      - milestone        # Maps to iteration/sprint
      - linked_pr        # Auto-linked via branch naming
    link_to_parent:
      system: azure_boards
      type: epic
      format: "AB#<work_item_id>"
      location: issue_body_footer

  bug:
    system: github_issues
    scope: per_repo
    rationale: >
      Bugs are discovered and fixed in code. GitHub Issues provides
      direct linkage to PRs, commits, and CI results.
    fields:
      - title
      - body
      - labels
      - assignees
      - milestone
      - linked_pr
    link_to_parent:
      system: azure_boards
      type: epic
      format: "AB#<work_item_id>"
      location: issue_body_footer

  task:
    system: github_issues
    scope: per_repo
    rationale: >
      Tasks are atomic engineering work items. They live where the code lives.
    fields:
      - title
      - body
      - labels
      - assignees
      - milestone
      - linked_pr
    link_to_parent:
      system: azure_boards
      type: epic
      format: "AB#<work_item_id>"
      location: issue_body_footer

They live where the code lives. fields: - title - body - labels - assignees - milestone - linked_pr link_to_parent: system: azure_boards type: epic format: "AB#<work_item_id>" location: issue_body_footer

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cross-System Linkage
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
linkage:

  # AB# tag format: used in GitHub PR titles, commit messages, and issue bodies
  # to create bidirectional links between GitHub and Azure Boards.
  ab_tag:
    format: "AB#<work_item_id>"
    examples:
      - "AB#1234"
      - "feat(erp): add invoice PDF export AB#1234"
      - "Closes #42. Parent epic: AB#1234"
    usage:
      pr_title: optional       # Include when PR implements a tracked epic
      commit_message: optional # Include for traceability
      issue_body: recommended  # Footer line linking to parent epic

  # GitHub → Azure Boards: automatic via Azure Boards GitHub integration
  github_to_boards:
    mechanism: "Azure Boards GitHub App"
    triggers:
      - "AB# mention in PR title or description"
      - "AB# mention in commit message"
    effect: "Creates link on Azure Boards work item → GitHub PR/commit"

  # Azure Boards → GitHub: manual link or via API
  boards_to_github:
    mechanism: "Development link on work item"
    format: "https://github.com/Insightpulseai/<repo>/issues/<number>"

parent epic # GitHub → Azure Boards: automatic via Azure Boards GitHub integration github_to_boards: mechanism: "Azure Boards GitHub App" triggers: - "AB# mention in PR title or description" - "AB# mention in commit message" effect: "Creates link on Azure Boards work item → GitHub PR/commit" # Azure Boards → GitHub: manual link or via API boards_to_github: mechanism: "Development link on work item" format: "https://github.com/Insightpulseai/<repo>/issues/<number>"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Area Path → Repo Mapping (for routing)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# When an Azure Boards work item is decomposed into GitHub Issues,
# the area path determines which repo receives the issue.
area_path_to_repo:
  "ipai-platform\\ERP": odoo
  "ipai-platform\\Agents": agents
  "ipai-platform\\Agents\\Runtime": agent-platform
  "ipai-platform\\Data": data-intelligence
  "ipai-platform\\Infra": infra
  "ipai-platform\\Design": design
  "ipai-platform\\Platform": platform
  "ipai-platform\\Web": web
  "ipai-platform\\Docs": docs
  "ipai-platform\\Mobile": mobile
  "ipai-platform\\MCP": mcp

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Migration Rules (for existing items)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
migration:

Path → Repo Mapping (for routing) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # When an Azure Boards work item is decomposed into GitHub Issues, # the area path determines which repo receives the issue. area_path_to_repo: "ipai-platform\\ERP": odoo "ipai-platform\\Agents": agents "ipai-platform\\Agents\\Runtime": agent-platform "ipai-platform\\Data": data-intelligence "ipai-platform\\Infra": infra "ipai-platform\\Design": design "ipai-platform\\Platform": platform "ipai-platform\\Web": web "ipai-platform\\Docs": docs "ipai-platform\\Mobile": mobile "ipai-platform\\MCP": mcp # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Migration Rules (for existing items) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- migration:

  # Items to migrate from GitHub Issues → Azure Boards
  promote_to_boards:
    criteria:
      - "GitHub Issue labeled 'epic' or 'initiative'"
      - "GitHub Issue that spans multiple repos"
      - "GitHub Issue with no code changes (pure planning)"
    fields_to_preserve:
      - title
      - description        # → Azure Boards description (HTML)
      - labels             # → Azure Boards tags
      - assignees          # → Azure Boards assigned_to
      - milestone          # → Azure Boards iteration_path
      - linked_prs         # → Azure Boards development links
      - comments           # → Azure Boards discussion (best-effort)
    post_migration:
      - "Close GitHub Issue with comment: 'Migrated to Azure Boards AB#<id>'"
      - "Add AB# reference to any linked PRs"

  # Items to keep in GitHub Issues (no migration needed)
  keep_in_github:
    criteria:
      - "Feature, Bug, or Task scoped to a single repo"
      - "Has linked PRs or commits"
      - "Active engineering work"
    action: "Add AB# link to parent epic in issue body if one exists"

- "Close GitHub Issue with comment: 'Migrated to Azure Boards AB#<id>'" - "Add AB# reference to any linked PRs" # Items to keep in GitHub Issues (no migration needed) keep_in_github: criteria: - "Feature, Bug, or Task scoped to a single repo" - "Has linked PRs or commits" - "Active engineering work" action: "Add AB# link to parent epic in issue body if one exists"

  # Bulk migration is NOT recommended. Migrate incrementally:
  # 1. New initiatives/epics → create in Azure Boards from day one
  # 2. Existing epics in GitHub → migrate when next touched
  # 3. Features/bugs/tasks → stay in GitHub, add AB# links
  strategy: incremental
  batch_migration: false
  reason: >
    Bulk migration risks broken links and context loss.
    Incremental migration ensures each item is validated on touch.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Label Conventions (GitHub Issues)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
labels:
  type:
    - "feature"
    - "bug"
    - "task"
    - "chore"
    - "docs"
  priority:
    - "P0-critical"
    - "P1-high"
    - "P2-medium"
    - "P3-low"
  domain:
    - "domain:erp"
    - "domain:agents"
    - "domain:data"
    - "domain:infra"
    - "domain:web"
    - "domain:platform"
    - "domain:design"
    - "domain:mobile"
    - "domain:mcp"
    - "domain:docs"
  status:
    - "blocked"
    - "needs-review"
    - "ready"

project:
  name: InsightPulse AI Platform
  id: ipai-platform
  status: active
  inception: "2025-10-01"
  planning_horizon: "2026-03-01 to 2026-12-31"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vision & Mission (reference only — SSOT is platform-strategy-2026.yaml)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
vision_ref: ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml#vision
mission_ref: ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml#mission

vision_summary: >
  Self-hosted, governed, repo-first enterprise platform: Odoo as ERP SoR,
  Databricks as data intelligence, AI Foundry as agent control plane,
  all validated through SSOT, CI gates, and evidence-backed operations.

mission_summary: >
  Integrated operating platform for finance, operations, AI copilots,
  analytics, and compliance using deterministic ownership, measurable
  outcomes, minimal architecture drift, and automation-first delivery.

— SSOT is platform-strategy-2026.yaml) # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- vision_ref: ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml#vision mission_ref: ssot/governance/platform-strategy-2026.yaml#mission vision_summary: > Self-hosted, governed, repo-first enterprise platform: Odoo as ERP SoR, Databricks as data intelligence, AI Foundry as agent control plane, all validated through SSOT, CI gates, and evidence-backed operations. mission_summary: > Integrated operating platform for finance, operations, AI copilots, analytics, and compliance using deterministic ownership, measurable outcomes, minimal architecture drift, and automation-first delivery.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Scope
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
scope:
  in_scope:
    - "Odoo CE 19 ERP (finance, HR, CRM, project, inventory)"
    - "Azure hosting (Container Apps, Front Door, managed PG, Key Vault)"
    - "AI agent framework (Azure AI Foundry, Claude, OpenAI)"
    - "Data analytics (Databricks, Superset, medallion architecture)"
    - "Automation platform (n8n, GitHub Actions, AzDo Pipelines)"
    - "SSO/Identity (Keycloak transitional → Entra ID)"
    - "BIR tax compliance (Philippine regulatory)"
    - "OCA community module curation and governance"

  out_of_scope:
    - "Consumer-facing mobile applications"
    - "Hardware / IoT device management"
    - "Third-party SaaS marketplace"
    - "Odoo Enterprise modules or odoo.com IAP"
    - "Multi-tenant customer hosting (single-tenant only)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stakeholder Roles
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
stakeholders:
  - role: platform-lead
    responsibility: "Architecture decisions, constitution amendments, strategy"
    authority: "Approves ADRs, infrastructure changes, new service additions"

  - role: product-lead

    authority: "Approves spec bundles, capability target adjustments"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
success_criteria:
  - id: SC-001
    description: "All 7 SMART objectives (OBJ-001..007) tracked with measurable KRs"
    objective_ref: "platform-strategy-2026.yaml#objectives"
    measurement: "Quarterly OKR review via enterprise_okrs.yaml"

  - id: SC-002
    description: "EE parity ≥80% via CE + OCA + ipai_*"
    current_baseline: "~35-45% (audited 2026-03-08)"
    target: "≥80% by 2026-12-31"

  - id: SC-003
    description: "All canonical SSOT files CI-validated"
    current_state: "3/3 governance layers have CI gates (PR #606)"
    target: "100% coverage, zero ungated SSOT changes"

  - id: SC-004
    description: "Azure infrastructure fully IaC-managed"
    target: "Zero console-only changes, all resources in resources.yaml"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constraints (ref: platform-constitution.yaml)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
constraints:
  - "CE-only: No Odoo Enterprise modules (CONST-001)"
  - "OCA-first: Config → OCA → Delta (CONST-002)"
  - "Cost-minimized: Self-hosted, no per-seat licensing (CONST-010)"
  - "Azure-native: All deployment on Azure Container Apps (CONST-004)"
  - "Secrets via Key Vault only (CONST-003)"
  - "Repo is SSOT — no console-only changes (CONST-006, CONST-007)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumptions
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
assumptions:
  - "GitHub Enterprise plan remains active (50k Actions minutes/mo)"
  - "Azure subscription budget supports Southeast Asia region resources"
  - "Single developer/operator model (solo seat, all roles)"
  - "OCA 19.0 branches available for must-have modules by Q2 2026"

No two repos deploy the same container or manage the same Azure resource."

  - id: iac-ownership
    rule: "Only the infra repo provisions shared infrastructure (Front Door, Key Vaults, VNets, NSGs, managed identities). Application repos consume these resources but never define them."

  - id: identity-isolation
    rule: "Each managed identity is bound to exactly one repo. Cross-repo identity sharing is prohibited. Cross-identity access uses federated credentials or RBAC grants defined in infra."

  - id: container-registry
    rule: "ipaiodoodevacr is for Odoo images only (odoo repo). ipaiwebacr is for all other platform images (infra repo, consumed by multiple repos)."

  - id: no-digitalocean
    rule: "All DigitalOcean references are deprecated. Azure Container Apps is the canonical compute surface."

  - id: key-vault-access
    rule: "Key Vault access policies are defined in infra repo IaC. No ad-hoc portal grants. Each managed identity gets least-privilege access."

        Remove references to db/schema/, db/migrations/, db/seeds/, db/rls/,
        docs/data-model/, scripts/generate_odoo_dbml.py from documentation
        files, or create the directories and populate them.
      effort: small

  p1_high:

        Move archive/root/scripts/generate_odoo_dbml.py to
        scripts/odoo/generate_odoo_dbml.py. Create CI workflow that

    - id: schema_drift_ci_gate

    - id: codeowners_schema_paths

        ops-platform/supabase/migrations/, supabase/db/schema/.
      effort: small

# =============================================================================
# Entra vs Azure Control Plane Boundary — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Defines what belongs in the Entra identity plane vs the Azure resource plane.
# This is an enforcement boundary, not a suggestion.
#
# Rule: Entra roles control Entra resources. Azure roles control Azure resources.
# These are separate control planes.
#
# Refs:
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/fundamentals/introduction-identity-access-management
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/role-based-access-control/custom-overview
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-26
# =============================================================================

schema_version: 1
kind: identity_boundary_contract
last_reviewed: "2026-03-26"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ENTRA-ONLY — identity authority surface
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# These objects exist only in Entra. They are not Azure resources.

entra_only:
  directory_objects:
    - users
    - groups
    - administrative_units
    - verified_domains
    - licenses

  access_policy:
    - conditional_access_policies
    - mfa_sign_in_methods
    - break_glass_accounts
    - entra_rbac_role_assignments

  application_identity:
    - app_registrations
    - enterprise_apps_service_principals
    - federated_identity_credentials

  ssot_files:
    - ssot/identity/entra_target_state.yaml
    - ssot/identity/entra_app_registrations.yaml
    - ssot/identity/entra_rbac_roles.yaml
    - ssot/identity/entra_branding.yaml
    - ssot/entra/app_registrations.azure_native.yaml
    - ssot/entra/app_registrations.excluded.yaml

"2026-03-26" # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # ENTRA-ONLY — identity authority surface # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # These objects exist only in Entra. They are not Azure resources. entra_only: directory_objects: - users - groups - administrative_units - verified_domains - licenses access_policy: - conditional_access_policies - mfa_sign_in_methods - break_glass_accounts - entra_rbac_role_assignments application_identity: - app_registrations - enterprise_apps_service_principals - federated_identity_credentials ssot_files: - ssot/identity/entra_target_state.yaml - ssot/identity/entra_app_registrations.yaml - ssot/identity/entra_rbac_roles.yaml - ssot/identity/entra_branding.yaml - ssot/entra/app_registrations.azure_native.yaml - ssot/entra/app_registrations.excluded.yaml

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# AZURE-ONLY — resource plane
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# These are Azure resources, even if they authenticate through Entra.

azure_only:
  workload:
    - container_apps
    - container_registries
    - app_service
    - function_apps

  data:
    - postgresql_flexible_server
    - storage_accounts
    - adls_gen2
    - databricks_workspaces
    - databricks_access_connectors

  ai:
    - azure_openai
    - ai_foundry
    - document_intelligence
    - computer_vision
    - language
    - ai_search

  networking:
    - vnets
    - nsgs
    - front_door
    - dns_zones
    - private_endpoints

  security:
    - key_vault
    - defender_for_cloud

  observability:
    - azure_monitor
    - log_analytics
    - application_insights

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CROSS-PLANE — managed identities
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Identity authority in Entra, lifecycle/attachment in Azure.

data: - postgresql_flexible_server - storage_accounts - adls_gen2 - databricks_workspaces - databricks_access_connectors ai: - azure_openai - ai_foundry - document_intelligence - computer_vision - language - ai_search networking: - vnets - nsgs - front_door - dns_zones - private_endpoints security: - key_vault - defender_for_cloud observability: - azure_monitor - log_analytics - application_insights # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # CROSS-PLANE — managed identities # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Identity authority in Entra, lifecycle/attachment in Azure.

cross_plane:
  managed_identities:
    rule: >
      Identity governance and trust model lives in Entra.
      Resource object lifecycle lives in Azure where it is attached.
      System-assigned MIs are tied to the parent Azure resource.
      User-assigned MIs are standalone Azure resources with Entra identity.

observability: - azure_monitor - log_analytics - application_insights # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # CROSS-PLANE — managed identities # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Identity authority in Entra, lifecycle/attachment in Azure. cross_plane: managed_identities: rule: > Identity governance and trust model lives in Entra. Resource object lifecycle lives in Azure where it is attached. System-assigned MIs are tied to the parent Azure resource. User-assigned MIs are standalone Azure resources with Entra identity.

    inventory:
      # From Azure resource inventory — the 13 managed identity resources
      - name: dbmanagedidentity
        type: system_assigned
        notes: "Databricks workspace system identity"
      - name: id-ipai-aca-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: container_apps
      - name: id-ipai-agents-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: agent_workloads
      - name: id-ipai-databricks-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: databricks
      - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: lakehouse_dev
      - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-prod
        type: user_assigned
        scope: lakehouse_prod
      - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-staging
        type: user_assigned
        scope: lakehouse_staging
      - name: mi-ipai-odoo-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: odoo_dev
      - name: mi-ipai-odoo-prod
        type: user_assigned
        scope: odoo_prod
      - name: mi-ipai-odoo-staging
        type: user_assigned
        scope: odoo_staging
      - name: mi-ipai-platform-dev
        type: user_assigned
        scope: platform_dev
      - name: mi-ipai-platform-prod
        type: user_assigned
        scope: platform_prod
      - name: mi-ipai-platform-staging
        type: user_assigned
        scope: platform_staging

databricks - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-dev type: user_assigned scope: lakehouse_dev - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-prod type: user_assigned scope: lakehouse_prod - name: mi-ipai-lakehouse-staging type: user_assigned scope: lakehouse_staging - name: mi-ipai-odoo-dev type: user_assigned scope: odoo_dev - name: mi-ipai-odoo-prod type: user_assigned scope: odoo_prod - name: mi-ipai-odoo-staging type: user_assigned scope: odoo_staging - name: mi-ipai-platform-dev type: user_assigned scope: platform_dev - name: mi-ipai-platform-prod type: user_assigned scope: platform_prod - name: mi-ipai-platform-staging type: user_assigned scope: platform_staging

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ENFORCEMENT RULES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

enforcement:
  - "Entra roles control Entra resources — never use Entra RBAC for Azure resources"
  - "Azure roles control Azure resources — never use Azure RBAC for Entra directory objects"
  - "Managed identity governance lives in Entra; resource attachment lives in Azure"
  - "Databricks access connectors are Azure resources, not Entra objects"
  - "Key Vault is an Azure resource — secrets access is via Azure RBAC, not Entra RBAC"
  - "App registrations are Entra objects — track in ssot/identity/ and ssot/entra/"
  - "No Supabase identity surface — all identity is Azure-native (Entra + managed identity)"

# Entra ID Company Branding — IPAI Platform
# SSOT for sign-in page customization across all Entra-authenticated apps
# Spec: spec/entra-identity-migration/
# Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/how-to-customize-branding
# Created: 2026-03-22

# Prerequisites:
#   - Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 license
#   - Organizational Branding Administrator role
#   - Admin center: https://entra.microsoft.com → Entra ID → Custom Branding

branding:

  # ─── Basics ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  basics:
    favicon:
      description: Browser tab icon
      source: design/assets/favicon-32x32.png
      requirements:
        size: 32x32 px
        format: PNG (preferred) or JPG
        max_file_size: 5 KB

    background_image:
      description: Full sign-in page background
      source: design/assets/entra-login-bg-1920x1080.png
      requirements:
        size: 1920x1080 px
        format: PNG (preferred) or JPG
        max_file_size: 300 KB
      notes: >
        Sign-in box partially obscures center of image.
        Place key branding elements in corners or edges.
        Use partial-screen layout template to preserve visibility.

    page_background_color:
      description: Fallback color when background image fails to load
      value: "#1a1a2e"  # IPAI dark navy — matches brand palette
      notes: Shown on slow connections before image loads

text:
# Entra ID RBAC Role Definitions — IPAI Platform
# SSOT for security group → app role mappings across all platform apps
# Spec: spec/entra-identity-migration/
# Created: 2026-03-22

roles:

  # ─── Role 1: Platform Administrator ────────────────────────────────
  - id: platform-admin
    entra_group: SG-IPAI-Admin
    description: Full platform administration across all apps
    scope: all-apps
    mfa_required: true
    conditional_access: CA-001, CA-004
    app_mappings:
      odoo:
        groups:
          - base.group_system
          - base.group_erp_manager
        description: Full Odoo system admin
      plane:
        role: admin
        description: Workspace admin with God Mode access
      superset:
        role: Admin
        description: Full Superset admin (datasources, dashboards, users)
      n8n:
        role: owner
        description: Workflow owner, credential management
      shelf:
        role: admin
        description: Full Shelf admin
      crm:
        role: admin
        description: Full CRM admin

  # ─── Role 2: Finance Manager ───────────────────────────────────────
  - id: finance-manager
    entra_group: SG-IPAI-Finance-Manager
    description: Finance operations lead — accounting, reporting, close, compliance oversight
    scope: finance-apps
    mfa_required: true
    conditional_access: CA-002
    app_mappings:
      odoo:
        groups:
          - account.group_account_manager
          - project.group_project_manager
          - ipai_finance_ppm.group_ipai_ppm_manager
        description: Full accounting + project management for finance workflows
      plane:
        role: member
        projects: [COMPLY, ERP, DATA]
        description: Finance compliance and ERP project access
      superset:
        role: Alpha
        description: Create/edit dashboards, access all finance datasources
      n8n:
        role: editor
        description: Edit finance automation workflows

metadata.title: Entra ID RBAC Role Definitions — IPAI Platform

operations lead — accounting, reporting, close, compliance oversight scope: finance-apps mfa_required: true conditional_access: CA-002 app_mappings: odoo: groups: - account.group_account_manager - project.group_project_manager - ipai_finance_ppm.group_ipai_ppm_manager description: Full accounting + project management for finance workflows plane: role: member projects: [COMPLY, ERP, DATA] description: Finance compliance and ERP project access superset: role: Alpha description: Create/edit dashboards, access all finance datasources n8n: role: editor description: Edit finance automation workflows

  # ─── Role 3: Finance User ─────────────────────────────────────────
  - id: finance-user
    entra_group: SG-IPAI-Finance-User
    description: Finance team member — invoicing, expense, day-to-day accounting
    scope: finance-apps
    mfa_required: false
    conditional_access: null
    app_mappings:
      odoo:
        groups:
          - account.group_account_invoice
          - project.group_project_user
        description: Invoice creation, expense submission, task execution
      plane:
        role: member
        projects: [COMPLY]
        description: View compliance tasks and wiki
      superset:
        role: Gamma
        description: View-only dashboards

  # ─── Role 4: Project Manager ───────────────────────────────────────
  - id: project-manager
    entra_group: SG-IPAI-Project-Manager
    description: Project and delivery management — milestones, resources, profitability
    scope: project-apps
    mfa_required: false
    conditional_access: null
    app_mappings:
      odoo:
        groups:
          - project.group_project_manager
          - hr_timesheet.group_hr_timesheet_approver
        description: Project management, timesheet approval, resource allocation
      plane:
        role: member
        projects: [ARCH, SPEC, INFRA, ERP, AI, DATA]
        description: Full project member across delivery projects
      superset:
        role: Alpha
        description: Create/edit project dashboards

View tax/compliance dashboards # ─── Role 6: Project User ───────────────────────────────────────── - id: project-user entra_group: SG-IPAI-Project-User description: General project contributor — task execution, time tracking scope: project-apps mfa_required: false conditional_access: null app_mappings: odoo: groups: - project.group_project_user - hr_timesheet.group_hr_timesheet_user description: Task execution, timesheet entry plane: role: member projects: [] # assigned per-project as needed description: Basic Plane member, project-specific access superset: role: Gamma description: View-only dashboards

  # ─── Role 7: Agent Workload (Non-Human) ────────────────────────────
  - id: agent-workload
    entra_group: null  # managed identity, not security group
    description: AI agent runtime identity — managed identity on ACA, not a human user
    scope: agent-runtime
    mfa_required: false  # managed identities are not subject to MFA
    conditional_access: null  # governed via RBAC + Key Vault + monitoring
    app_mappings:
      odoo:
        groups: []
        description: API access via ipai_odoo_copilot bridge (not a login user)
      plane:
        role: null
        description: API access via Plane API key (not a login user)
      superset:
        role: null
        description: No direct Superset access (reads via Databricks/PG)
    governance:
      auth_method: Azure Managed Identity (system-assigned)
      key_vault: kv-ipai-dev
      monitoring: Azure Monitor + Entra sign-in logs
      future: Entra Agent ID (when GA/Frontier access confirmed)

text:
# =============================================================================
# Microsoft Entra ID Target State — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Last updated: 2026-03-23
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-23"
owner: "platform-architecture"

tenant:
  name: Default Directory
  default_domain: ceoinsightpulseai.onmicrosoft.com
  verified_custom_domain: insightpulseai.com
  additional_subdomains: 0
  subdomain_policy: >
    Do not add Entra subdomains unless a distinct UPN/identity
    namespace is truly required. Root domain auto-verifies children.

licensing:
  current: entra_id_free
  minimum_target: entra_id_p1
  preferred_target: entra_id_p2
  p1_unlocks:
    - conditional_access
    - group_based_app_assignment
    - self_service_password_reset
    - dynamic_groups
  p2_unlocks:
    - identity_protection
    - risk_based_conditional_access
    - privileged_identity_management
    - access_reviews

authentication:
  current_baseline: security_defaults
  target_baseline: conditional_access_after_p1
  legacy_mfa_sspr_policy: must_migrate_to_modern
  auth_methods_enabled:
    - passkey_fido2
    - microsoft_authenticator
    - temporary_access_pass
    - software_oath
    - email_otp
  auth_methods_deferred:
    - sms
    - voice
    - certificate_based
    - qr_code
  conditional_access_policies_after_p1:
    - name: require_mfa_for_admins
      scope: global_admins + privileged_roles
    - name: require_strong_auth_all_users
      scope: all_users
    - name: block_legacy_auth
      scope: all_users
    - name: secure_mfa_registration
      scope: all_users_excluding_breakglass

users:
  current_count: 4
  target_count: 4-8
  emergency_access_accounts:
    required: 2
    excluded_from: conditional_access_lockout
    naming: breakglass-1@insightpulseai.com, breakglass-2@insightpulseai.com

access_reviews authentication: current_baseline: security_defaults target_baseline: conditional_access_after_p1 legacy_mfa_sspr_policy: must_migrate_to_modern auth_methods_enabled: - passkey_fido2 - microsoft_authenticator - temporary_access_pass - software_oath - email_otp auth_methods_deferred: - sms - voice - certificate_based - qr_code conditional_access_policies_after_p1: - name: require_mfa_for_admins scope: global_admins + privileged_roles - name: require_strong_auth_all_users scope: all_users - name: block_legacy_auth scope: all_users - name: secure_mfa_registration scope: all_users_excluding_breakglass users: current_count: 4 target_count: 4-8 emergency_access_accounts: required: 2 excluded_from: conditional_access_lockout naming: breakglass-1@insightpulseai.com, breakglass-2@insightpulseai.com

groups:
  current_count: 12
  target_count: 8-15
  recommended:
    - grp-global-admins
    - grp-platform-admins
    - grp-developers
    - grp-finance-tax
    - grp-data-intelligence
    - grp-odoo-users
    - grp-diva-approvers
    - grp-breakglass-exclusions
  policy: >
    All app/role assignment must be group-based.
    No direct user-to-app assignment unless unavoidable.

enterprise_applications:
  current_count: 4
  target:
    - name: Databricks
      status: keep
      group_assignment: grp-data-intelligence
    - name: GitHub Subscription Permission Validation
      status: keep
      notes: Microsoft-managed system app
    - name: ipai-n8n-entra
      status: keep
      group_assignment: grp-platform-admins
    - name: Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools
      status: keep
      group_assignment: grp-platform-admins
    - name: Odoo ERP SSO
      status: add_if_using_entra_signin
      group_assignment: grp-odoo-users
    - name: Tableau Cloud
      status: keep_or_add
      group_assignment: grp-data-intelligence

No direct user-to-app assignment unless unavoidable. enterprise_applications: current_count: 4 target: - name: Databricks status: keep group_assignment: grp-data-intelligence - name: GitHub Subscription Permission Validation status: keep notes: Microsoft-managed system app - name: ipai-n8n-entra status: keep group_assignment: grp-platform-admins - name: Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools status: keep group_assignment: grp-platform-admins - name: Odoo ERP SSO status: add_if_using_entra_signin group_assignment: grp-odoo-users - name: Tableau Cloud status: keep_or_add group_assignment: grp-data-intelligence

app_registrations:
  current_count: 6
  keep:
    - name: InsightPulse AI - Odoo ERP
      purpose: Odoo SSO/API integration
    - name: InsightPulse AI - Tableau Cloud

# Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization
# SSOT for which Odoo schemas sync to Plane, CRM, and Shelf
# Spec: spec/entra-identity-migration/ (unified auth eliminates identity sync)
# Created: 2026-03-22

# ─── Architecture Principle ────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Identity is NOT a sync problem.
# Entra ID is the single identity spine — all 6 apps authenticate via OIDC.
# JIT provisioning creates local user records on first login.
# No user replication, no password sync, no identity federation between apps.
#
# Only BUSINESS DATA syncs between apps:
#   - Odoo → Plane: project/task metadata
#   - Odoo → CRM: lead/contact projections
#   - Odoo ↔ Shelf: inventory (bidirectional)

identity:
  provider: Microsoft Entra ID
  protocol: OIDC
  sync_method: none  # JIT provisioning per app, no user replication
  apps:
    - odoo     # erp.insightpulseai.com
    - plane    # plane.insightpulseai.com
    - superset # superset.insightpulseai.com
    - n8n      # n8n.insightpulseai.com
    - shelf    # shelf.insightpulseai.com
    - crm      # crm.insightpulseai.com
  notes: >
    Each app creates a local user profile on first Entra login.
    Role/group assignment comes from Entra security group claims.
    No app-to-app user sync needed.

# ─── Odoo → Plane (Project/Task Metadata) ──────────────────────────────
sync_plane:
  direction: bidirectional
  transport: webhook + REST API
  trigger: Odoo server action on write + Plane webhooks (HMAC-SHA256)
  rate_limit: 60 req/min (Plane API)
  pagination: cursor-based

metadata.title: Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization

text:
shelf.insightpulseai.com - crm # crm.insightpulseai.com notes: > Each app creates a local user profile on first Entra login. Role/group assignment comes from Entra security group claims. No app-to-app user sync needed. # ─── Odoo → Plane (Project/Task Metadata) ────────────────────────────── sync_plane: direction: bidirectional transport: webhook + REST API trigger: Odoo server action on write + Plane webhooks (HMAC-SHA256) rate_limit: 60 req/min (Plane API) pagination: cursor-based

  odoo_to_plane:
    description: Project and task metadata projection
    schemas:
      - odoo_model: project.project
        plane_entity: Project
        fields:
          - odoo: name → plane: name
          - odoo: description → plane: description
          - odoo: date_start → plane: start_date
          - odoo: date → plane: target_date
          - odoo: ipai_ppm_budget_amount → plane: custom_field (budget)
          - odoo: ipai_ppm_cost_center → plane: custom_field (cost_center)
          - odoo: tag_ids.name → plane: labels
        key: odoo project.project.id ↔ plane project UUID (stored in ipai_plane_project_uuid)

      - odoo_model: project.task
        plane_entity: Issue
        fields:
          - odoo: name → plane: name
          - odoo: description → plane: description_html
          - odoo: stage_id.name → plane: state (mapped)
          - odoo: user_ids.email → plane: assignees (by Entra email)
          - odoo: priority → plane: priority (mapped: 0→none, 1→urgent)
          - odoo: date_deadline → plane: target_date
          - odoo: tag_ids.name → plane: labels
        key: odoo project.task.id ↔ plane issue UUID (stored in ipai_plane_issue_uuid)
        stage_mapping:
          draft: Backlog
          in_progress: In Progress
          review: In Review
          done: Done
          cancelled: Cancelled

metadata.title: Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization

text:
description → plane: description_html - odoo: stage_id.name → plane: state (mapped) - odoo: user_ids.email → plane: assignees (by Entra email) - odoo: priority → plane: priority (mapped: 0→none, 1→urgent) - odoo: date_deadline → plane: target_date - odoo: tag_ids.name → plane: labels key: odoo project.task.id ↔ plane issue UUID (stored in ipai_plane_issue_uuid) stage_mapping: draft: Backlog in_progress: In Progress review: In Review done: Done cancelled: Cancelled

  plane_to_odoo:
    description: Status and assignment updates flow back
    schemas:
      - plane_entity: Issue
        odoo_model: project.task
        fields:
          - plane: state → odoo: stage_id (reverse mapped)
          - plane: assignees → odoo: user_ids (by Entra email)
          - plane: priority → odoo: priority (reverse mapped)
        webhook_events:
          - issue.updated
          - issue.deleted
        notes: >
          Only status/assignment changes flow back.
          Task creation always originates in Odoo (SoR).

  module: ipai_plane_connector  # Odoo module handling sync
  mcp_server: plane  # MCP server at mcp.plane.so/http/mcp

# ─── Odoo → CRM (Lead/Contact Projection) ──────────────────────────────
sync_crm:
  direction: odoo_to_crm  # read projection, CRM is not SoR for contacts
  transport: REST API (scheduled sync)
  trigger: Odoo cron job (ir.cron) — every 15 minutes

metadata.title: Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization

text:
- issue.deleted notes: > Only status/assignment changes flow back. Task creation always originates in Odoo (SoR). module: ipai_plane_connector # Odoo module handling sync mcp_server: plane # MCP server at mcp.plane.so/http/mcp # ─── Odoo → CRM (Lead/Contact Projection) ────────────────────────────── sync_crm: direction: odoo_to_crm # read projection, CRM is not SoR for contacts transport: REST API (scheduled sync) trigger: Odoo cron job (ir.cron) — every 15 minutes

  schemas:
    - odoo_model: res.partner
      crm_entity: Contact
      filter: "partner.customer_rank > 0 OR partner.supplier_rank > 0"
      fields:
        - odoo: name → crm: name
        - odoo: email → crm: email
        - odoo: phone → crm: phone
        - odoo: mobile → crm: mobile
        - odoo: street, street2, city, state_id, zip, country_id → crm: address
        - odoo: company_type → crm: type (company/individual)
        - odoo: parent_id.name → crm: company_name
        - odoo: category_id.name → crm: tags
        - odoo: user_id.email → crm: owner (by Entra email)
      key: odoo res.partner.id ↔ crm contact UUID (stored in ipai_crm_contact_uuid)

    - odoo_model: crm.lead
      crm_entity: Deal
      fields:
        - odoo: name → crm: name
        - odoo: partner_id → crm: contact (via mapped UUID)
        - odoo: expected_revenue → crm: value
        - odoo: probability → crm: probability
        - odoo: stage_id.name → crm: stage (mapped)
        - odoo: user_id.email → crm: owner (by Entra email)
        - odoo: date_deadline → crm: close_date
        - odoo: tag_ids.name → crm: tags
        - odoo: description → crm: notes
      key: odoo crm.lead.id ↔ crm deal UUID (stored in ipai_crm_deal_uuid)

text:
odoo: partner_id → crm: contact (via mapped UUID) - odoo: expected_revenue → crm: value - odoo: probability → crm: probability - odoo: stage_id.name → crm: stage (mapped) - odoo: user_id.email → crm: owner (by Entra email) - odoo: date_deadline → crm: close_date - odoo: tag_ids.name → crm: tags - odoo: description → crm: notes key: odoo crm.lead.id ↔ crm deal UUID (stored in ipai_crm_deal_uuid)

    - odoo_model: sale.order
      crm_entity: Activity
      filter: "order.state in ('sale', 'done')"
      fields:
        - odoo: name → crm: reference
        - odoo: partner_id → crm: contact (via mapped UUID)
        - odoo: amount_total → crm: value
        - odoo: date_order → crm: date
      notes: Read-only activity log in CRM, not editable

  module: ipai_crm_connector  # Odoo module handling projection

# ─── Odoo ↔ Shelf (Inventory — Bidirectional) ──────────────────────────
sync_shelf:
  direction: bidirectional
  transport: event-driven (Odoo server actions + Shelf webhooks) + scheduled reconciliation
  trigger:
    realtime: Odoo server action on stock.move confirm + Shelf inventory webhooks
    batch: Odoo cron job — full reconciliation every 4 hours

  odoo_to_shelf:
    description: Product catalog and stock levels
    schemas:
      - odoo_model: product.product
        shelf_entity: Item
        fields:
          - odoo: name → shelf: name
          - odoo: default_code → shelf: sku
          - odoo: barcode → shelf: barcode
          - odoo: categ_id.complete_name → shelf: category
          - odoo: list_price → shelf: price
          - odoo: standard_price → shelf: cost
          - odoo: uom_id.name → shelf: unit
          - odoo: weight → shelf: weight
          - odoo: image_128 → shelf: thumbnail (base64)
          - odoo: active → shelf: active
        key: odoo product.product.id ↔ shelf item UUID (stored in ipai_shelf_item_uuid)

metadata.title: Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization

text:
odoo: default_code → shelf: sku - odoo: barcode → shelf: barcode - odoo: categ_id.complete_name → shelf: category - odoo: list_price → shelf: price - odoo: standard_price → shelf: cost - odoo: uom_id.name → shelf: unit - odoo: weight → shelf: weight - odoo: image_128 → shelf: thumbnail (base64) - odoo: active → shelf: active key: odoo product.product.id ↔ shelf item UUID (stored in ipai_shelf_item_uuid)

      - odoo_model: stock.quant
        shelf_entity: StockLevel
        fields:
          - odoo: product_id → shelf: item (via mapped UUID)
          - odoo: location_id.complete_name → shelf: location
          - odoo: quantity → shelf: on_hand
          - odoo: reserved_quantity → shelf: reserved
          - odoo: lot_id.name → shelf: lot_number
        notes: >
          Aggregated by product+location.
          Shelf displays real-time stock from Odoo quants.

      - odoo_model: stock.lot
        shelf_entity: Lot
        fields:
          - odoo: name → shelf: lot_number
          - odoo: product_id → shelf: item (via mapped UUID)
          - odoo: expiration_date → shelf: expiry_date
          - odoo: ref → shelf: reference
        key: odoo stock.lot.id ↔ shelf lot UUID (stored in ipai_shelf_lot_uuid)

  shelf_to_odoo:
    description: Inventory adjustments and receiving confirmations
    schemas:
      - shelf_entity: InventoryAdjustment
        odoo_model: stock.quant
        action: stock.quant.inventory_adjust_action
        fields:
          - shelf: item → odoo: product_id (via reverse UUID lookup)
          - shelf: location → odoo: location_id (by complete_name)
          - shelf: new_quantity → odoo: inventory_quantity
          - shelf: reason → odoo: inventory_diff_reason
        notes: >
          Shelf barcode scans trigger inventory adjustments in Odoo.
          Each adjustment creates an Odoo stock.move for audit trail.

metadata.title: Schema Sync Map — Cross-App Data Synchronization

text:
description: Inventory adjustments and receiving confirmations schemas: - shelf_entity: InventoryAdjustment odoo_model: stock.quant action: stock.quant.inventory_adjust_action fields: - shelf: item → odoo: product_id (via reverse UUID lookup) - shelf: location → odoo: location_id (by complete_name) - shelf: new_quantity → odoo: inventory_quantity - shelf: reason → odoo: inventory_diff_reason notes: > Shelf barcode scans trigger inventory adjustments in Odoo. Each adjustment creates an Odoo stock.move for audit trail.

      - shelf_entity: ReceivingConfirmation
        odoo_model: stock.picking
        action: button_validate
        fields:
          - shelf: purchase_reference → odoo: origin (PO number lookup)
          - shelf: items[].sku → odoo: move_ids.product_id (by default_code)
          - shelf: items[].received_qty → odoo: move_ids.quantity
          - shelf: items[].lot_number → odoo: move_ids.lot_ids
        notes: >
          Shelf confirms receipt against Odoo purchase orders.
          Validates quantities and creates lot records if new.

  reconciliation:
    schedule: "0 */4 * * *"  # every 4 hours
    method: >
      Full stock.quant snapshot compared to Shelf StockLevel.
      Discrepancies logged to ipai_shelf_sync_log.
      Auto-correct if delta < 5 units; flag for review if larger.

  module: ipai_shelf_connector  # Odoo module handling inventory sync

infrastructure:
  orchestrator: n8n  # n8n.insightpulseai.com

  error_handling:
    retry: 3 attempts with exponential backoff
    dead_letter: ops.sync_dead_letter table in Supabase
    alerting: Slack #ops-alerts channel
  logging:
    sync_events: ops.platform_events (Supabase, append-only)
    module_logs: Odoo ir.logging (per-module)

text:
# =============================================================================
# DNS & Mail Authority Boundary — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Defines the single source of truth for each layer of domain/mail config.
# Prevents split-brain DNS and duplicated mail records.
#
# Domain: insightpulseai.com
# Last updated: 2026-03-26
# =============================================================================

schema_version: 1
kind: dns_mail_authority
last_reviewed: "2026-03-26"
domain: insightpulseai.com

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# AUTHORITY MODEL — who owns what
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

authority:
  registrar:
    provider: squarespace
    owns:
      - domain_registration
      - nameserver_delegation
    does_not_own:
      - dns_records
      - mail_configuration
    nameservers:
      - ns1-05.azure-dns.com
      - ns2-05.azure-dns.net
      - ns3-05.azure-dns.org
      - ns4-05.azure-dns.info

  dns:
    provider: azure_dns
    owns:
      - all_dns_records
      - mx_records
      - spf_txt_records
      - dkim_txt_records
      - dmarc_txt_records
      - cname_records
      - a_records
    notes: >
      Azure DNS is authoritative for insightpulseai.com once nameservers
      are delegated from Squarespace. All DNS record changes must be made
      in Azure DNS, not in Squarespace or Zoho.

  mail:
    provider: zoho
    owns:
      - mailbox_hosting
      - dkim_key_generation
      - dmarc_report_guidance
      - admin_console_settings
    does_not_own:
      - dns_records
    notes: >
      Zoho is the mail service. DKIM keys are generated in Zoho but
      published as TXT records in Azure DNS. DMARC policy is published
      in Azure DNS. Zoho is not the DNS authority.

All DNS record changes must be made in Azure DNS, not in Squarespace or Zoho. mail: provider: zoho owns: - mailbox_hosting - dkim_key_generation - dmarc_report_guidance - admin_console_settings does_not_own: - dns_records notes: > Zoho is the mail service. DKIM keys are generated in Zoho but published as TXT records in Azure DNS. DMARC policy is published in Azure DNS. Zoho is not the DNS authority.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# REQUIRED MAIL DNS RECORDS (in Azure DNS)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

mail_dns_records:
  mx:
    - host: "@"
      type: MX
      priority: 10
      value: mx.zoho.com
    - host: "@"
      type: MX
      priority: 20
      value: mx2.zoho.com
    - host: "@"
      type: MX
      priority: 50
      value: mx3.zoho.com

  spf:
    - host: "@"
      type: TXT
      value: "v=spf1 include:zohomail.com ~all"

  dkim:
    - host: zoho._domainkey
      type: TXT
      value: "v=DKIM1; k=rsa; "
      status: verified

  dmarc:
    - host: _dmarc
      type: TXT
      value: "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc-reports@insightpulseai.com"
      notes: >
        Canonical DMARC record. Policy is quarantine (not none).
        Reporting goes to role mailbox dmarc-reports@insightpulseai.com.
        Zoho admin must be updated to match this value — the old
        p=none / personal address config is superseded.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ENFORCEMENT RULES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

dkim: - host: zoho._domainkey type: TXT value: "v=DKIM1; k=rsa; " status: verified dmarc: - host: _dmarc type: TXT value: "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc-reports@insightpulseai.com" notes: > Canonical DMARC record. Policy is quarantine (not none). Reporting goes to role mailbox dmarc-reports@insightpulseai.com. Zoho admin must be updated to match this value — the old p=none / personal address config is superseded. # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # ENFORCEMENT RULES # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

enforcement:
  - "Azure DNS is the single authoritative DNS zone — no split-brain"
  - "Squarespace retains registrar role only — no DNS record edits there"
  - "Zoho is mail service only — DNS records for mail live in Azure DNS"
  - "MX, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC must all exist in Azure DNS"
  - "Do not maintain duplicate mail DNS records in Squarespace"
  - "DKIM keys are generated by Zoho, published in Azure DNS"
  - "DMARC reporting should use a role mailbox, not a personal address"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEPRECATED PROVIDERS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

deprecated:
  - provider: cloudflare
    role: dns
    replacement: azure_dns
    date: "2026-03-26"
    notes: >
      Cloudflare was previously used as DNS proxy. Azure DNS is now
      authoritative via direct nameserver delegation from Squarespace.
      Remove Cloudflare references from subdomain-registry.yaml.

"DKIM keys are generated by Zoho, published in Azure DNS" - "DMARC reporting should use a role mailbox, not a personal address" # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # DEPRECATED PROVIDERS # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── deprecated: - provider: cloudflare role: dns replacement: azure_dns date: "2026-03-26" notes: > Cloudflare was previously used as DNS proxy. Azure DNS is now authoritative via direct nameserver delegation from Squarespace. Remove Cloudflare references from subdomain-registry.yaml.

  - provider: mailgun
    role: smtp
    replacement: zoho_smtp
    date: "2026-03-11"
    notes: "mg.insightpulseai.com fully retired"

systems:
  supabase:
    role: ssot
    instance: spdtwktxdalcfigzeqrz
    owns: "Platform/control-plane entities, metadata, workflow state, vector embeddings, agent memory"

  odoo:
    role: system_of_record
    instance: ipai-odoo-dev-web

  adls:
    role: analytical_lake
    account: adlsipaidev  # pending provisioning

  azure_ai_foundry:
    role: compute_only
    instance: aifoundry-ipai-dev
    owns: "No data ownership. Model training and scoring compute."

text:
version: 1
namespace: odoo.integrations.databricks
last_reviewed: "2026-03-15"
source:
  kind: integration_contract
  knowledge_base: lakehouse/ssot/benchmark/databricks_apps_lakebase_kb.yaml
  skill_contract: ssot/ai/agent_skills_databricks.yaml
description: >
  Integration patterns between Odoo CE/Copilot and Azure Databricks,
  grounded in the Databricks Apps + Lakebase architecture guide.
  Defines how Odoo operational data flows to/from the lakehouse and
  how Odoo Copilot agents leverage Databricks capabilities.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. Data Flow Patterns
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
data_flows:
  odoo_to_lakehouse:
    label: Odoo -> Lakehouse (Analytical Ingestion)
    description: >
      Replicate Odoo operational data into Unity Catalog Delta tables
      for analytics, ML, and reporting. This is the forward ETL path.
    method: n8n_cdc_or_batch
    source: Odoo PostgreSQL (self-hosted)
    destination: Unity Catalog Delta tables
    pattern:
      - n8n triggers on Odoo webhook or schedule
      - Extracts records via Odoo XML-RPC or JSON-RPC
      - Writes to ADLS Gen2 staging area
      - Databricks Auto Loader or DLT ingests into Bronze/Silver/Gold
    entities_to_replicate:
      priority_1:
        - account.move (invoices, bills, entries)
        - account.move.line (journal items)
        - account.payment
        - hr.expense
        - hr.expense.sheet (expense reports)
        - account.bank.statement.line (bank transactions)
      priority_2:
        - account.account (chart of accounts)
        - account.journal
        - account.analytic.account
        - account.tax
        - res.partner
        - hr.employee
    refresh_cadence:
      operational: every 15 minutes
      analytical: daily
      compliance: real-time for tax-sensitive entities

ADLS Gen2 staging area - Databricks Auto Loader or DLT ingests into Bronze/Silver/Gold entities_to_replicate: priority_1: - account.move (invoices, bills, entries) - account.move.line (journal items) - account.payment - hr.expense - hr.expense.sheet (expense reports) - account.bank.statement.line (bank transactions) priority_2: - account.account (chart of accounts) - account.journal - account.analytic.account - account.tax - res.partner - hr.employee refresh_cadence: operational: every 15 minutes analytical: daily compliance: real-time for tax-sensitive entities

  lakehouse_to_odoo:
    label: Lakehouse -> Odoo (Reverse ETL / Insights Delivery)
    description: >
      Deliver analytical insights, ML predictions, and aggregated
      metrics back into Odoo operational workflows via Lakebase.
    method: lakebase_synced_tables
    source: Unity Catalog Delta tables (Gold layer)
    destination: Lakebase synced tables -> Odoo Copilot queries
    pattern:
      - Gold tables computed in Databricks (aggregations, predictions, features)
      - Synced to Lakebase via managed sync pipeline
      - Odoo Copilot agents query Lakebase for operational context
      - No custom ETL pipeline needed
    sync_mode_by_use_case:
      expense_anomaly_scores: triggered
      reconciliation_suggestions: continuous
      financial_kpis: snapshot
      compliance_risk_flags: continuous
      vendor_payment_predictions: triggered

workflows via Lakebase. method: lakebase_synced_tables source: Unity Catalog Delta tables (Gold layer) destination: Lakebase synced tables -> Odoo Copilot queries pattern: - Gold tables computed in Databricks (aggregations, predictions, features) - Synced to Lakebase via managed sync pipeline - Odoo Copilot agents query Lakebase for operational context - No custom ETL pipeline needed sync_mode_by_use_case: expense_anomaly_scores: triggered reconciliation_suggestions: continuous financial_kpis: snapshot compliance_risk_flags: continuous vendor_payment_predictions: triggered

  bidirectional_state:
    label: Bidirectional State (Copilot Actions)
    description: >
      Odoo Copilot agents read from Lakebase (analytical context) and
      write to Lakebase (action state), creating a closed loop between
      analytics and operations.
    read_path:
      source: Lakebase synced tables
      use: Grounding agent responses with analytical context
      examples:
        - "What is the reconciliation match rate this month?"
        - "Show me expense anomalies flagged by the ML model"
        - "What is the predicted cash position for next week?"
    write_path:
      destination: Lakebase app state tables
      use: Recording agent actions and user decisions
      examples:
        - Agent records expense approval decision
        - Agent logs reconciliation match acceptance
        - Agent persists investigation thread state

analytical context examples: - "What is the reconciliation match rate this month?" - "Show me expense anomalies flagged by the ML model" - "What is the predicted cash position for next week?" write_path: destination: Lakebase app state tables use: Recording agent actions and user decisions examples: - Agent records expense approval decision - Agent logs reconciliation match acceptance - Agent persists investigation thread state

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. Agent Architecture Patterns
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
agent_patterns:
  stateful_finance_agent:
    label: Stateful Finance Copilot Agent
    description: >
      Odoo Copilot agent that uses Lakebase for conversational memory
      and analytical grounding, following the Databricks LangGraph
      checkpointing pattern.
    components:
      llm: Azure OpenAI (via Odoo copilot-provider.yaml)
      tools:
        - Odoo XML-RPC actions (create, update, read)
        - Unity Catalog functions (analytical queries)
        - Lakebase queries (operational context)
      memory: Lakebase via langgraph-checkpoint-postgres
      orchestration: LangGraph
      deployment: Odoo module + Databricks AI Agent Framework
    workflow_example:
      name: Month-end close investigation
      steps:
        - Agent receives "Review outstanding reconciliation items"
        - Queries Lakebase synced table for unreconciled transactions
        - Retrieves ML-predicted match suggestions from Gold table
        - Presents ranked suggestions to user
        - User approves/rejects matches
        - Agent records decisions in Lakebase state table
        - Agent executes approved reconciliations in Odoo
        - Checkpoint saved; investigation resumable tomorrow

Databricks AI Agent Framework workflow_example: name: Month-end close investigation steps: - Agent receives "Review outstanding reconciliation items" - Queries Lakebase synced table for unreconciled transactions - Retrieves ML-predicted match suggestions from Gold table - Presents ranked suggestions to user - User approves/rejects matches - Agent records decisions in Lakebase state table - Agent executes approved reconciliations in Odoo - Checkpoint saved; investigation resumable tomorrow

  governed_tool_agent:
    label: Governed Tool Agent (UC Functions as Odoo Tools)
    description: >
      Pattern for exposing Odoo data as Unity Catalog SQL functions
      that agents can invoke with full governance, lineage, and audit.
    pattern:
      - Define SQL functions in Unity Catalog that query Odoo-replicated data
      - Register functions as agent tools via unitycatalog-langchain
      - Agent invokes tools with automatic permission checks
      - Lineage tracked through Unity Catalog
    example_functions:
      - name: get_vendor_payment_history
        description: Returns payment history for a vendor over N months
        input: vendor_id STRING, months INTEGER
        source: Gold table aggregating account.payment records
      - name: get_expense_anomaly_score
        description: Returns ML anomaly score for an expense report
        input: expense_report_id STRING
        source: ML inference output table
      - name: get_close_task_status
        description: Returns current status of month-end close tasks
        input: period STRING
        source: Odoo project.task replicated to Delta

- name: get_vendor_payment_history description: Returns payment history for a vendor over N months input: vendor_id STRING, months INTEGER source: Gold table aggregating account.payment records - name: get_expense_anomaly_score description: Returns ML anomaly score for an expense report input: expense_report_id STRING source: ML inference output table - name: get_close_task_status description: Returns current status of month-end close tasks input: period STRING source: Odoo project.task replicated to Delta

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. Security Model
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
security:
  auth_chain:
    - layer: Odoo
      method: Session-based auth + ACL/record rules
      scope: Odoo UI and API access
    - layer: n8n
      method: API key or OAuth for Odoo XML-RPC
      scope: Data replication triggers
    - layer: Databricks
      method: OAuth M2M via service principal
      scope: Unity Catalog, Lakebase, Model Serving
    - layer: Lakebase
      method: OAuth token as Postgres password
      scope: Table-level GRANT permissions
      token_refresh: Automatic via WorkspaceClient (15-min cycle)

  governance_alignment:
    description: >
      Unity Catalog governance aligns with Odoo ACL model. Data replicated
      from Odoo inherits both Odoo-level permissions (at replication time)
      and Unity Catalog permissions (at query time).
    rules:
      - Synced tables are read-only; agents cannot write to analytical data
      - App state tables require explicit GRANT per service principal
      - UC function permissions are separate from table permissions
      - Audit trail covers both Odoo actions and Databricks queries

aligns with Odoo ACL model. Data replicated from Odoo inherits both Odoo-level permissions (at replication time) and Unity Catalog permissions (at query time). rules: - Synced tables are read-only; agents cannot write to analytical data - App state tables require explicit GRANT per service principal - UC function permissions are separate from table permissions - Audit trail covers both Odoo actions and Databricks queries

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. Deployment Model
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
deployment:
  odoo_side:
    components:
      - ipai_ai_copilot module (Odoo agent UI)
      - ipai_ai_core module (LLM provider abstraction)
      - n8n workflows (data replication)
    config:
      - copilot-provider.yaml (Azure OpenAI settings)
      - agent_skills_databricks.yaml (skill definitions)

  databricks_side:
    components:
      - Lakebase instance (managed Postgres)
      - Synced tables (analytical data -> Postgres)
      - App state tables (agent memory + action state)
      - Unity Catalog functions (governed tools)
      - Model serving endpoint (if using Databricks-hosted LLM)
    deployment_method: Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs)
    bundle_structure:
      - databricks.yml (targets: dev, staging, production)
      - resources/database.yml (Lakebase instance + synced tables)
      - resources/functions.yml (UC function definitions)
      - agent/ (agent source code)

  azure_infrastructure:
    - Azure Databricks workspace
    - ADLS Gen2 (lakehouse storage)
    - Azure Key Vault (secrets for service principals)
    - Odoo CE on Azure VM/AKS (self-hosted)
    - n8n on Azure VM/AKS (self-hosted)

(if using Databricks-hosted LLM) deployment_method: Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) bundle_structure: - databricks.yml (targets: dev, staging, production) - resources/database.yml (Lakebase instance + synced tables) - resources/functions.yml (UC function definitions) - agent/ (agent source code) azure_infrastructure: - Azure Databricks workspace - ADLS Gen2 (lakehouse storage) - Azure Key Vault (secrets for service principals) - Odoo CE on Azure VM/AKS (self-hosted) - n8n on Azure VM/AKS (self-hosted)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 5. Implementation Priority
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
implementation_priority:
  phase_1:
    label: Analytical Grounding
    description: Odoo data replicated to lakehouse, Gold tables queryable via Copilot
    deliverables:
      - n8n replication workflows for priority_1 entities
      - Bronze/Silver/Gold Delta tables in Unity Catalog
      - Lakebase synced tables for Gold layer
      - query_lakebase_synced skill operational in Copilot

  phase_2:
    label: Governed Tools
    description: UC functions expose analytical context as agent-callable tools
    deliverables:
      - 3-5 UC functions for finance context
      - invoke_uc_function skill operational
      - Lineage and audit visible in Unity Catalog

  phase_3:
    label: Stateful Agents
    description: Copilot agents persist memory across sessions
    deliverables:
      - Lakebase checkpoint tables for LangGraph
      - checkpoint_save and checkpoint_resume skills operational
      - Multi-session finance workflows demonstrable

  phase_4:
    label: Closed Loop
    description: Agents write action state back to Lakebase
    deliverables:
      - App state tables for agent decisions
      - write_lakebase_state skill operational
      - Bidirectional flow verified end-to-end

flows:
  - id: odoo_supabase_master_data_sync
    name: Odoo to Supabase master data sync
    source_system: odoo
    target_system: supabase
    direction: odoo_to_supabase
    engine_type: supabase_etl
    trigger_type: schedule
    data_owner: odoo
    execution_owner: supabase
    state: planned
    criticality: high
    evidence_ref: docs/evidence/integrations/README.md
    notes: >-
      Canonical data replication path for master data (partners, products,
      accounts). Must not be implemented as an n8n workflow unless an
      exception is documented via exception_reason.

  - id: odoo_operational_webhook_automation
    name: Odoo operational webhook automation
    source_system: odoo
    target_system: n8n
    direction: external_to_odoo
    engine_type: n8n
    trigger_type: webhook
    data_owner: odoo
    execution_owner: n8n
    state: planned
    criticality: medium
    evidence_ref: docs/evidence/integrations/README.md
    notes: >-
      Use n8n for event-driven orchestration, side effects,
      notifications, and approval workflows. Not for bulk
      canonical replication.

  - id: n8n_supabase_task_bus
    name: n8n to Supabase task bus writes
    source_system: n8n
    target_system: supabase
    direction: external_to_supabase
    engine_type: n8n
    trigger_type: event
    data_owner: n8n
    execution_owner: n8n
    state: planned
    criticality: medium
    evidence_ref: docs/evidence/integrations/README.md
    notes: >-
      n8n writes operational events and task queue entries to Supabase.
      This is workflow automation, not canonical replication.

# SAP Joule + SAP Concur + Odoo on Azure — Integration SSOT
# Spec: spec/sap-joule-concur-odoo-azure/
# Contract: docs/contracts/SAP_JOULE_CONCUR_ODOO_AZURE_CONTRACT.md

version: "1.0"
schema: ssot.integrations.v1
updated_at: "2026-03-13"

systems:
  sap_joule:
    role: conversational_surface
    owner: sap
    description: "AI copilot/agent in SAP ecosystem with Microsoft 365 Copilot interop"

  sap_concur:
    role: expense_travel_source
    owner: sap
    description: "Spend management — expense reports, travel bookings, invoices"

  odoo:
    role: erp_system_of_record
    owner: insightpulseai
    runtime: azure_container_apps
    instance: ipai-odoo-dev-web
    description: "Accounting, project, approval, posting, operational control"

  azure:
    role: integration_runtime
    owner: microsoft
    services:
      identity: "Microsoft Entra ID"
      secrets: "Azure Key Vault (kv-ipai-dev)"
      observability: "Azure Monitor / Application Insights"
      ai: "Azure AI Foundry (aifoundry-ipai-dev)"
      compute: "Azure Container Apps (cae-ipai-dev)"

flows:
  - id: concur_expense_to_odoo_draft
    type: document_exchange
    source: sap_concur
    target: odoo
    mediation: azure
    write_mode: draft_only
    idempotency_key: concur_report_id
    target_model: account.move
    target_field: ref
    audit_required: true
    retry_policy:
      max_attempts: 5
      backoff: exponential
      dead_letter: true

  - id: joule_query_odoo_operational_context
    type: event_orchestration
    source: sap_joule
    target: odoo
    mediation: azure
    write_mode: read_only
    endpoints:
      - /api/v1/projects/summary
      - /api/v1/expenses/status/{report_id}
      - /api/v1/approvals/pending
      - /api/v1/budget/analytic/{account_id}
    audit_required: true

  - id: joule_bounded_action
    type: event_orchestration
    source: sap_joule
    target: odoo
    mediation: azure
    write_mode: bounded_write
    allowed_actions:
      - request_approval
      - query_status
    audit_required: true

text:
odoo mediation: azure write_mode: draft_only idempotency_key: concur_report_id target_model: account.move target_field: ref audit_required: true retry_policy: max_attempts: 5 backoff: exponential dead_letter: true - id: joule_query_odoo_operational_context type: event_orchestration source: sap_joule target: odoo mediation: azure write_mode: read_only endpoints: - /api/v1/projects/summary - /api/v1/expenses/status/{report_id} - /api/v1/approvals/pending - /api/v1/budget/analytic/{account_id} audit_required: true - id: joule_bounded_action type: event_orchestration source: sap_joule target: odoo mediation: azure write_mode: bounded_write allowed_actions: - request_approval - query_status audit_required: true

  - id: entra_sso_for_concur
    type: identity_access
    source: azure
    target: sap_concur
    mediation: none
    protocol: saml_oidc
    audit_required: true

  - id: entra_sso_for_odoo
    type: identity_access
    source: azure
    target: odoo
    mediation: none
    protocol: oidc
    audit_required: true

  - id: employee_master_sync
    type: master_data_sync
    source: odoo
    target: sap_concur
    mediation: azure
    write_mode: reference
    source_model: hr.employee
    audit_required: true

  - id: vendor_master_sync
    type: master_data_sync
    source: odoo
    target: sap_concur
    mediation: azure
    write_mode: reference
    source_model: res.partner
    filter: "supplier=True"
    audit_required: true

data_ownership:
  expense_reports: sap_concur

  journal_entries: odoo
  employee_master: odoo
  vendor_master: odoo
  project_budget: odoo
  user_identity: azure
  secrets: azure
