text:
schema_version: "1.0"
namespace: microsoft_marketplace.outlook_addin
last_reviewed: "2026-03-28"
description: >
  Machine-readable contract for publishing Odoo inbox integration as a
  Microsoft 365 / Outlook add-in. Validated by marketplace evidence:
  Odoo Inbox Addin, Odoo Connector, and Odoo Connector for Outlook
  are already listed as Outlook apps on Microsoft Marketplace.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Package identity
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
package:
  name: "Odoo Inbox for Outlook"
  publisher: "InsightPulse AI"
  package_type: outlook_addin  # outlook_addin | teams_app | m365_app
  manifest_version: "1.17"
  notes: >
    Outlook add-ins follow the Microsoft 365 app model.
    Publishing through Teams Store also makes the app discoverable
    in Outlook and Microsoft 365 app stores.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Surfaces
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
surfaces:
  outlook_web: true
  outlook_desktop: true
  outlook_mobile: false  # mobile support deferred
  teams_integration: false  # separate decision

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Capabilities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
capabilities:
  message_read:
    enabled: true
    description: "Show Odoo context when reading emails"
    permissions: [ReadItem]
  message_compose:
    enabled: true
    description: "Create Odoo records from compose view"
    permissions: [ReadWriteItem]
  contextual:
    enabled: true
    description: "Activate on emails matching Odoo contacts"
    permissions: [ReadItem]
    activation_rules: [known_entity_email]
  task_pane:
    enabled: true
    description: "Side panel showing Odoo CRM/contact info"
    permissions: [ReadItem]

support deferred teams_integration: false # separate decision # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Capabilities # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- capabilities: message_read: enabled: true description: "Show Odoo context when reading emails" permissions: [ReadItem] message_compose: enabled: true description: "Create Odoo records from compose view" permissions: [ReadWriteItem] contextual: enabled: true description: "Activate on emails matching Odoo contacts" permissions: [ReadItem] activation_rules: [known_entity_email] task_pane: enabled: true description: "Side panel showing Odoo CRM/contact info" permissions: [ReadItem]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Auth mapping
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
auth:
  entra_app_id: "07bd9669"
  sso_type: oidc
  permissions:
    - openid
    - profile
    - email
    - User.Read
  backend_auth: bearer_validation
  odoo_user_mapping: email_to_res_users
  notes: >
    Outlook add-in SSO uses Entra OIDC. Backend validates bearer token
    and maps Entra user to Odoo res.users via email. Same Entra app
    as Odoo web login (07bd9669).

true description: "Side panel showing Odoo CRM/contact info" permissions: [ReadItem] # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Auth mapping # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- auth: entra_app_id: "07bd9669" sso_type: oidc permissions: - openid - profile - email - User.Read backend_auth: bearer_validation odoo_user_mapping: email_to_res_users notes: > Outlook add-in SSO uses Entra OIDC. Backend validates bearer token and maps Entra user to Odoo res.users via email. Same Entra app as Odoo web login (07bd9669).

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Backend integration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
backend:
  api_endpoint: https://erp.insightpulseai.com/ipai/outlook
  operations:
    - id: lookup_contact
      method: POST
      path: /ipai/outlook/contact/lookup
      description: "Find Odoo partner by email address"
    - id: create_lead
      method: POST
      path: /ipai/outlook/crm/lead
      description: "Create CRM lead from email"
    - id: log_activity
      method: POST
      path: /ipai/outlook/activity/log
      description: "Log email as Odoo activity on partner"
    - id: create_task
      method: POST
      path: /ipai/outlook/task/create
      description: "Create project task from email"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Store assets
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
store_assets:
  app_title: "Odoo Inbox for Outlook"
  short_description: "Connect Outlook to your Odoo ERP — contacts, leads, activities."
  long_description_ready: false
  icon_128x128: pending
  icon_64x64: pending
  icon_32x32: pending
  screenshots_ready: false
  support_url: https://insightpulseai.com/support
  privacy_url: https://insightpulseai.com/privacy
  terms_url: https://insightpulseai.com/terms

POST path: /ipai/outlook/activity/log description: "Log email as Odoo activity on partner" - id: create_task method: POST path: /ipai/outlook/task/create description: "Create project task from email" # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Store assets # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- store_assets: app_title: "Odoo Inbox for Outlook" short_description: "Connect Outlook to your Odoo ERP — contacts, leads, activities." long_description_ready: false icon_128x128: pending icon_64x64: pending icon_32x32: pending screenshots_ready: false support_url: https://insightpulseai.com/support privacy_url: https://insightpulseai.com/privacy terms_url: https://insightpulseai.com/terms

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Release gates
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
gates:
  manifest_created: false
  manifest_validated: false
  entra_permissions_configured: false
  backend_endpoints_deployed: false
  odoo_module_created: false  # ipai_outlook_connector
  sideload_tested: false
  org_catalog_published: false
  store_assets_complete: false
  partner_center_submitted: false
  store_review_approved: false

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Relationship to other surfaces
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
related_surfaces:
  gmail_addon:
    ssot: ssot/google-workspace/gmail-addon-contract.yaml
    relationship: "Parallel inbox integration for Google Workspace"
    shared_backend_pattern: true
    notes: "Both surfaces use email-to-Odoo-partner mapping and similar operations"
  saas_offer:
    ssot: ssot/microsoft-marketplace/saas-offer.yaml
    relationship: "Companion listing — SaaS is the hosted product, this is the inbox extension"
  copilot_agent:
    ssot: ssot/microsoft365/distribution-contract.yaml
    relationship: "Future — Copilot agent is a separate packaging path"

schema_version: "1.0"
namespace: microsoft_marketplace.saas_offer
last_reviewed: "2026-03-28"
description: >
  Machine-readable contract for publishing Odoo Copilot on Azure as a
  Microsoft Marketplace SaaS offer. This is the fastest realistic
  Microsoft Marketplace path because it matches an externally hosted
  product with its own backend and web app.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Offer identity
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
offer:
  name: "Odoo Copilot on Azure"
  publisher: "InsightPulse AI"
  offer_type: saas
  lane: primary  # primary | secondary | deferred
  listing_mode: contact_me  # transactable | byol | free | trial | contact_me
  azure_benefit_eligible: false
  certified_software_target: false
  listing_mode_notes: >
    Start with contact_me (lowest friction). Upgrade to transactable
    after validating demand and setting up SaaS fulfillment APIs.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Messaging — AvaTax-style listing copy structure
# Benchmark: Avalara AvaTax SaaS detail page on Microsoft Marketplace
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
messaging:
  category_statement: "AI-first ERP for modern teams — Odoo CE 19 on Azure with built-in copilot."
  headline: "Odoo Copilot on Azure"
  subheadline: "Enterprise ERP with AI copilot, governed data, and Microsoft identity — no Enterprise license required."
  overview: >
    Odoo Copilot on Azure combines Odoo CE 19 with an AI copilot powered
    by Microsoft Foundry, enterprise identity via Microsoft Entra ID, and
    a governed data layer through Databricks and Fabric. Purpose-built for
    teams that need ERP capabilities without Enterprise licensing, with
    AI assistance that understands your business context.
  capability_bullets:
    - "AI copilot for ERP — informational, navigational, and transactional assistance"
    - "Odoo CE 19 with 80%+ Enterprise parity via OCA + custom modules"
    - "Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for workforce and workload identity"
    - "Governed data layer — medallion architecture with gold-only copilot grounding"
    - "Azure-native deployment — Container Apps, Front Door, Key Vault, managed PostgreSQL"
    - "Philippines BIR tax compliance built in"
    - "Financial close automation with audit trails"
    - "Gmail and Outlook inbox integration"
  integrations:
    - "Microsoft Entra ID (SSO, conditional access)"
    - "Microsoft Foundry (AI copilot backend)"
    - "Azure Databricks (governed data engineering)"
    - "Microsoft Fabric (mirroring, OneLake)"
    - "Power BI (business reporting)"
    - "Azure Key Vault (secrets management)"
    - "Azure Container Apps (compute)"
    - "Azure Front Door (edge, WAF, TLS)"
    - "Google Workspace (Gmail add-on)"
    - "Zoho SMTP (outbound email)"
  scale_proof:
    - "24 custom IPAI modules in production"
    - "10-benchmark architecture governance model"
    - "297+ automated contract assertions across 14 test suites"
    - "2,917-page documentation site"
    - "CI-enforced SSOT contracts for runtime, identity, data, and interop"
  trust_proof:
    - "Microsoft Entra ID P2 for identity and conditional access"
    - "Azure Key Vault for all secrets — zero hardcoded credentials"
    - "Managed identity for service-to-service authentication"
    - "Gold-only copilot grounding — AI never reads raw operational data"
    - "Append-only audit logs for copilot actions"
    - "OCA community modules for proven, peer-reviewed functionality"
  long_description_ready: false

SSOT contracts for runtime, identity, data, and interop" trust_proof: - "Microsoft Entra ID P2 for identity and conditional access" - "Azure Key Vault for all secrets — zero hardcoded credentials" - "Managed identity for service-to-service authentication" - "Gold-only copilot grounding — AI never reads raw operational data" - "Append-only audit logs for copilot actions" - "OCA community modules for proven, peer-reviewed functionality" long_description_ready: false

version: 1
namespace: ipai.ssot.migration.production_seed_plan.v1
artifact_type: policy

subject: production_seed_plan
updated_at: "2026-03-16"

# ============================================================================
# UNIFIED PRODUCTION SEED PLAN
# ============================================================================
# Single source of truth for Odoo production database bootstrap.
# Canonical reference: ssot/migration/seed_canonical_map.yaml
# Audit reference: docs/architecture/SEED_DATA_INVENTORY.md
# ============================================================================

target_databases:
  dev: odoo_dev
  staging: odoo_staging
  prod: odoo
  forbidden: [odoo_prod]

goals:
  - reproducible_bootstrap
  - deterministic_seed_order
  - deduplicated_seed_sources
  - post_seed_validation
  - evidence_emission

# ============================================================================
# PHASE 1: Odoo CE Core + OCA Baseline
# ============================================================================
phases:
  - id: phase_1_core
    title: "Odoo CE core + OCA baseline"
    source_of_truth: config/addons.manifest.yaml
    actions:
      - name: create_database
        command: "odoo-bin --database=$DB_NAME --init=base --stop-after-init"
        idempotent: true
      - name: install_oca_baseline
        command: "python3 scripts/odoo/install_oca_from_ssot.py --db=$DB_NAME"
        reference: config/addons.manifest.yaml
    outputs:
      - initialized_database
      - base_models_present
      - oca_modules_installed
    validation:
      - "SELECT count(*) FROM ir_module_module WHERE state='installed' AND name='base'"
      - "SELECT count(*) FROM ir_module_module WHERE state='installed' AND author LIKE '%OCA%'"
    estimated_duration: "10-15 minutes"

# ==========================================================================
  # PHASE 2: Canonical IPAI Seed Modules (Deduplicated)
  # ==========================================================================
  - id: phase_2_ipai_seed
    title: "IPAI seed modules (canonical only)"
    source_of_truth: ssot/migration/seed_canonical_map.yaml
    canonical_modules:
      - module: ipai_finance_workflow
        purpose: "Finance stages (5), projects (2), month-end tasks (20)"
        note: "Currently installable=False — must be set to True or data moved to installable carrier"
        status: blocked_by_installability
      - module: ipai_project_seed
        purpose: "Generic project templates"
        status: ready
      - module: ipai_zoho_mail
        purpose: "Zoho SMTP mail server config"
        status: ready
      - module: ipai_bir_tax_compliance
        purpose: "BIR deadlines, tax rates, ATC codes"
        status: ready
      - module: ipai_ai_copilot
        purpose: "Copilot tools, mail channel, bot partner"
        status: ready
    blocked_modules:
      - module: ipai_finance_close_seed
        reason: "Duplicate finance stages/projects/tasks — conflicts with ipai_finance_workflow"
      - module: ipai_mailgun_smtp
        reason: "Mailgun is deprecated — replaced by ipai_zoho_mail"
    actions:
      - name: install_canonical_ipai_modules
        command: "odoo-bin --database=$DB_NAME --init=$CANONICAL_MODULES --stop-after-init"
    validation:
      - "No deprecated seed modules installed"
      - "Finance stages count == 5 (not 6 or 11)"
      - "Month-end tasks count == 20 (not 39 or 95)"
      - "Mail server: Zoho at sequence=1, no Mailgun"

ipai_finance_close_seed reason: "Duplicate finance stages/projects/tasks — conflicts with ipai_finance_workflow" - module: ipai_mailgun_smtp reason: "Mailgun is deprecated — replaced by ipai_zoho_mail" actions: - name: install_canonical_ipai_modules command: "odoo-bin --database=$DB_NAME --init=$CANONICAL_MODULES --stop-after-init" validation: - "No deprecated seed modules installed" - "Finance stages count == 5 (not 6 or 11)" - "Month-end tasks count == 20 (not 39 or 95)" - "Mail server: Zoho at sequence=1, no Mailgun"

  # ==========================================================================
  # PHASE 3: Companies, Users, Groups, Mail Config
  # ==========================================================================
  - id: phase_3_org_identity
    title: "Companies, users, groups, mail config"
    source_of_truth: scripts/odoo/seed_prod_users.py
    actions:
      - name: seed_companies_and_users
        command: "python3 scripts/odoo/seed_prod_users.py --db=$DB_NAME"
        idempotent: true
        note: "Search-or-create pattern, safe to re-run"
    outputs:
      - company: "InsightPulse AI"
        country: PH
        currency: PHP
      - company: "TBWA\\Santiago Mangada Puno"
      - users:
          - CEO (group_system, group_erp_manager)
          - DevOps (group_system)
          - Finance Manager (account.group_account_manager)
          - Project Manager (project.group_project_manager)
          - Portal User (base.group_portal)
    validation:
      - "SELECT count(*) FROM res_company WHERE name LIKE 'InsightPulse%'"
      - "SELECT count(*) FROM res_users WHERE active=true AND share=false"
    post_actions:
      - "Change default passwords (set to 'changeme' by script)"
      - "Validate mail server config matches environment"

currency: PHP - company: "TBWA\\Santiago Mangada Puno" - users: - CEO (group_system, group_erp_manager) - DevOps (group_system) - Finance Manager (account.group_account_manager) - Project Manager (project.group_project_manager) - Portal User (base.group_portal) validation: - "SELECT count(*) FROM res_company WHERE name LIKE 'InsightPulse%'" - "SELECT count(*) FROM res_users WHERE active=true AND share=false" post_actions: - "Change default passwords (set to 'changeme' by script)" - "Validate mail server config matches environment"

  # ==========================================================================
  # PHASE 4: Supabase Control Plane Seeds
  # ==========================================================================
  - id: phase_4_control_plane
    title: "Supabase and external control-plane seeds"
    source_of_truth: supabase/seeds/
    note: "Separate concern — Supabase is NOT Odoo. Seeds target Supabase PostgreSQL, not Odoo PG."
    actions:
      - name: apply_supabase_seeds
        command: "supabase db seed --db-url=$SUPABASE_DB_URL"
        scope: supabase_only
    seed_files:
      - supabase/seeds/001_hr_seed.sql
      - supabase/seeds/002_finance_seed.sql (DEPRECATED — verify before applying)
      - supabase/seeds/003_odoo_dict_seed.sql
    validation:
      - "Supabase tables populated"
      - "No Supabase seeds applied to Odoo PG"

# ========================================================================== - id: phase_4_control_plane title: "Supabase and external control-plane seeds" source_of_truth: supabase/seeds/ note: "Separate concern — Supabase is NOT Odoo. Seeds target Supabase PostgreSQL, not Odoo PG." actions: - name: apply_supabase_seeds command: "supabase db seed --db-url=$SUPABASE_DB_URL" scope: supabase_only seed_files: - supabase/seeds/001_hr_seed.sql - supabase/seeds/002_finance_seed.sql (DEPRECATED — verify before applying) - supabase/seeds/003_odoo_dict_seed.sql validation: - "Supabase tables populated" - "No Supabase seeds applied to Odoo PG"

  # ==========================================================================
  # PHASE 5: Validation + Evidence
  # ==========================================================================
  - id: phase_5_validation
    title: "Post-init validation and evidence emission"
    source_of_truth: scripts/odoo/validate_seed_state.py
    actions:
      - name: run_validation
        command: "python3 scripts/odoo/validate_seed_state.py --db=$DB_NAME --plan=ssot/migration/production_seed_plan.yaml"
    checks:
      - modules_installed_match_canonical_list
      - no_deprecated_seed_modules_installed
      - company_count_matches
      - user_count_and_groups_match
      - finance_stage_count_is_5
      - month_end_task_count_is_20
      - bir_task_single_canonical_set
      - mail_server_zoho_primary
      - no_mailgun_server
      - mail_suppression_for_non_prod
    outputs:
      - "docs/evidence/{date}/production-seed-validation.json"
    fail_behavior: "Non-zero exit on any drift or failure"

Odoo PG" # ========================================================================== # PHASE 5: Validation + Evidence # ========================================================================== - id: phase_5_validation title: "Post-init validation and evidence emission" source_of_truth: scripts/odoo/validate_seed_state.py actions: - name: run_validation command: "python3 scripts/odoo/validate_seed_state.py --db=$DB_NAME --plan=ssot/migration/production_seed_plan.yaml" checks: - modules_installed_match_canonical_list - no_deprecated_seed_modules_installed - company_count_matches - user_count_and_groups_match - finance_stage_count_is_5 - month_end_task_count_is_20 - bir_task_single_canonical_set - mail_server_zoho_primary - no_mailgun_server - mail_suppression_for_non_prod outputs: - "docs/evidence/{date}/production-seed-validation.json" fail_behavior: "Non-zero exit on any drift or failure"

# ============================================================================
# KNOWN BLOCKERS
# ============================================================================
blockers:
  - id: finance_workflow_installability
    description: "ipai_finance_workflow is marked installable=False but is the canonical finance seed source"
    resolution: "Either set installable=True or move canonical seed XML to a dedicated installable module"
    priority: high

  - id: bir_task_dedup
    description: "tasks_bir.xml (27 tasks) duplicates 07_tasks_bir_tax.xml (33 tasks) within same module"
    resolution: "Delete tasks_bir.xml per seed_canonical_map.yaml"
    priority: medium

  - id: supabase_mirror_cleanup
    description: "ops-platform/supabase/seeds/ is an exact copy of supabase/seeds/"
    resolution: "Delete mirror directories per seed_canonical_map.yaml"
    priority: low

text:
schema_version: "1.0"
updated_at: "2026-03-15"

n8n_target:
  url: https://n8n.insightpulseai.com
  api_base: /api/v1
  port: 5678

import_policy:
  default_state: disabled  # All imported disabled; enable per-wave
  url_rewrite:
    old: https://spdtwktxdalcfigzeqrz.supabase.co
    new: https://supabase.insightpulseai.com
  credential_rewrite:
    strategy: manual  # Credentials must be re-created on target n8n

enablement_waves:
  - wave: 1
    name: "Health & Control"
    description: "Health checks, monitoring, heartbeat workflows"
    enable_after: "All Phase 5 consumer smoke tests pass"
    workflows:
      # Health checks
      - automations/n8n/workflows/01-health-check.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/control-plane/health-check-scheduler.json
      # Control plane backup
      - automations/n8n/workflows/control-plane/backup-scheduler.json
      # MCP connection test
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/test/00_mcp_connection_test.json

  - wave: 2
    name: "Read-Only Integrations"
    description: "Workflows that only read from Supabase (dashboards, reports, exports, queries)"
    enable_after: "Wave 1 healthy for 24h"
    workflows:
      # MCP read-only workflows
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/01_mcp_odoo_query.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/02_mcp_supabase_ops.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/03_mcp_finance_summary.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/04_mcp_infra_health.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/05_mcp_workflow_monitor.json
      - automations/n8n/workflows/claude-ai-mcp/read-only/06_mcp_sync_trigger.json
      # Reporting / cache refresh

# =============================================================================
# IPAI Addons Manifest — ssot/odoo/addons.manifest.yaml
# =============================================================================
# SSOT for IPAI custom modules with classification, identity awareness,
# and publish surface declarations.
#
# Canonical OCA manifest: config/addons.manifest.yaml
# This file focuses on ipai_* modules and their runtime properties.
#
# Fields:
#   name:             Module technical name
#   class:            core | oca | bridge | local | l10n | experimental
#   identity_aware:   true if module consumes or enforces identity (Entra/RBAC)
#   publish_surface:  list of surfaces where module exposes functionality
#                     odoo = Odoo web client
#                     copilot = Copilot / Pulser
#                     m365 = Microsoft 365 plugin
#                     api = REST / JSON-RPC API
#   description:      One-line purpose
#   deprecated:       true if module is deprecated (optional, default false)
#   lifecycle:        active | beta | deprecated | planned
#   owner:            team/person responsible
#   identity_mode:    entra_oidc | managed_identity | api_key | none
#   interactive_user_required: true if module requires human login
#   workload_identity_required: true if module requires service auth
#   privilege_level:  standard | elevated | admin
#   audit_required:   true if actions must be logged to audit trail
#   copilot_surface:  true if module exposes copilot capabilities
#   capability_type:  informational | navigational | transactional (copilot modules)
#   launcher_modes:   floating | embedded | host (copilot modules)
#   m365_copilot_interop:
#     enabled:        true if exposed via M365 Copilot
#     render_mode:    host_safe | host_degraded | odoo_native_only
#     fallback_target: URL or surface for fallback
#   doc_surface:      docs path for this module
#   runtime_root:     addon directory path
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-28
# =============================================================================

exposes copilot capabilities # capability_type: informational | navigational | transactional (copilot modules) # launcher_modes: floating | embedded | host (copilot modules) # m365_copilot_interop: # enabled: true if exposed via M365 Copilot # render_mode: host_safe | host_degraded | odoo_native_only # fallback_target: URL or surface for fallback # doc_surface: docs path for this module # runtime_root: addon directory path # # Last updated: 2026-03-28 # =============================================================================

# =============================================================================
# Odoo Asset Bundle Ownership Map
# =============================================================================
# Machine-readable registry of which ipai_* modules contribute to which
# Odoo asset bundles, and what surface type they target.
#
# Surface types: backend, frontend, website, editor, pos, report
# Source types: core (Odoo CE), oca (OCA), ipai (custom bridge/meta)
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-18
# =============================================================================

bundle_contributions:
  - module: ipai_odoo_copilot
    source_type: ipai
    installable: true
    bundles:
      - key: web.assets_backend
        surface: backend
        files:
          - ipai_odoo_copilot/static/src/js/copilot_systray.js
          - ipai_odoo_copilot/static/src/xml/copilot_systray.xml
          - ipai_odoo_copilot/static/src/scss/copilot_systray.scss

  - module: ipai_ai_copilot
    source_type: ipai
    installable: false  # DEPRECATED 2026-03-13
    bundles:
      - key: web.assets_backend
        surface: backend
        files:
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/js/copilot_service.js
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/js/copilot_sidebar.js
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/js/copilot_palette.js
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/xml/copilot_sidebar.xml
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/xml/copilot_palette.xml
          - ipai_ai_copilot/static/src/css/copilot.css

  - module: ipai_finance_ppm
    source_type: ipai
    installable: true
    bundles: []
    # ipai_finance_ppm has static/src/js and static/src/html but
    # does not declare assets in __manifest__.py — served via reports/templates

  - module: cts_theme_rozz
    source_type: ipai
    installable: true
    bundles: []
    # Theme module with static/src/js — check manifest for asset declarations

  - module: ipai_theme_copilot
    source_type: ipai
    installable: true
    bundles: []
    # Theme SCSS module — check manifest for asset declarations

ipai_finance_ppm source_type: ipai installable: true bundles: [] # ipai_finance_ppm has static/src/js and static/src/html but # does not declare assets in __manifest__.py — served via reports/templates - module: cts_theme_rozz source_type: ipai installable: true bundles: [] # Theme module with static/src/js — check manifest for asset declarations - module: ipai_theme_copilot source_type: ipai installable: true bundles: [] # Theme SCSS module — check manifest for asset declarations

# =============================================================================
# Validation rules
# =============================================================================
# 1. Every file listed here must exist on disk
# 2. Every module listed here must have a matching __manifest__.py
# 3. installable field must match __manifest__.py installable value
# 4. No file should appear in more than one module's bundle contributions
# =============================================================================

provider: azure_openai
mode: responses
base_url_env: AZURE_OPENAI_BASE_URL
api_key_env: AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY
deployment_env: AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT
api_mode_env: AZURE_OPENAI_API_MODE
timeout_seconds: 60
requires_auth: true
requires_persona_context: true
ir_config_parameter_keys:
  base_url: ipai_ask_ai_azure.base_url
  api_key: ipai_ask_ai_azure.api_key
  deployment: ipai_ask_ai_azure.model
  api_mode: ipai_ask_ai_azure.api_mode
notes:
  - "Azure deployment name is required; do not assume base model family name is sufficient."
  - "No live secrets may be committed in repo files."
  - "Env vars are seeded into ir.config_parameter at module init; DB values take precedence."
  - "Supported API modes: responses (default), chat_completions."

version: 1
namespace: azure.document_intelligence
source:
  kind: vendor_docs_contract
  docs:
    - azure_document_intelligence_v4_0
    - azure_form_recognizer_migration
description: >
  Canonical entity contract for Azure Document Intelligence (formerly Form
  Recognizer) prebuilt model outputs. Defines the business-schema for extracted
  document fields used by copilots, expense automation, invoice processing,
  and compliance workflows. Not a claim of exact API response parity.

enums:
  analysis_status:
    values: [not_started, running, succeeded, failed]
  model_type:
    values: [prebuilt, custom_template, custom_neural, composed, classification]
  document_type:
    values: [invoice, receipt, id_document, tax_w2, tax_1040, tax_1098, tax_1099, bank_statement, check, pay_stub, credit_card, contract, health_insurance_card, marriage_certificate, layout, read, general_document]
  confidence_level:
    values: [high, medium, low]
  content_format:
    values: [json, markdown, pdf]

entities:
  analysis_result:
    label: Analysis Result
    description: Top-level result from a Document Intelligence analyze operation.
    primary_key: operation_id
    fields:
      - {name: operation_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: model_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: status, type: enum, enum_ref: analysis_status, required: true}
      - {name: created_at, type: datetime, required: true}
      - {name: completed_at, type: datetime, required: false}
      - {name: api_version, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: content_format, type: enum, enum_ref: content_format, required: false}
      - {name: page_count, type: integer, required: false}
      - {name: document_count, type: integer, required: false}

domain-specific document extraction. primary_key: model_id fields: - {name: model_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: model_type, type: enum, enum_ref: model_type, required: true} - {name: description, type: string, required: false} - {name: created_at, type: datetime, required: true} - {name: api_version, type: string, required: true} - {name: doc_types, type: "array[string]", required: false} - {name: training_dataset_path, type: string, required: false} - {name: accuracy_score, type: number, required: false}

relationships:
  - from: analysis_result
    to: extracted_document
    type: one_to_many
    via: operation_id
  - from: extracted_document
    to: extracted_field
    type: one_to_many
    via: document_id
  - from: analysis_result
    to: extracted_table
    type: one_to_many
    via: operation_id
  - from: extracted_table
    to: table_cell
    type: one_to_many
    via: table_id
  - from: extracted_document
    to: invoice_extract
    type: one_to_one
    via: document_id
  - from: extracted_document
    to: receipt_extract
    type: one_to_one
    via: document_id
  - from: receipt_extract
    to: expense_receipt_mapping
    type: one_to_one
    via: receipt_document_id

# =============================================================================
# Odoo 19 Integration Adoption Decision Table — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Defines the adoption priority for each integration lane:
#   1. Native Odoo config first
#   2. OCA module second
#   3. ipai_* bridge only for external Azure/Foundry services
#
# Ref: https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/applications/general/integrations.html
# Last reviewed: 2026-03-27
# =============================================================================

schema_version: 1
kind: integration_adoption_table
last_reviewed: "2026-03-27"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# NATIVE FIRST — Odoo 19 documents these as built-in integration surfaces.
# Prefer configuration over custom code.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

native_first:
  payments:
    providers: [stripe, paypal, adyen, authorize_net, razorpay, xendit]
    notes: "Built-in payment providers. Config only — no custom module needed."

  bank_sync:
    notes: "Native bank synchronization. Config per institution."

  edi:
    notes: "Electronic invoicing / EDI. Native Odoo 19 compliance surface."

  document_digitization:
    notes: "Native Odoo document digitization. For Azure Doc Intelligence, use external bridge."

  commerce_connectors:
    providers: [amazon, shopee, gelato, google_merchant_center]
    notes: "Built-in marketplace connectors. Config + activation."

  website:
    surfaces: [domain_names, google_search_console, address_autocomplete, cdn, spam_protection]
    notes: "Native website integration lanes."

  pos_terminals:
    notes: "POS payment terminal integrations. Hardware config."

  philippines_localization:
    notes: "PH-specific tax/compliance localization in Odoo 19 finance tree."

Config + activation." website: surfaces: [domain_names, google_search_console, address_autocomplete, cdn, spam_protection] notes: "Native website integration lanes." pos_terminals: notes: "POS payment terminal integrations. Hardware config." philippines_localization: notes: "PH-specific tax/compliance localization in Odoo 19 finance tree."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OCA FIRST — Use OCA when native is insufficient but the pattern exists.
# Check OCA before creating ipai_*.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

oca_first:
  generic_connector_framework:
    repo: OCA/connector
    notes: "Jobs queue, async tasks, channels, decoupled connector components."

  importer_interfaces:
    repo: OCA/connector-interfaces
    notes: "Import-session style connector flows (ODBC, CSV, etc.)."

  electronic_payment_extensions:
    repo: OCA/bank-payment
    notes: "Electronic payment addons beyond native Odoo payment providers."

  odoo_to_odoo:
    repo: OCA/connector-odoo2odoo
    notes: "Intercompany process patterns. Use if multi-company sync needed."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# EXTERNAL BRIDGE FIRST — Azure/Foundry services outside Odoo's native lane.
# These stay as thin ipai_* bridges, not feature-parity reimplementations.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

external_bridge_first:
  entra_oidc:
    module: ipai_auth_oidc
    notes: "Entra ID authentication. Native auth_oauth + OCA auth_oidc."

  azure_document_intelligence:
    module: ipai_enterprise_bridge
    notes: "Azure Doc Intelligence OCR. Thin bridge to Foundry endpoint."

Use if multi-company sync needed." # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # EXTERNAL BRIDGE FIRST — Azure/Foundry services outside Odoo's native lane. # These stay as thin ipai_* bridges, not feature-parity reimplementations. # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── external_bridge_first: entra_oidc: module: ipai_auth_oidc notes: "Entra ID authentication. Native auth_oauth + OCA auth_oidc." azure_document_intelligence: module: ipai_enterprise_bridge notes: "Azure Doc Intelligence OCR. Thin bridge to Foundry endpoint."

  foundry_agents:
    module: ipai_odoo_copilot
    notes: "Foundry agent runtime / Pulser orchestration."

  ai_search:
    module: ipai_odoo_copilot
    notes: "Azure AI Search for RAG grounding. Part of copilot tool executor."

  databricks_fabric_analytics:
    repo: data-intelligence
    notes: "Sits entirely outside Odoo. No Odoo module — data flows via ETL."

  ops_control_plane:
    repo: platform
    notes: "Platform observability and control. Not an Odoo module."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ADOPTION RULE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

adoption_rule:
  - "Check Odoo 19 native integrations first"
  - "If native lane exists, prefer configuration over custom code"
  - "If native is insufficient, check OCA before creating ipai_*"
  - "Reserve ipai_* for thin bridges to external Azure/Foundry services"
  - "Never build feature-parity reimplementations inside ipai_*"

schema_version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-17"
status: canonical

canonical_base_modules:
  - account
  - purchase
  - hr_expense
  - documents
  - mail
  - contacts

canonical_custom_modules:
  - id: ipai_enterprise_bridge
    role: shared config, security groups, feature flags, audit mixins
    depends: [base, mail]
    type: bridge

  - id: ipai_ai_core
    role: agent session records, tool registry, correlation IDs, AI contract layer
    depends: [ipai_enterprise_bridge, mail]
    type: core_bridge

  - id: ipai_foundry_bridge
    role: Odoo to Foundry Agent Service runtime integration
    depends: [ipai_ai_core, ipai_enterprise_bridge]
    type: external_bridge

  - id: ipai_document_intelligence_bridge
    role: Odoo to Azure Document Intelligence OCR/extraction
    depends: [documents, ipai_enterprise_bridge, ipai_ai_core]
    type: external_bridge

  - id: ipai_odoo_copilot
    role: embedded copilot UI and action surface inside Odoo
    depends: [ipai_enterprise_bridge, ipai_ai_core, mail]
    type: ux_bridge

  - id: ipai_ap_expense_capture
    role: invoice/expense capture, matching, dedupe, routing, exceptions
    depends: [account, purchase, hr_expense, documents, ipai_document_intelligence_bridge, ipai_ai_core]
    type: workflow

install_order:
  - ipai_enterprise_bridge
  - ipai_ai_core
  - ipai_foundry_bridge
  - ipai_document_intelligence_bridge
  - ipai_odoo_copilot
  - ipai_ap_expense_capture

excluded_or_deferred:
  - id: ipai_agent
    reason: fold into ipai_ai_core or ipai_foundry_bridge
  - id: ipai_theme_copilot
    reason: fold UI into ipai_odoo_copilot
  - id: ipai_hr_expense_liquidation
    reason: refactor into ipai_ap_expense_capture or keep as later optional
  - id: ipai_helpdesk
    reason: unrelated to AP/expense copilot target
  - id: ipai_mail_compat
    reason: Zoho mail config is runtime-only (Azure Key Vault), not module
  - id: ipai_foundation
    reason: absorbed by ipai_enterprise_bridge

- ipai_odoo_copilot - ipai_ap_expense_capture excluded_or_deferred: - id: ipai_agent reason: fold into ipai_ai_core or ipai_foundry_bridge - id: ipai_theme_copilot reason: fold UI into ipai_odoo_copilot - id: ipai_hr_expense_liquidation reason: refactor into ipai_ap_expense_capture or keep as later optional - id: ipai_helpdesk reason: unrelated to AP/expense copilot target - id: ipai_mail_compat reason: Zoho mail config is runtime-only (Azure Key Vault), not module - id: ipai_foundation reason: absorbed by ipai_enterprise_bridge

safe_tools:

  - create_vendor_bill_draft
  - create_expense_draft

  - route_for_approval
  - explain_capture_exception

forbidden_tools:
  - post_vendor_bill
  - approve_payment
  - delete_record
  - modify_accounting_period

rules:
  - "Agent runtime/orchestration/memory/evals → Foundry, not Odoo"
  - "OCR extraction → Document Intelligence, not Odoo"
  - "Transactional drafts/approvals/audit → Odoo"
  - "Financial posting/payment tools are forbidden for agents in v1"
  - "No standalone memory/eval/prompt modules in Odoo"

version: 2
namespace: odoo.copilot.benchmark
last_reviewed: "2026-03-15"
description: >
  Odoo Copilot Benchmark Scorecard V2. Scores Odoo Copilot against five
  benchmark anchors: Odoo AI, SAP Joule, SAP AFC, Microsoft Foundry,
  Microsoft Copilot for Finance, and Databricks Financial Services.

benchmark_anchors:
  - key: odoo_ai
    label: Odoo AI / Odoo AI Agents
    role: Native Odoo embedding, grounded knowledge, in-product actionability
  - key: sap_joule
    label: SAP Joule
    role: Enterprise assistant UX, informational/navigational/transactional support, workplace integration
  - key: sap_afc
    label: SAP Advanced Financial Closing
    role: Close orchestration, task planning, dependency handling, status monitoring
  - key: microsoft_foundry
    label: Microsoft Foundry
    role: Agent platform maturity, hosted orchestration, control plane, evaluation, tracing, monitoring, knowledge connection
  - key: microsoft_copilot_finance
    label: Microsoft Copilot for Finance / agent platform
    role: Finance-assistant UX, business-data-connected agents, role-based workflow support
  - key: databricks_fsi
    label: Databricks Financial Services
    role: Analytics/intelligence ceiling for fraud, risk, treasury, compliance, and recommendations

scoring_scale:
  min: 0
  max: 5
  labels:
    0: absent
    1: concept only / demo quality
    2: partial and fragile
    3: usable in controlled production scope
    4: strong and competitive
    5: benchmark-class for intended scope

categories:
  - key: odoo_native_actions
    label: Odoo-native actions
    weight: 18
    question: Can the copilot safely perform meaningful Odoo actions, not just answer questions?
    checks:
      - creates / updates records correctly
      - respects ACLs and business rules
      - supports approval-aware actions
      - handles failed actions cleanly
      - gives clear confirmation when appropriate
    five_looks_like: >
      Reliable Odoo-native execution across real workflows, contextual action
      suggestions inside forms/lists/workflow screens, strong confirmation/error/retry handling.

  - key: knowledge_grounding
    label: Knowledge grounding / RAG
    weight: 14
    question: Are answers grounded in Odoo data plus trusted documents and knowledge sources?
    checks:
      - record-grounded answers
      - doc / SOP / policy grounding
      - relevance and ranking quality
      - low hallucination rate
      - evidence visibility
    five_looks_like: >
      Grounded answers across records + documents + linked knowledge, consistent
      retrieval quality, clear provenance path for important outputs.

knowledge_grounding label: Knowledge grounding / RAG weight: 14 question: Are answers grounded in Odoo data plus trusted documents and knowledge sources? checks: - record-grounded answers - doc / SOP / policy grounding - relevance and ranking quality - low hallucination rate - evidence visibility five_looks_like: > Grounded answers across records + documents + linked knowledge, consistent retrieval quality, clear provenance path for important outputs.

  - key: finance_workflow_depth
    label: Finance workflow depth
    weight: 18
    question: Does it materially help finance teams complete finance work?
    checks:
      - reconciliation support
      - close preparation support
      - exception handling
      - AP / AR / accounting task support
      - controller / accounting role usefulness
      - tax / compliance-adjacent assistance
    five_looks_like: >
      Materially reduces finance ops workload, supports close/review/exception
      handling/follow-through, role-aware finance assistance.

  - key: agent_configurability
    label: Agent configurability
    weight: 10
    question: Can admins or power users define scoped agents without code-heavy rework?
    checks:
      - prompt/system config
      - tool permissions
      - source / KB selection
      - reusable agent profiles
      - environment-scoped settings
    five_looks_like: >
      Admin-friendly agent creation, deterministic scoping of tools and knowledge,
      reusable agents per role or process.

  - key: multi_step_orchestration
    label: Multi-step orchestration
    weight: 8
    question: Can it chain steps across tools and workflows instead of only doing single-turn chat?
    checks:
      - multi-step task plans
      - mid-flow approvals
      - retries / recovery
      - state persistence
      - resumable workflows
    five_looks_like: >
      Executes bounded workflows end-to-end, pauses for approval correctly,
      resumes with state and auditability.

and knowledge, reusable agents per role or process. - key: multi_step_orchestration label: Multi-step orchestration weight: 8 question: Can it chain steps across tools and workflows instead of only doing single-turn chat? checks: - multi-step task plans - mid-flow approvals - retries / recovery - state persistence - resumable workflows five_looks_like: > Executes bounded workflows end-to-end, pauses for approval correctly, resumes with state and auditability.

  - key: embedded_ux_quality
    label: Embedded UX quality
    weight: 8
    question: Does it feel native inside Odoo, or like an external assistant pasted into the product?
    checks:
      - context-sensitive entry points
      - side panel / chat / inline action UX
      - screen-aware prompting
      - low-friction invocation
      - density and readability in operational screens
    five_looks_like: >
      Natural Odoo embedding, minimal mode switching, strong contextual
      ergonomics in day-to-day work.

  - key: governance_safety_auditability
    label: Governance / safety / auditability
    weight: 9
    question: Is it safe enough for enterprise operational use?
    checks:
      - role-aware access
      - approval gates
      - audit logs
      - action traceability
      - clear fallback behavior
      - observability of tool use and outcomes
    five_looks_like: >
      Enterprise-safe controls, auditable action trail, strong permission
      boundaries, safe handling of ambiguous or unsafe actions.

in day-to-day work. - key: governance_safety_auditability label: Governance / safety / auditability weight: 9 question: Is it safe enough for enterprise operational use? checks: - role-aware access - approval gates - audit logs - action traceability - clear fallback behavior - observability of tool use and outcomes five_looks_like: > Enterprise-safe controls, auditable action trail, strong permission boundaries, safe handling of ambiguous or unsafe actions.

  - key: agent_platform_control_plane
    label: Agent platform / control plane maturity
    weight: 10
    question: Is Odoo Copilot just an assistant, or a manageable enterprise agent platform?
    benchmark_target: Microsoft Foundry
    checks:
      - hosted / durable agent runtime
      - tool registry and governance
      - workflow evaluation
      - tracing / observability
      - monitoring / alerts
      - knowledge-base connection
      - environment isolation
      - automation / SDK friendliness
    five_looks_like: >
      Agents are durable, governable, and observable. Tools/knowledge/runtime are
      centrally managed. Workflows are evaluable and monitorable. Platform is
      production-safe and automatable.

  - key: analytics_recommendations
    label: Analytics / recommendations
    weight: 5
    question: Does it provide useful proactive insight, not just reactive answers?
    checks:
      - anomaly surfacing
      - prioritization
      - recommendation quality
      - trend awareness
      - actionability of insights
    five_looks_like: >
      Useful, grounded recommendations with clear business relevance, sensible
      prioritization by impact and confidence.

are centrally managed. Workflows are evaluable and monitorable. Platform is production-safe and automatable. - key: analytics_recommendations label: Analytics / recommendations weight: 5 question: Does it provide useful proactive insight, not just reactive answers? checks: - anomaly surfacing - prioritization - recommendation quality - trend awareness - actionability of insights five_looks_like: > Useful, grounded recommendations with clear business relevance, sensible prioritization by impact and confidence.

competitive_snapshot:
  - benchmark: Odoo AI / Agents
    main_role: Native ERP copilot
    lesson: Must feel natively actionable inside Odoo
  - benchmark: SAP Joule
    main_role: Enterprise assistant UX
    lesson: Must support informational + navigational + transactional assistance cleanly
  - benchmark: SAP Advanced Financial Closing
    main_role: Finance close depth
    lesson: Must improve close-task orchestration maturity
  - benchmark: Microsoft Foundry
    main_role: Agent platform / control plane
    lesson: Must be governable, observable, evaluable, and KB-connected
  - benchmark: Microsoft Copilot for Finance / agent platform
    main_role: Finance assistant UX + role workflows
    lesson: Must support finance-role-specific assistance, not generic chat
  - benchmark: Databricks Financial Services
    main_role: Intelligence layer
    lesson: Should integrate to deeper analytics rather than pretend to replace them early

target_thresholds:
  - { max_score: 2.4, label: Prototype only }
  - { min_score: 2.5, max_score: 3.4, label: Narrow pilot }
  - { min_score: 3.5, max_score: 3.9, label: Acceptable v1 production scope }
  - { min_score: 4.0, max_score: 4.2, label: Strong competitive product }
  - { min_score: 4.3, label: Enterprise-ready benchmark tier }

lesson: Should integrate to deeper analytics rather than pretend to replace them early target_thresholds: - { max_score: 2.4, label: Prototype only } - { min_score: 2.5, max_score: 3.4, label: Narrow pilot } - { min_score: 3.5, max_score: 3.9, label: Acceptable v1 production scope } - { min_score: 4.0, max_score: 4.2, label: Strong competitive product } - { min_score: 4.3, label: Enterprise-ready benchmark tier }

red_flags:
  - Hallucinates business data or claims actions it did not perform
  - Can bypass approval-sensitive workflows
  - Breaks Odoo ACL or record-rule expectations
  - Weak or absent audit trail
  - Finance assistance is shallow or generic
  - RAG is document-only and not Odoo-contextual
  - Feels like generic chat, not workflow-native assistance
  - Cannot handle multi-step tasks
  - No role-specific behavior
  - No usable evaluation / tracing / monitoring model
  - Knowledge integration is brittle or unsafe
  - Recommendations lack business grounding

practical_target:
  beat: Odoo native AI on Odoo-specific workflow integration
  approach:
    - SAP on finance-process depth
    - Microsoft 365 Copilot on assistant UX
    - Microsoft Foundry on agent platform maturity
  integrate_not_replace: Databricks for deeper intelligence

benchmark_statement: >
  Odoo Copilot should be benchmarked against Microsoft Foundry for
  agent-platform maturity, not for ERP-native workflow depth. Foundry defines
  the bar for hosted agents, control-plane governance, evaluations, monitoring,
  knowledge connection, and SDK-first operationalization of enterprise AI apps
  and agents.

version: 1
namespace: odoo.copilot.finance_tools
description: >
  Canonical finance/expense tool contract for Odoo Copilot. These tools are
  business-safe surfaces, not direct database access primitives. Intended for
  approval-aware copilots, agent runtimes, and workflow orchestration.

defaults:
  approval_mode: policy_driven
  audit_required: true
  idempotency_required: true

tool_categories:
  - read
  - workflow
  - recommendation
  - action

tools:
  - key: read_invoice
    category: read
    label: Read invoice
    description: Fetch a customer invoice header and key financial state.
    inputs:
      - {name: invoice_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: invoice_number, type: string, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: invoice
      fields:
        - id
        - invoice_number
        - partner_id
        - invoice_date
        - due_date
        - amount_total
        - residual_amount
        - state
        - payment_state
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

  - key: list_overdue_invoices
    category: read
    label: List overdue invoices
    description: Return unpaid or partially paid invoices past due date.
    inputs:
      - {name: company_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: partner_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: as_of_date, type: date, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: invoice
      fields:
        - id
        - invoice_number
        - partner_id
        - due_date
        - amount_total
        - residual_amount
        - payment_state
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

- key: list_overdue_invoices category: read label: List overdue invoices description: Return unpaid or partially paid invoices past due date. inputs: - {name: company_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: partner_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: as_of_date, type: date, required: false} outputs: entity: invoice fields: - id - invoice_number - partner_id - due_date - amount_total - residual_amount - payment_state access: minimum_scope: finance.read approval_required: false

  - key: read_vendor_bill
    category: read
    label: Read vendor bill
    description: Fetch a vendor bill and current payable/payment status.
    inputs:
      - {name: bill_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: bill_number, type: string, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: vendor_bill
      fields:
        - id
        - bill_number
        - vendor_id
        - bill_date
        - due_date
        - amount_total
        - residual_amount
        - state
        - payment_state
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

  - key: list_unreconciled_bank_transactions
    category: read
    label: List unreconciled bank transactions
    description: Fetch bank/cash lines not yet reconciled.
    inputs:
      - {name: journal_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: from_date, type: date, required: false}
      - {name: to_date, type: date, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: bank_transaction
      fields:
        - id
        - transaction_date
        - label
        - partner_id
        - amount
        - is_reconciled
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

minimum_scope: finance.read approval_required: false - key: list_unreconciled_bank_transactions category: read label: List unreconciled bank transactions description: Fetch bank/cash lines not yet reconciled. inputs: - {name: journal_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: from_date, type: date, required: false} - {name: to_date, type: date, required: false} outputs: entity: bank_transaction fields: - id - transaction_date - label - partner_id - amount - is_reconciled access: minimum_scope: finance.read approval_required: false

  - key: suggest_reconciliation_matches
    category: recommendation
    label: Suggest reconciliation matches
    description: Suggest likely matches between bank transactions and accounting documents.
    inputs:
      - {name: bank_transaction_id, type: string, required: true}
    outputs:
      entity: recommendation
      fields:
        - candidate_document_id
        - candidate_document_type
        - confidence_score
        - explanation
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

{name: total, type: number, required: true} - {name: quantity, type: number, required: false} - {name: unit_price, type: number, required: false} - {name: included_taxes_amount, type: number, required: false} - {name: analytic_account_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: receipt_attachment_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: notes, type: string, required: false} outputs: entity: expense fields: [id, status, total, employee_id, category_id] access: minimum_scope: expenses.write approval_required: false audit_event: expense_created

  - key: submit_expense_report
    category: workflow
    label: Submit expense report
    description: Create or submit an expense report for approval.
    inputs:
      - {name: report_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: summary, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: employee_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: expense_line_ids, type: "array[string]", required: true}
    outputs:
      entity: expense_report
      fields: [id, summary, state, total_amount, submitted_at]
    access:
      minimum_scope: expenses.submit
      approval_required: false
      audit_event: expense_report_submitted

  - key: approve_expense_report
    category: workflow
    label: Approve expense report
    description: Approve a submitted expense report.
    inputs:
      - {name: report_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: approver_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: approval_note, type: string, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: expense_report
      fields: [id, state, approved_at]
    access:
      minimum_scope: expenses.approve
      approval_required: true
      audit_event: expense_report_approved

fields: [id, summary, state, total_amount, submitted_at] access: minimum_scope: expenses.submit approval_required: false audit_event: expense_report_submitted - key: approve_expense_report category: workflow label: Approve expense report description: Approve a submitted expense report. inputs: - {name: report_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: approver_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: approval_note, type: string, required: false} outputs: entity: expense_report fields: [id, state, approved_at] access: minimum_scope: expenses.approve approval_required: true audit_event: expense_report_approved

  - key: post_expense_report
    category: workflow
    label: Post expense report
    description: Post an approved expense report into accounting.
    inputs:
      - {name: report_id, type: string, required: true}
    outputs:
      entity: expense_report
      fields: [id, state, posted_at]
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.post
      approval_required: true
      audit_event: expense_report_posted

  - key: reimburse_expense_report
    category: action
    label: Reimburse expense report
    description: Reimburse a posted expense report to the employee.
    inputs:
      - {name: report_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: reimbursement_method, type: enum, required: true, enum_values: [direct, payslip, vendor_bill, other]}
      - {name: journal_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: payment_date, type: date, required: false}
    outputs:
      entity: expense_reimbursement
      fields: [id, expense_report_id, amount, state, payment_date]
    access:
      minimum_scope: expenses.reimburse
      approval_required: true
      audit_event: expense_report_reimbursed

action label: Reimburse expense report description: Reimburse a posted expense report to the employee. inputs: - {name: report_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: reimbursement_method, type: enum, required: true, enum_values: [direct, payslip, vendor_bill, other]} - {name: journal_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: payment_date, type: date, required: false} outputs: entity: expense_reimbursement fields: [id, expense_report_id, amount, state, payment_date] access: minimum_scope: expenses.reimburse approval_required: true audit_event: expense_report_reimbursed

  - key: propose_finance_close_summary
    category: recommendation
    label: Propose finance close summary
    description: Summarize open items, overdue items, unreconciled lines, and expense backlog for period close.
    inputs:
      - {name: company_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: period_start, type: date, required: true}
      - {name: period_end, type: date, required: true}
    outputs:
      entity: close_summary
      fields:
        - open_customer_invoices
        - open_vendor_bills
        - unreconciled_bank_transactions
        - unposted_expense_reports
        - reimbursement_backlog
        - narrative_summary
    access:
      minimum_scope: finance.read
      approval_required: false

guardrails:
  prohibitions:
    - no direct destructive posting reversal without explicit policy allowance
    - no reimbursement approval bypass
    - no write operation without audit event emission
    - no cross-company mutation without explicit company scope
  required_runtime_checks:
    - acl_check
    - company_scope_check
    - state_transition_check
    - idempotency_key_check
    - audit_log_write

version: 1
namespace: odoo.expenses
source:
  kind: user_docs_contract
  docs:
    - odoo_19_finance_expenses
description: >
  Canonical business-entity contract for Odoo 19 expense workflows. This is a
  business/schema contract for integrations, analytics, copilots, and approval
  orchestration. It is not a claim of exact ORM/database parity.

enums:
  expense_status:
    values: [draft, submitted, approved, posted, reimbursed, refused, cancelled]
  paid_by:
    values: [employee, company]
  reimbursement_method:
    values: [direct, payslip, vendor_bill, other]

entities:
  expense_category:
    label: Expense Category
    description: Policy/config object for expense entry behavior and accounting treatment.
    primary_key: id
    fields:
      - {name: id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: name, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: account_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: company_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: unit_of_measure, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: requires_receipt, type: boolean, required: false, default: false}
      - {name: is_reinvoiceable, type: boolean, required: false, default: false}
      - {name: is_active, type: boolean, required: true, default: true}

version: 1
namespace: odoo.finance
source:

description: >
  Canonical business-entity contract for Odoo 19 finance/accounting user-facing
  objects. This is a business/schema contract for integrations, analytics,
  copilots, and workflow modeling. It is not a claim of exact ORM/database parity.

enums:
  document_state:
    values: [draft, submitted, approved, posted, paid, cancelled]
  payment_state:
    values: [not_paid, partial, in_payment, paid, reversed, legacy]
  paid_by:
    values: [employee, company]
  move_type:
    values: [out_invoice, out_refund, in_invoice, in_refund, entry]
  maturity:
    values: [missing, scaffolded, partial, operational, hardened]

entities:
  account:
    label: Account
    description: General ledger account from chart of accounts.
    primary_key: id
    fields:
      - name: id
        type: string
        required: true
      - name: code
        type: string
        required: true
      - name: name
        type: string
        required: true
      - name: account_type
        type: string
        required: true
      - name: company_id
        type: string
        required: true
      - name: currency_id
        type: string
        required: false
      - name: is_active
        type: boolean
        required: true
        default: true

  journal:
    label: Journal
    description: Accounting journal used for invoices, bills, payments, and entries.
    primary_key: id
    fields:
      - {name: id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: name, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: code, type: string, required: true}

      - {name: company_id, type: string, required: true}
      - {name: currency_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: default_account_id, type: string, required: false}
      - {name: status, type: string, required: false}

{name: journal_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: payment_date, type: date, required: true} - {name: payment_amount, type: number, required: true} - {name: payment_currency_id, type: string, required: true} - {name: payment_method, type: string, required: false} - {name: memo, type: string, required: false} - {name: partner_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: employee_id, type: string, required: false} - {name: state, type: enum, enum_ref: document_state, required: false}

  bank_transaction:

version: 1
repo: odoo
generated_from: manual_policy
policy:
  doctrine:
    - "Odoo 19 CE is the canonical runtime baseline."
    - "OCA-first for EE-parity and business capability."
    - "ipai_* is reserved for integration bridges, approved meta-bundles, and bounded vertical/feature packaging."
    - "Only canonical packages define the default install surface."
    - "Optional packages must be explicitly selected by domain need."
    - "Packages not classified here must not be considered part of the canonical platform surface."
  install_order:
    - ipai_enterprise_bridge
    - ipai_scout_bundle
    - ipai_ces_bundle

canonical:
  bridge:
    - name: ipai_enterprise_bridge
      role: base_bridge
      description: >
        Thin cross-cutting bridge layer for shared config, approvals,
        AI/infra glue, shared mixins, and security groups.
      required: true
      default_install: true
      allowed_scope:
        - config
        - approvals
        - ai_integration_glue
        - infra_integration_glue
        - shared_mixins
        - security_groups
      forbidden_scope:
        - ee_parity_business_logic
        - standalone_vertical_business_flows

  bundles:
    - name: ipai_scout_bundle
      role: vertical_bundle
      description: >
        Retail / operations meta-bundle. Depends on approved CE/OCA/business modules
        for sales, purchase, stock, POS, and accounting flows.
      required: false
      default_install: true
      depends_on:
        - ipai_enterprise_bridge
      domain: retail_ops

    - name: ipai_ces_bundle
      role: vertical_bundle
      description: >
        Creative services meta-bundle. Depends on approved CE/OCA/business modules
        for project, HR, timesheet, mail, and portal flows.
      required: false
      default_install: true
      depends_on:
        - ipai_enterprise_bridge
      domain: creative_ops

Depends on approved CE/OCA/business modules for project, HR, timesheet, mail, and portal flows. required: false default_install: true depends_on: - ipai_enterprise_bridge domain: creative_ops

schema_version: "1.0"
namespace: odoo.runtime_contract
last_reviewed: "2026-03-28"
description: >
  Machine-readable runtime contract for the Odoo CE 19 container. Defines
  source roots, data directories, log paths, addons_path resolution, identity
  configuration, and Foundry auth. Consumable by CI validators and agents.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source roots — directories that contain Odoo code or addons (repo-relative)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
source_roots:
  - addons/ipai
  - addons/oca

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Addon paths — top-level ordered list (canonical, used by tests)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
addon_paths:
  container:
    - /opt/odoo/addons
    - /workspaces/odoo/addons/ipai
    - /workspaces/odoo/addons/oca
  local:
    - vendor/odoo/addons
    - addons/ipai
    - addons/oca

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config paths — top-level (canonical, used by tests)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
config_path:
  container: /etc/odoo/odoo.conf
  local: config/dev/odoo.conf

data_dir:
  container: /var/lib/odoo
  local: /tmp/odoo-data

log_path:
  container: /var/log/odoo/odoo-server.log
  local: /tmp/odoo-data/odoo-server.log

log_level: info

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Health endpoint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
health_endpoint: /web/health

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Container image
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
container_image: acripaiodoo.azurecr.io/ipai-odoo:latest
container_registry: acripaiodoo.azurecr.io

text:
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Identity implementation patterns — maps surfaces to auth sample families
# Benchmark: Microsoft identity platform sample catalog
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
identity_samples:
  # Benchmark: Microsoft identity platform sample catalog + app-scenarios guide
  # Rule: "Do not treat 'OAuth works in the widget' as the end state;
  #         choose implementation patterns per surface."
  odoo_web:
    surface: odoo_web_client
    app_type: web_application
    flow: oidc_authorization_code
    library: msal_python_via_auth_oauth
    sample_family: web-app-sign-in
    notes: "Interactive sign-in + session. Server owns the session."
  copilot_widget_embedded:
    surface: pulser_widget_inside_odoo
    app_type: inherits_odoo_session
    flow: inherits_odoo_session
    library: none  # rides Odoo session cookie
    sample_family: null
    notes: >
      Default: widget is part of Odoo web app session, not a second
      independent Entra app. Only split to SPA if standalone frontend.
  copilot_widget_standalone:
    surface: pulser_standalone_frontend
    app_type: spa
    flow: authorization_code_pkce
    library: msal_js
    sample_family: spa-sign-in
    notes: "Only if widget is decoupled from Odoo web session."
  backend_api:
    surface: copilot_gateway_api
    app_type: protected_web_api
    flow: bearer_validation
    library: msal_python
    sample_family: web-api-protected
    notes: "Validates tokens from widget/M365. Enforces Odoo authorization."
  backend_api_downstream_user:
    surface: copilot_gateway_api_obo
    app_type: web_api
    flow: on_behalf_of
    library: msal_python
    sample_family: web-api-obo
    notes: "Only when downstream API call requires user delegation."
  foundry_bridge:
    surface: aca_to_foundry
    app_type: service_daemon
    flow: client_credentials_or_managed_identity
    library: azure_identity_sdk
    sample_family: daemon-managed-identity
    notes: >
      No interactive user. Workload identity only. Browser/widget must
      never call Foundry directly for privileged copilot behavior.
  gmail_addon:
    surface: gmail_workspace_addon
    app_type: google_workspace_addon
    flow: http_endpoint_backend
    library: google_identity
    sample_family: gmail-addon-http-runtime
    notes: >
      Google handles Gmail-side auth. Backend maps Gmail user to Odoo
      user and performs protected business actions. Not a Chrome extension.
  m365_copilot_interop:
    surface: m365_copilot_plugin
    app_type: protected_web_api
    flow: token_validation_plus_optional_obo_plus_daemon_backend
    library: msal_python
    sample_family: web-api-obo
    notes: >
      Host-facing surface is web API. Backend execution uses daemon/workload
      identity for Foundry/Search. Split host request handling from backend
      workload execution.
  databricks_jobs:
    surface: batch_analytics
    app_type: service_daemon
    flow: managed_identity
    library: azure_identity_sdk
    sample_family: daemon-managed-identity
    notes: "Batch processing. No interactive user."
  cli_tools:
    surface: developer_tooling
    app_type: public_client
    flow: device_code
    library: msal_python
    sample_family: device-code
    notes: "Developer tooling. Rare use."

interop:
  foundry_endpoint: "https://aifoundry-ipai-dev.eastus2.api.azureml.ms"
  m365_plugin_id: "placeholder-pending-registration"
  render_classes:
    - host-safe       # renders in Odoo web client
    - degraded        # renders with reduced features (e.g., mobile, iframe)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Observables — endpoints and commands for runtime verification

observables:
  health_check:
    method: GET
    path: /web/health
    expected_status: 200
  addon_discovery:
    method: GET
    path: /web/session/modules
    description: "Returns list of installed modules (requires auth)"
  config_dump:
    command: "odoo-bin --config /etc/odoo/odoo.conf --stop-after-init 2>&1 | head -50"
    description: "Dump effective configuration at startup"
  startup_log:
    path: /var/log/odoo/odoo-server.log
    description: "Odoo server startup log for verification"

data_intelligence:
  architecture_model: medallion
  benchmark: "Databricks + Fabric (techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azurearchitectureblog/data-intelligence-end-to-end)"
  last_reviewed: "2026-03-28"

  sources:
    - name: odoo_pg
      type: postgresql
      server: pg-ipai-odoo
      database: odoo
      ingestion: fabric_mirroring
      cdc: true
      notes: "Operational system of record. Fabric Mirroring for CDC to OneLake."
    - name: adls_raw
      type: azure_data_lake
      purpose: raw files, documents, external feeds
      ingestion: databricks_autoloader
    - name: external_feeds
      type: api
      purpose: third-party data (exchange rates, tax tables)
      ingestion: databricks_dlt

for verification" data_intelligence: architecture_model: medallion benchmark: "Databricks + Fabric (techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azurearchitectureblog/data-intelligence-end-to-end)" last_reviewed: "2026-03-28" sources: - name: odoo_pg type: postgresql server: pg-ipai-odoo database: odoo ingestion: fabric_mirroring cdc: true notes: "Operational system of record. Fabric Mirroring for CDC to OneLake." - name: adls_raw type: azure_data_lake purpose: raw files, documents, external feeds ingestion: databricks_autoloader - name: external_feeds type: api purpose: third-party data (exchange rates, tax tables) ingestion: databricks_dlt

  layers:
    bronze:
      purpose: raw_retention_auditability
      format: delta
      schema_mode: schema_on_read
      retention: append_only
      governance: unity_catalog
      notes: >
        Raw data preserved as-is from source systems. Source fidelity maintained.
        Used for audit trails and reprocessing. Never served directly to consumers.
    silver:
      purpose: cleansed_joined_curated
      format: delta
      schema_mode: schema_on_write
      governance: unity_catalog
      transformations:
        - deduplication
        - type_casting
        - business_key_resolution
        - join_enrichment
        - null_handling
      notes: >
        Cleansed and normalized data. Business keys resolved. Ready for
        domain-specific aggregation but not yet consumer-facing.
    gold:
      purpose: business_ready_semantic_serving
      format: delta
      schema_mode: schema_on_write
      governance: unity_catalog
      serving_targets:
        - power_bi_semantic_model
        - databricks_sql_endpoint
        - copilot_grounding_index
        - fabric_onelake
      notes: >
        Business-ready dimensional and aggregated data. SCD handling applied.
        This is the only layer served to BI, Copilot, and external consumers.

text:
type_casting - business_key_resolution - join_enrichment - null_handling notes: > Cleansed and normalized data. Business keys resolved. Ready for domain-specific aggregation but not yet consumer-facing. gold: purpose: business_ready_semantic_serving format: delta schema_mode: schema_on_write governance: unity_catalog serving_targets: - power_bi_semantic_model - databricks_sql_endpoint - copilot_grounding_index - fabric_onelake notes: > Business-ready dimensional and aggregated data. SCD handling applied. This is the only layer served to BI, Copilot, and external consumers.

  governance:
    primary_catalog: unity_catalog
    workspace: dbw-ipai-dev
    sql_warehouse: e7d89eabce4c330c
    metadata_publication: microsoft_purview
    data_classification: true
    lineage_tracking: true
    row_column_security: true

  serving:
    semantic_models: required
    copilot_grounding: gold_preferred
    direct_bronze_access: false
    direct_silver_access: false
    fallback_policy: >
      When a requested domain has no gold table, copilot falls back to
      operational ORM query and clearly indicates data is not from the
      governed analytical source. Never silently serve bronze/silver as gold.

  interoperability:
    storage_format: delta_lake
    uniform_support:
      - delta
      - iceberg
    notes: >
      Storage and semantic layer stays interoperable via Delta UniForm.
      Copilot grounding does not assume one locked-in downstream consumer.

  platform_services:
    identity: entra_id
    monitoring: azure_monitor
    secrets: key_vault
    cicd:
      - azure_devops
      - github
    cloud_security: defender_for_cloud
    cost_management: azure_cost_management

  key_principle: >
    Copilot, BI, and downstream AI consume a governed semantic/gold layer.
    They never ground directly on raw operational exhaust. Copilot does not
    redefine business truth — it consumes governed semantic products.
