schema_version: "1.0"
target_state: upstream_ci_translation
description: >
  Maps upstream Odoo CI/workflow intent to Azure DevOps pipeline responsibilities.
  GitHub Actions must NOT be reintroduced — this is a translation map only.
updated_at: "2026-03-18"

authority:
  ci_cd: azure_devops
  source_control: github
  github_actions: decommissioned

# Verified 2026-03-18: upstream GitHub Actions presence
upstream_github_actions_audit:
  odoo/odoo: none  # uses runbot, not GHA
  odoo/docker: none
  odoo/upgrade-util: none  # uses pre-commit hooks (ruff, typos, check-xml/yaml)
  odoo/documentation: none
  odoo/runbot: none
  odoo/tutorials: none
  odoo/owl: deploy.yml  # Node.js CI — npm test + lint + format-check + build
  odoo/OdooLocust: none
  odoo/design-themes: none
  summary: >
    Only odoo/owl has a GitHub Actions workflow. All other repos use runbot
    or pre-commit hooks. This confirms that copying GHA from upstream is
    not applicable — upstream barely uses GHA themselves.

# =============================================================================
# Upstream workflow intent → Azure DevOps pipeline mapping
# =============================================================================
translations:

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Static validation
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: lint-python
    upstream_intent: Python linting (flake8, pylint-odoo, isort, black)
    source_repos:
      - odoo/odoo
      - OCA/maintainer-quality-tools
    workflow_category: static_validation
    why_it_matters: >
      Catches syntax errors, style violations, and OCA-specific anti-patterns
      before tests run.
    azdo_pipeline: ci-validation
    azdo_stage: Lint
    azdo_job_type: standard
    priority: must_have
    notes: >
      Use pylint-odoo plugin for Odoo-specific checks.
      OCA maintainer-quality-tools defines the canonical ruleset.

schema_version: "1.0"
target_state: upstream_reference_map
description: Machine-readable map of official Odoo upstream repos and their engineering relevance.
updated_at: "2026-03-18"

policy:
  adopt_mode: reference_only
  ci_authority: azure_devops
  github_actions_in_repo: decommissioned
  translation_target: azure_devops_pipelines

repos:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Tier 1 — Active Engineering Reference
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: odoo-core
    repo: odoo/odoo
    url: https://github.com/odoo/odoo
    tier: 1
    relevance: CE runtime source, ORM API, core addon reference, upgrade migration scripts
    category_tags:
      - runtime_contract
      - packaging_runtime
      - migration_upgrade
      - test_topology
      - frontend_pattern
    recommended_use:
      - ORM API patterns for ipai_* module development
      - Core addon _inherit reference
      - Test class patterns (TransactionCase, HttpCase, tagged)
      - Upgrade migration script patterns (pre/post-migrate)
      - OWL component patterns for frontend widgets
    adopt_as_reference_only: true
    translate_into_plane:
      - ci-validation  # test patterns → lint/test stages
      - runtime-delivery  # packaging patterns → image build
    github_actions: none  # verified 2026-03-18: .github/ has only ISSUE_TEMPLATE + PR template
    upstream_ci_system: runbot
    key_paths:
      - odoo/tests/common.py  # test base classes (TransactionCase, HttpCase, etc.)
      - odoo/tests/tag_selector.py  # TagsSelector for test filtering
      - setup/requirements-check.py  # dependency version validation
    notes: >
      Mirrored locally at vendor/odoo/. Do not fork or vendor separately.
      No GitHub Actions in this repo — Odoo uses runbot for CI.

    key_paths:
      - 19.0/Dockerfile  # Ubuntu Noble, wkhtmltopdf multi-arch, pip install odoo
      - 19.0/entrypoint.sh  # DB config from env, wait-for-psql, Docker secrets support
      - 19.0/wait-for-psql.py  # PostgreSQL readiness polling with timeout
      - 19.0/odoo.conf  # minimal default config
    notes: >
      Adapt patterns to our Dockerfile at odoo/Dockerfile.
      Do not use upstream Dockerfile verbatim — we have ACA-specific runtime needs.
      Version-branched structure (17.0/, 18.0/, 19.0/) — always reference 19.0/.

- id: odoo-upgrade-util
    repo: odoo/upgrade-util
    url: https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util
    tier: 1
    relevance: Upgrade helper library for migration scripts
    category_tags:
      - migration_upgrade
    recommended_use:
      - Migration script helpers (rename_field, rename_model, merge_module)

      - debug-statements
    key_paths:
      - src/util/fields.py  # rename_field, remove_field, transform helpers
      - src/util/helpers.py  # table_of_model, version_between, version_gte
      - src/util/models.py  # model-level migration operations
      - src/util/modules.py  # module merge/rename/remove
      - src/util/domains.py  # domain expression adaptation
      - src/util/accounting.py  # accounting-specific migration helpers
      - src/util/pg.py  # PostgreSQL-level utilities
      - tools/generate-inherit.py  # inheritance graph generator
    notes: >
      Primary reference for writing pre-migrate.py / post-migrate.py in ipai_* modules.
      Installable as pip package for migration script development.
      29 utility modules covering fields, models, ORM, domains, accounting, HR payroll.

# org__azure__service_selection_policy__v1.policy.yaml
apiVersion: ipai.policy/v1
kind: ServiceSelectionPolicy
metadata:
  name: azure-service-selection-policy
  version: "1.0.0"
  owner: platform-team
  last_updated: "2026-03-16"

spec:
  service_classes:
    approved_core:
      description: "Always available, budgeted, actively used"
      services:
        - Azure Container Apps
        - Azure Front Door
        - Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
        - Azure Key Vault
        - Azure Container Registry
        - Azure Blob Storage / ADLS Gen2
        - Azure OpenAI Service
        - Azure AI Document Intelligence
        - Azure Databricks
        - Azure Monitor / Log Analytics

    conditional:
      description: "Available with justification, not default"
      services:
        - Azure AI Search
        - Azure AI Language
        - Azure Computer Vision
        - Azure AI Hub / Foundry
        - Azure DevOps (pipelines only)
        - Azure Virtual Machines (Supabase exception only)

    not_baseline:
      description: "Not part of standard stack, requires exception"
      services:
        - Azure Kubernetes Service
        - Azure Functions
        - Azure Logic Apps
        - Azure Cosmos DB
        - Azure Service Bus
        - Azure Event Grid
        - Azure API Management

  repo_defaults:
    agents/:
      consumes: [Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Document Intelligence, Azure AI Search, Azure Key Vault]
    odoo/:
      consumes: [Azure Container Apps, Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Azure Key Vault, Azure Front Door]
    lakehouse/:
      consumes: [Azure Databricks, Azure Blob Storage / ADLS Gen2, Azure Key Vault]
    ops-platform/:
      consumes: [Azure Monitor, Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Registry]
    .github/:
      consumes: [Azure Container Registry, Azure Container Apps]

API Management repo_defaults: agents/: consumes: [Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Document Intelligence, Azure AI Search, Azure Key Vault] odoo/: consumes: [Azure Container Apps, Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Azure Key Vault, Azure Front Door] lakehouse/: consumes: [Azure Databricks, Azure Blob Storage / ADLS Gen2, Azure Key Vault] ops-platform/: consumes: [Azure Monitor, Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Registry] .github/: consumes: [Azure Container Registry, Azure Container Apps]

  rules:
    - "New service adoption requires architecture review"
    - "not_baseline services need written exception with cost justification"
    - "Every consumed service must appear in repo service binding registry"

# org__foundry__model_selection_policy__v1.policy.yaml
apiVersion: ipai.policy/v1
kind: ModelSelectionPolicy
metadata:
  name: foundry-model-selection-policy
  version: "1.0.0"
  owner: platform-team
  last_updated: "2026-03-16"

spec:
  source_preference:
    - azure_ai_foundry  # Primary: Azure-deployed models
    - github_models      # Secondary: GitHub Models marketplace
    - direct_provider    # Tertiary: Direct API (Anthropic, OpenAI)

  alias_classes:
    chat_default:
      description: "General conversation, summarization"
      current_model: gpt-4o
      fallback: gpt-4o-mini
    coding_default:
      description: "Code generation, review, refactoring"
      current_model: claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514
      fallback: gpt-4o
    reasoning_default:
      description: "Complex reasoning, planning, analysis"
      current_model: o4-mini
      fallback: claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514
    embedding_default:
      description: "Text embeddings for search/RAG"
      current_model: text-embedding-3-large
      fallback: text-embedding-3-small
    vision_default:
      description: "Image understanding, document analysis"
      current_model: gpt-4o
      fallback: gpt-4o-mini
    router_balanced:
      description: "Model router for cost/quality balance"
      current_model: gpt-4o-mini
      note: "Used for classification, routing, triage"

  guardrails:
    - "Repos call aliases, never raw model catalog IDs"
    - "Alias resolution lives in ops-platform model registry"
    - "Model changes require eval comparison before swap"
    - "Cost tracking per alias per repo per month"

# org__foundry__promotion_policy__v1.policy.yaml
apiVersion: ipai.policy/v1
kind: PromotionPolicy
metadata:
  name: foundry-promotion-policy
  version: "1.0.0"
  owner: platform-team
  last_updated: "2026-03-16"

spec:
  states:
    draft:
      description: "Initial development, not deployed"
      allowed_transitions: [dev]
    dev:
      description: "Deployed to dev environment"
      allowed_transitions: [staging, deprecated]
      gates: []
    staging:
      description: "Deployed to staging, under evaluation"
      allowed_transitions: [prod, dev, deprecated]
      gates:
        - eval_suite_pass: true
        - min_eval_score: 0.85
        - code_review_approved: true
    prod:
      description: "Live in production"
      allowed_transitions: [deprecated]
      gates:
        - eval_suite_pass: true
        - min_eval_score: 0.90
        - load_test_pass: true
        - security_review_pass: true
        - rollback_plan_documented: true
    deprecated:
      description: "Scheduled for removal"
      allowed_transitions: []
      gates:
        - replacement_agent_in_prod: true
        - traffic_migrated: true

  ci_enforcement:
    block_on_eval_failure: true
    require_manifest_version_bump: true
    require_changelog_entry: true

# org__foundry__runtime_strategy__v1.policy.yaml
# Org-level policy: How IPAI uses Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
apiVersion: ipai.policy/v1
kind: RuntimeStrategy
metadata:
  name: foundry-runtime-strategy
  version: "1.0.0"
  owner: platform-team
  last_updated: "2026-03-16"

spec:
  agent_runtime: foundry_agent_service
  sdk_routing:
    foundry_sdk:
      use_for: [agent_lifecycle, evals, traces, deployments]
      repos: [agents/, ops-platform/]
    openai_sdk:
      use_for: [direct_model_calls, embeddings, completions]
      repos: [agents/skills/, odoo/addons/ipai/ipai_ai_*]
    agent_framework:
      use_for: [local_multi_agent, graph_workflows, checkpointing]
      repos: [agents/]
      note: "Use for local dev/testing; Foundry Agent Service for production"
    foundry_tools_sdk:
      use_for: [document_intelligence, search, speech, vision]
      repos: [agents/, ops-platform/]

  repo_ownership:
    agents/:
      runtime: foundry_agent_service
      manifests: agents/foundry/agents/
      tools: agents/foundry/tools/
      evals: agents/foundry/evals/
      policies: agents/foundry/policies/
    ops-platform/:
      registries: ops-platform/ssot/
      service_catalog: ops-platform/ssot/azure/
    odoo/:
      contracts: odoo/ssot/odoo/
      note: "Odoo is system of record for finance; agents call approved Odoo tools"

  promotion_model:
    states: [draft, dev, staging, prod, deprecated]
    publish_gates:
      dev_to_staging: [eval_pass, code_review]
      staging_to_prod: [eval_pass, load_test, security_review]

# org__foundry__tool_allowlist__v1.policy.yaml
apiVersion: ipai.policy/v1
kind: ToolAllowlist
metadata:
  name: foundry-tool-allowlist
  version: "1.0.0"
  owner: platform-team
  last_updated: "2026-03-16"

spec:
  categories:
    document_processing:
      - tool: document_intelligence
        provider: azure
        modes: [read, layout, prebuilt]
        allowed_document_classes: [vendor_bill, receipt, contract, bank_statement, tax_document, policy_document]
      - tool: azure_search
        provider: azure
        use_for: [knowledge_retrieval, document_search]

    odoo_actions:
      - tool: odoo_create_vendor_bill
        provider: odoo_rpc
        requires: [finance_approval_workflow]
      - tool: odoo_create_expense
        provider: odoo_rpc
        requires: [expense_workflow]
      - tool: odoo_search_partner
        provider: odoo_rpc
      - tool: odoo_get_journal_entries
        provider: odoo_rpc
        requires: [finance_read_access]
      - tool: odoo_update_move_state
        provider: odoo_rpc
        requires: [finance_approval_workflow]
        note: "Never bypass Odoo workflow state machine"

    notifications:
      - tool: slack_send_message
        provider: slack_api
        channels: [finance-alerts, ops-alerts]
      - tool: email_send
        provider: zoho_smtp
        note: "Route through Odoo ir.mail_server"

  global_rules:
    - "Tools must have typed input/output schemas"
    - "No tool may bypass Odoo workflow state transitions"
    - "All tool calls must emit OpenTelemetry spans"
    - "Secrets resolved from Azure Key Vault at runtime, never hardcoded"

# Diva Goals -- Platform SSOT Entry
# AI-native strategy, capability, and execution control plane.

slug: diva-goals
product_name: Diva Goals
positioning: >
  AI-native strategy, capability, and execution control plane for the agentic
  DevOps SDLC. Orchestration, review, and readiness layer over Azure Boards,
  GitHub, CI/CD, eval gates, runtime observability, L&D systems, and agent
  registries. Not a replacement for any source system.

status: spec-complete
version: "2.0"
last_updated: "2026-03-23"

spec_bundle: spec/diva-goals/
architecture_doc: docs/architecture/DIVA_GOALS_TARGET_STATE.md

product_axes:
  - business_execution
  - human_capability
  - agent_capability
  - organizational_readiness

  goals_portfolio:
    source_of_truth: azure-boards
    diva_goals_role: read-align-review
  specs:
    source_of_truth: spec-kit
    diva_goals_role: read-trace-validate

    source_of_truth: github
    diva_goals_role: read-evidence
  ci_cd_pipelines:
    source_of_truth: azure-devops-pipelines
    diva_goals_role: read-evidence
  eval_gates:
    source_of_truth: eval-framework

  runtime_observability:
    source_of_truth: app-insights-databricks-powerbi
    diva_goals_role: read-evidence
  people_capability:
    source_of_truth: hr-ld-systems
    diva_goals_role: read-evidence-for-capability
  agent_capability:
    source_of_truth: agent-registries-evals-skill-packs
    diva_goals_role: read-evidence-for-readiness
  orchestration_review:
    source_of_truth: diva-goals
    diva_goals_role: write-own

principles:
  - id: P1
    name: graph-not-list
    description: >
      Goals, KRs, initiatives, specs, work items, agent runs, capabilities,
      and evidence form a directed acyclic graph, not a flat list.
  - id: P2
    name: derived-status-default
    description: >
      Status is computed from source system evidence by default.
      Manual status is an override, not the norm.
  - id: P3
    name: evidence-addressable
    description: >
      Every piece of evidence has a stable URI and can be referenced
      from any entity in the graph.
  - id: P4
    name: agents-are-participants
    description: >
      Agents appear in the graph as participants with identity,
      capability declarations, confidence scores, and audit trails.
  - id: P5
    name: staleness-visible
    description: >
      Evidence older than its freshness policy is flagged as stale.
      Stale evidence degrades derived status to unverified.
  - id: P6
    name: reviews-structured
    description: >
      Review ceremonies have defined inputs, outputs, and cadence.
      They are not ad-hoc meetings.
  - id: P7
    name: layered-authority
    description: >
      Source systems are authoritative for their domain. Diva Goals is
      authoritative for orchestration and review. Humans are authoritative
      for approval.
  - id: P8
    name: capability-is-load-bearing
    description: >
      Capability requirements on strategic priorities are constraints that
      affect readiness status and surface as blockers when unmet.
  - id: P9
    name: humans-and-agents-are-capability-actors
    description: >
      Both people and agents are capability-bearing actors with roles,
      skills, development paths, and readiness states.
  - id: P10
    name: learning-tied-to-outcomes
    description: >
      Learning plans and skill-pack development exist to close capability
      gaps that block strategic outcomes. Unlinked learning is waste.

    - release
    - runtime_metric
  chain: >
    objective -> key_result -> initiative -> spec_bundle -> work_item ->
    agent_run -> pull_request -> pipeline_run -> eval_gate -> release ->
    runtime_metric

policy_flags:
  no_manual_only_status: true
  no_status_without_evidence: true
  no_silent_agent_mutation: true
  no_duplicated_truth_stores: true
  humans_approve_agents_propose: true
  stale_evidence_auto_flag: true
  override_requires_justification: true

policy_extensions:
  readiness_override_requires_audit: true
  agent_autonomy_upgrade_requires_eval: true
  capability_gap_blockers_must_surface: true

agent_stack:
  orchestration_sdk: microsoft-agent-framework

  deployment_mode: foundry-hosted
  knowledge_runtime: foundry-iq-mcp-knowledge-base-retrieve

    - boards-spec-github-traceability
    - policy-check

      - task_adherence
      - intent_resolution
      - tool_call_accuracy
      - groundedness
    pipeline: foundry-eval-pipeline
    gate: block-deploy-on-eval-fail

type: exception cadence: on-demand focus: drift-eval-failure-stale-evidence-escalation policy_flags: no_manual_only_status: true no_status_without_evidence: true no_silent_agent_mutation: true no_duplicated_truth_stores: true humans_approve_agents_propose: true stale_evidence_auto_flag: true override_requires_justification: true policy_extensions: readiness_override_requires_audit: true agent_autonomy_upgrade_requires_eval: true capability_gap_blockers_must_surface: true agent_stack: orchestration_sdk: microsoft-agent-framework managed_runtime: azure-ai-foundry-agent-service deployment_mode: foundry-hosted knowledge_runtime: foundry-iq-mcp-knowledge-base-retrieve skills: - strategy-review - capability-review - boards-spec-github-traceability - policy-check observability: foundry-tracing-plus-azure-monitor evaluation: evaluators: - task_completion - task_adherence - intent_resolution - tool_call_accuracy - groundedness pipeline: foundry-eval-pipeline gate: block-deploy-on-eval-fail

workflow_topology:
  - id: goal-status-synthesis
    trigger: source-system-event
    flow: event -> evidence-collector -> graph-update -> derived-status -> drift-check -> notification
  - id: review-pack-generation
    trigger: scheduled-or-on-demand
    flow: review-synthesis-agent -> aggregate -> summarize -> draft-inputs -> store
  - id: capability-readiness-review
    trigger: quarterly-or-on-demand
    flow: capability-aggregation -> gap-scoring -> readiness-assessment -> learning-effectiveness -> draft-pack
  - id: drift-orphan-detection
    trigger: continuous-plus-daily-scan
    flow: graph-scan -> status-vs-evidence -> orphan-check -> staleness-check -> flag-generation

block-deploy-on-eval-fail workflow_topology: - id: goal-status-synthesis trigger: source-system-event flow: event -> evidence-collector -> graph-update -> derived-status -> drift-check -> notification - id: review-pack-generation trigger: scheduled-or-on-demand flow: review-synthesis-agent -> aggregate -> summarize -> draft-inputs -> store - id: capability-readiness-review trigger: quarterly-or-on-demand flow: capability-aggregation -> gap-scoring -> readiness-assessment -> learning-effectiveness -> draft-pack - id: drift-orphan-detection trigger: continuous-plus-daily-scan flow: graph-scan -> status-vs-evidence -> orphan-check -> staleness-check -> flag-generation

target_architecture:
  summary: >
    Databricks is the governed data backbone; AI Foundry is the primary
    generative AI and agent application plane; Fabric / Power BI is the
    downstream semantic and reporting plane; Purview is the governance
    overlay; Azure + Entra + GitHub + Azure DevOps form the control and
    engineering envelope.
  canonical_tiers:
    tier_1_canonical:
      - ai_foundry
      - azure
      - devops
      - entra
      - fabric
      - github
      - purview
      - visual_studio_code
    tier_2_bounded:
      - microsoft_365_admin
      - copilot_studio
      - power_automate
      - power_platform
      - defender
    tier_3_not_core:
      - data_explorer
      - intune

decision_enums:
  - keep_and_expand
  - keep
  - keep_bounded
  - keep_downstream_only
  - keep_platform_control
  - optional_later
  - optional_bounded
  - out_of_scope
  - do_not_make_core

    tier: "tier_1_canonical"
    owner_plane: "agent-platform"
    role: >
      Primary surface for agent orchestration, model access, evaluations,
      tracing, safety, and enterprise AI application management.
    boundaries:

        - "RAG and tool-calling patterns"

    dependencies:

      - "devops"
      - "purview"
    notes:
      - "Treat as the primary gen-AI and agent plane."
      - "Ground on Databricks-governed data rather than replacing Databricks."

schema_version: "1.0"
namespace: release.contract
last_reviewed: "2026-03-27"
description: >
  Machine-readable definition of what "production-complete" means per
  deployable surface. Each surface declares its pipeline, required stages,
  smoke criteria, and evidence requirements. Used by CI to gate promotion.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Surfaces
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
surfaces:

  web_landing:
    display_name: Web Landing Page
    pipeline: azure-pipelines/web-landing-deploy.yml
    acr_image: acripaiodoo.azurecr.io/ipai-website:latest
    container_app: ipai-website-dev
    resource_group: rg-ipai-dev-odoo-runtime
    public_url: https://www.insightpulseai.com
    dockerfile: web/ipai-landing/Dockerfile
    trigger_paths:
      - web/ipai-landing/**
    required_stages:
      - build
      - deploy
      - smoke
      - evidence
    smoke_criteria:
      http_status: 200
      content_must_contain: InsightPulse
      content_must_not_contain: null
    evidence_artifact: evidence-web-landing-*

  odoo_erp:
    display_name: Odoo CE 19 ERP
    pipeline: azure-pipelines/odoo-deploy.yml
    acr_image: acripaiodoo.azurecr.io/ipai-odoo:19.0-copilot
    container_app: ipai-odoo-dev-web
    resource_group: rg-ipai-dev-odoo-runtime
    public_url: https://erp.insightpulseai.com
    dockerfile: platform/runtime/docker/docker/Dockerfile.odoo-copilot
    trigger_paths:
      - addons/ipai/**
      - config/**
      - platform/runtime/docker/docker/Dockerfile.odoo-copilot
    required_stages:
      - build
      - deploy
      - smoke
      - evidence
    smoke_criteria:
      http_status: 200
      smoke_path: /web/login
      content_must_contain: Odoo
      content_must_not_contain: Internal Server Error
    evidence_artifact: evidence-odoo-erp-*

- build - deploy - smoke - evidence smoke_criteria: http_status: 200 content_must_contain: InsightPulse content_must_not_contain: null evidence_artifact: evidence-web-landing-* odoo_erp: display_name: Odoo CE 19 ERP pipeline: azure-pipelines/odoo-deploy.yml acr_image: acripaiodoo.azurecr.io/ipai-odoo:19.0-copilot container_app: ipai-odoo-dev-web resource_group: rg-ipai-dev-odoo-runtime public_url: https://erp.insightpulseai.com dockerfile: platform/runtime/docker/docker/Dockerfile.odoo-copilot trigger_paths: - addons/ipai/** - config/** - platform/runtime/docker/docker/Dockerfile.odoo-copilot required_stages: - build - deploy - smoke - evidence smoke_criteria: http_status: 200 smoke_path: /web/login content_must_contain: Odoo content_must_not_contain: Internal Server Error evidence_artifact: evidence-odoo-erp-*

  databricks:
    display_name: Databricks Bundles
    pipeline: azure-pipelines/databricks-deploy.yml
    databricks_host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    warehouse_id: e7d89eabce4c330c
    trigger_paths:
      - databricks/**
      - data-intelligence/**
    required_stages:
      - detect
      - validate
      - deploy
      - smoke
      - evidence
    smoke_criteria:
      warehouse_health: SUCCEEDED
    bundles:
      - databricks/bundles/foundation_python
      - databricks/bundles/lakeflow_ingestion
      - databricks/bundles/sql_warehouse
    evidence_artifact: evidence-databricks-*

  bi_layer:
    display_name: BI Deploy & Promote
    pipeline: azure-pipelines/bi-deploy-promote.yml
    databricks_host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    warehouse_id: e7d89eabce4c330c
    trigger_paths:
      - databricks/bundles/sql_warehouse/src/sql/serving/*
      - data-intelligence/semantic/powerbi/*
    required_stages:
      - validate_repo
      - deploy_serving
      - validate
    smoke_criteria:
      serving_view_count_min: 9
    evidence_artifact: null  # inline validation

databricks_host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net warehouse_id: e7d89eabce4c330c trigger_paths: - databricks/** - data-intelligence/** required_stages: - detect - validate - deploy - smoke - evidence smoke_criteria: warehouse_health: SUCCEEDED bundles: - databricks/bundles/foundation_python - databricks/bundles/lakeflow_ingestion - databricks/bundles/sql_warehouse evidence_artifact: evidence-databricks-* bi_layer: display_name: BI Deploy & Promote pipeline: azure-pipelines/bi-deploy-promote.yml databricks_host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net warehouse_id: e7d89eabce4c330c trigger_paths: - databricks/bundles/sql_warehouse/src/sql/serving/* - data-intelligence/semantic/powerbi/* required_stages: - validate_repo - deploy_serving - validate smoke_criteria: serving_view_count_min: 9 evidence_artifact: null # inline validation

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Promotion policy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
promotion:
  dev_to_staging:
    requires:
      - all_required_stages_pass
      - evidence_artifact_published
      - smoke_green
    gate: manual_approval
    environment: staging

  staging_to_prod:
    requires:
      - staging_smoke_green
      - evidence_artifact_published
      - no_critical_alerts
    gate: manual_approval
    environment: prod

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Evidence contract
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
evidence:
  format: json
  schema_version: "1.0"
  required_fields:
    - surface
    - timestamp
    - build_number
    - commit_sha
    - image_ref
    - smoke_url
    - pipeline
  artifact_naming: "evidence-{surface}-{build_number}"
  retention_days: 90

version: 1
release_gates:
  spec_bundle_required: true
  ssot_validation_required: true
  evidence_pack_required: true
  tests_required: true
  deployment_contract_required: true
  fail_closed: true

promotion:
  prod_requires_acceptance_status:
    - accepted
    - ready_for_release
  prod_requires_runtime_status:
    - conditional-approved
    - approved
  block_on_missing_evidence: true
  block_on_schema_drift: true
  block_on_topology_mismatch: true

  microsoft_marketplace:

id: org_topology
version: 1.2.0
updated: "2026-03-21"
status: target_state

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Repos
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
repos:
  - id: .github
    plane: governance
    purpose: Org governance, reusable workflows, policy baselines, issue forms, and CI contracts
    status: active

  - id: odoo
    plane: transactional
    purpose: Transactional system of record — Odoo 19 CE + OCA + thin IPAI bridge modules
    status: active

  - id: platform
    plane: control
    purpose: Platform control plane and SSOT — vault references, platform metadata, backend/control-plane contracts, and edge/runtime glue
    status: active

  - id: data-intelligence
    plane: intelligence
    purpose: Databricks + Unity Catalog analytics engineering, medallion contracts, semantic-serving prep, and BI delivery artifacts
    status: split_required
    current_surface: embedded_in_monorepo
    transition_paths:
      - infra/databricks/
      - odoo/infra/databricks/sql/
      - lakehouse/contracts/
    split_plan: docs/architecture/data-intelligence/repo-split-plan.md

  - id: agent-platform
    plane: agent
    purpose: Agent runtime platform — orchestration, task ledger, execution contracts, control-room integrations, and runtime glue
    status: active

  - id: web
    plane: experience
    purpose: User-facing web surfaces — ops console, marketing sites, docs sites, and browser-facing product apps
    status: planned

  - id: control

  - id: intelligence
    description: Data engineering, analytics, governed data products
  - id: agent
    description: Agent runtime, tools, evaluation, orchestration
  - id: experience
    description: User-facing apps, portals, extensions
  - id: substrate
    description: Cloud infrastructure, identity, networking
  - id: automation
    description: Workflow engines, schedulers, event-driven jobs
  - id: doctrine
    description: Agent definitions, skills, knowledge, evaluations
  - id: visual
    description: Design tokens, brand assets, component libraries
  - id: documentation
    description: Architecture docs, strategy, evidence packs
  - id: bootstrap
    description: Starter kits, repo templates, scaffolds

# Naming Corrections
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
naming_corrections:
  - wrong: control-plane
    correct: platform
    note: concept alias, not repo name

  - wrong: design-system
    correct: design
    note: internal folder name, not repo name

# =============================================================================
# VS Code Engineering Cockpit — Target Capabilities (SSOT extract)
# =============================================================================
# Source: docs/research/wholesale-saas-erp-azure-architecture-study.md
# ADR:    docs/architecture/ADR_VSCODE_ENGINEERING_COCKPIT.md
# Date:   2026-03-17
# =============================================================================

cockpit:
  name: vscode-devcontainer-first
  version: "1.0"
  date_accepted: "2026-03-17"

core_rules:
  - rule: Repo-scoped settings own correctness
    scope: workspace .vscode/settings.json
    enforced_by: PR review + CI lint

  - rule: User settings own personal UX only
    scope: VS Code user settings
    examples: [font, color theme, UI layout]
    banned: [runtime config, interpreter paths, language server overrides]

  - rule: Devcontainer is the runtime/tooling contract
    scope: devcontainer.json
    constraints:
      python: ">=3.10"  # Odoo 19; repo standard 3.11
      postgresql: ">=13"
    enforced_by: devcontainer build + CI parity

  - rule: No hardcoded local interpreter paths
    pattern: "Use ${workspaceFolder} or devcontainer-relative paths"
    banned_examples:
      - "/Users/*/...pyenv/..."
      - "C:\\Users\\*\\..."

settings_hierarchy:
  - location: User settings
    owns: Personal UX (font, UI layout)
    why: Avoid per-developer drift on runtime

  - location: Root .vscode/settings.json
    owns: Repo-wide exclusions, formatters, YAML/JSON schemas, tooling toggles
    why: Portable, applies across subprojects

  - location: odoo/.vscode/settings.json
    owns: Python interpreter (devcontainer), Odoo lint paths, debug defaults
    why: Odoo 19 constraints are stricter than general repo

  - location: Nested frontend .vscode/
    owns: TypeScript/Node version, formatter/linter
    why: Isolates frontend from Python/Odoo

owns: Personal UX (font, UI layout) why: Avoid per-developer drift on runtime - location: Root .vscode/settings.json owns: Repo-wide exclusions, formatters, YAML/JSON schemas, tooling toggles why: Portable, applies across subprojects - location: odoo/.vscode/settings.json owns: Python interpreter (devcontainer), Odoo lint paths, debug defaults why: Odoo 19 constraints are stricter than general repo - location: Nested frontend .vscode/ owns: TypeScript/Node version, formatter/linter why: Isolates frontend from Python/Odoo

  - location: BANNED
    items:
      - Hardcoded pyenv paths
      - Machine-specific filesystem paths
      - Explicit python.languageServer overrides

target_capabilities:
  - name: Deterministic Odoo runtime
    implementation: Devcontainer image pinned; Python >=3.10; PG >=13
    workspace: odoo/ devcontainer

  - name: Addon import resolution
    implementation: addons-path in launch/debug tasks; lint paths match addon roots
    workspace: odoo/.vscode/

  - name: Ruff formatting/linting
    implementation: Odoo PEP8 exceptions mapped into Ruff config
    workspace: repo root config

  - name: YAML/JSON schema support
    implementation: Workspace schema associations
    workspace: root .vscode/settings.json

  - name: Prompt/eval authoring
    implementation: Versioned files; Foundry eval runs in CI gates
    workspace: agent-platform/

  - name: MCP-aware development
    implementation: Tool surfaces as MCP servers; Agent SDK integration
    workspace: agent-platform/

  - name: Devcontainer features reuse
    implementation: Reusable Features from spec/official repos
    workspace: root + subfolders

  - name: Monorepo performance
    implementation: files.watcherExclude / search.exclude in workspace settings
    workspace: root .vscode/settings.json

  - name: Debug/test tasks
    implementation: VS Code tasks/launch configs in repo
    workspace: root + odoo/

  - name: Pipeline authoring
    implementation: Repo-scoped schemas; Azure Pipelines approvals for prod
    workspace: root

servers; Agent SDK integration workspace: agent-platform/ - name: Devcontainer features reuse implementation: Reusable Features from spec/official repos workspace: root + subfolders - name: Monorepo performance implementation: files.watcherExclude / search.exclude in workspace settings workspace: root .vscode/settings.json - name: Debug/test tasks implementation: VS Code tasks/launch configs in repo workspace: root + odoo/ - name: Pipeline authoring implementation: Repo-scoped schemas; Azure Pipelines approvals for prod workspace: root

python_standard:
  version: "3.11"
  runtime: pyenv odoo-19-dev virtualenv
  note: >
    Odoo 19 requires >=3.10. Repo standardizes on 3.11 for
    stability. Devcontainer image must match.

# =============================================================================
# Wholesale SaaS ERP on Azure — Research / Adopted Target
# =============================================================================
#
# Origin: deep-research synthesis for Azure-native wholesale ERP platform.
# Status: adopted — normalized to six-plane Azure-first architecture.
#
# Canonical SSOT: ssot/architecture/platform-boundaries.yaml
# Long-form rationale: docs/architecture/WHOLESALE_SAAS_ERP_ON_AZURE.md
#
# Last updated: 2026-03-18
# =============================================================================

target_architecture:
  name: wholesale-saas-erp-azure
  status: adopted
  version: "2.0"
  architecture_model: six_plane_azure_first

  control_plane:
    identity: azure_entra_id
    planning: azure_devops_boards
    ci_cd: azure_devops_pipelines
    source_control: github
    governance: azure_policy

  system_of_record:

    workloads:

      - inventory_fulfillment
      - procurement
      - accounting
      - bir_tax_compliance

  system_of_intelligence:
    platform: azure_databricks
    architecture: medallion_lakehouse
    governance: unity_catalog
    storage: adls_gen2
    canonical: true  # not optional
    workloads:

      - sales_forecasting
      - operational_bi
      - ml_feature_serving

  system_of_agency:

    role: agent_runtime_tracing_evals_monitoring

    document_processing: azure_ai_document_intelligence
    workloads:

      - autonomous_vendor_billing

      - document_ocr_extraction

system_of_record: platform: odoo_ce_19 compute: azure_container_apps database: azure_postgresql_flexible isolation: database_per_tenant workloads: - sales_order_management - inventory_fulfillment - procurement - accounting - bir_tax_compliance system_of_intelligence: platform: azure_databricks architecture: medallion_lakehouse governance: unity_catalog storage: adls_gen2 canonical: true # not optional workloads: - financial_consolidation - sales_forecasting - operational_bi - ml_feature_serving system_of_agency: platform: microsoft_foundry role: agent_runtime_tracing_evals_monitoring models: - anthropic_claude - azure_openai document_processing: azure_ai_document_intelligence workloads: - copilot_chat - autonomous_vendor_billing - exception_handling - document_ocr_extraction

  integration:
    api_gateway: azure_api_management
    event_bus: azure_service_bus
    edge: azure_front_door
    api_gateway_role:
      - governed_ingress_egress_boundary
      - not_system_of_record
      - not_agent_runtime

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  truth_authorities:

    code: github
    release: azure_pipelines
    live_inventory: azure_resource_graph

    intended_state: repo_ssot

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  tenancy:

      app_model: shared_runtime_farm

# =============================================================================
# Wholesale SaaS ERP on Azure — Binding Decisions (SSOT extract)
# =============================================================================
# Source: docs/research/wholesale-saas-erp-azure-architecture-study.md
# ADR:    docs/architecture/ADR_ERP_PLATFORM_ROLE_SPLIT.md
# Date:   2026-03-17
# =============================================================================

architecture:
  name: three-plane-saas-erp
  version: "2.0"
  date_accepted: "2026-03-17"

planes:
  system_of_record:
    platform: Odoo CE 19
    owns:
      - orders
      - invoices
      - inventory_moves
      - purchasing
      - accounting_postings
      - master_data
      - multi_company
      - multi_warehouse
      - multi_currency
    must_not_own:
      - analytics_lake
      - agent_orchestration
      - agent_governance
    runtime:
      python: ">=3.10"  # Odoo 19 requirement; repo standard is 3.11
      postgresql: ">=13"
      hosting: Azure Container Apps
      database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

  system_of_intelligence:
    platform: Azure Databricks + Unity Catalog
    owns:
      - lakehouse_storage
      - medallion_layers  # bronze → silver → gold
      - data_products
      - governed_context_datasets
      - cross_domain_analytics
    must_not_own:
      - erp_transactions
      - transactional_core_database
    governance: Unity Catalog  # permissions, auditing, lineage, quality, discovery

  agent_runtime:
    platform: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
    owns:
      - agent_hosting
      - agent_tracing
      - agent_evaluation
      - agent_publish_promotion
      - agent_monitoring
    must_not_own:
      - data_engineering  # Databricks
      - erp_posting       # Odoo
    lifecycle: create → test → trace → evaluate → publish → monitor
    agent_boundary_rule: >
      Agents recommend; Odoo commits. No agent may post to ledger,
      create orders, or mutate inventory without an Odoo-side guardrail.

permissions, auditing, lineage, quality, discovery agent_runtime: platform: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service owns: - agent_hosting - agent_tracing - agent_evaluation - agent_publish_promotion - agent_monitoring must_not_own: - data_engineering # Databricks - erp_posting # Odoo lifecycle: create → test → trace → evaluate → publish → monitor agent_boundary_rule: > Agents recommend; Odoo commits. No agent may post to ledger, create orders, or mutate inventory without an Odoo-side guardrail.

tenancy:
  default_model: database-per-tenant
  rationale: >
    Aligns with Odoo's multi-database hosting pattern and hostname-based
    database routing via dbfilter.
  tiers:
    standard:
      pattern: shared_runtime_db_per_tenant
      routing: dbfilter by hostname
      isolation: database boundary
    enterprise:
      pattern: deployment_per_tenant_stamp
      isolation: dedicated Odoo + Postgres
      trigger: revenue threshold or regulatory requirement
    growth_model: hybrid  # shared default, stamp on upgrade

integration_spine:
  api_gateway: Azure API Management  # tool gateway + external API governance
  messaging: Azure Service Bus       # durable cross-system (queues + topics)
  eventing: Azure Event Grid         # reactive fan-out
  agent_sdk: Anthropic Agent SDK + MCP  # tool connectivity standard

devops_coexistence:
  portfolio: Azure DevOps Boards    # epics, features, stories, audit
  source_control: GitHub            # monorepo, PRs, code review
  deployment: Azure Pipelines       # approvals/checks for prod
  linking: "AB#"                    # Azure Boards ↔ GitHub commit linking
  runner_policy:
    default: microsoft-hosted       # reimaged per run
    self_hosted: only for private-network or specialized deps

eventing: Azure Event Grid # reactive fan-out agent_sdk: Anthropic Agent SDK + MCP # tool connectivity standard devops_coexistence: portfolio: Azure DevOps Boards # epics, features, stories, audit source_control: GitHub # monorepo, PRs, code review deployment: Azure Pipelines # approvals/checks for prod linking: "AB#" # Azure Boards ↔ GitHub commit linking runner_policy: default: microsoft-hosted # reimaged per run self_hosted: only for private-network or specialized deps

roadmap:
  - phase: P0
    name: Foundations
    focus: Landing zone, identity, secrets, observability, devcontainer cockpit
  - phase: P1
    name: MVP ERP
    focus: Odoo 19 DB-per-tenant + dbfilter, customer portal, APIM
  - phase: P2
    name: Analytics
    focus: Databricks lakehouse + Unity Catalog, medallion from Odoo
  - phase: P3
    name: Copilots
    focus: Foundry agents with trace/eval/monitor, Document Intelligence
  - phase: P4
    name: Multi-tenant hardening
    focus: Hybrid tenancy, approval gates, SLOs, stamp factory

avoid_entirely:
  - Databricks as transactional core
  - ERP posting via agents without guardrails
  - Untracked VS Code user-setting dependencies
  - Agents without trace/eval gates
  - Supabase as control plane (narrowed to self-hosted VM exception)

# SSOT: Public Route Registry
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/INSIGHTPULSEAI_TARGET_STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md §2.1
# Updated: 2026-03-25

version: "1.0.0"

edge: azure_front_door
resource: ipai-fd-dev

routes:
  landing:
    path: /
    method: GET
    auth: public
    backend: ipai-website-dev
    service: Landing page / marketing
    repo: web/ipai-landing/

  ask_pulser:
    path: /ask
    method: GET
    auth: public_anonymous
    backend: ipai-copilot-gateway
    service: Ask Pulser (read-only, no PII)
    repo: web/packages/pulser-widget/
    agent: ask-pulser-agent
    grounding: [approved_public_docs, pricing_faq, architecture_pages, release_notes]

  lead_capture:
    path: /api/v1/leads
    method: POST
    auth: public_captcha
    backend: ipai-api-facade
    service: Lead capture → Odoo crm.lead
    repo: web/packages/api-facade/
    odoo_target: crm.lead.create

  demo_booking:
    path: /api/v1/demo
    method: POST
    auth: public_captcha
    backend: ipai-api-facade
    service: Demo booking → Odoo calendar.event
    repo: web/packages/api-facade/
    odoo_target: calendar.event.create

  contact:
    path: /api/v1/contact
    method: POST
    auth: public_captcha
    backend: ipai-api-facade
    service: Contact form → Odoo mail
    repo: web/packages/api-facade/
    odoo_target: mail.mail.create

  odoo_erp:
    path: /erp/*
    method: "*"
    auth: entra_oidc
    backend: ipai-odoo-dev-web
    service: Odoo ERP (authenticated)
    hostname: erp.insightpulseai.com

  document_ocr:
    path: /ocr/*
    method: POST
    auth: entra_bearer
    backend: ipai-ocr-dev
    service: Document upload + OCR
    hostname: ocr.insightpulseai.com

  mcp:
    path: /mcp/*
    method: "*"
    auth: entra_managed_identity
    backend: ipai-mcp-dev
    service: MCP tool coordination (internal)
    hostname: mcp.insightpulseai.com
